Dod, John, of Cheshire, fellow Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1585, B.A. 1578; incorp. as M.A. 11 July, 1585 (youngest of the 17 children of John Dod, of Shocklach, Cheshire), 'the Decalogist,' rector of Hanwell, Oxon, 1585, vicar of St. Stephen, Coleman Street, London, 1597, minister of Coggeshall 1609, and of Fenny Drayton, co. Leicester, of Canons Ashby and Fawsley, Northants, though for a time silenced in each, died at Fawsley, buried there 19 Aug., 1645, aged 90. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Fasti, i. 232.

Dod, John, s. Nehemiah, of Coggeshall, Essex, minister.; admitted to St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 17 Oct., 1646, incorp. (? as B.A.) 6 March, 1648-9, fellow Corpus Christi Coll., by the parliamentary visitors 1648, M.A. 28 April, 1649, his father rector of Doddinghurst, Essex, 1647. See Add. MS., 15,671, p. 5; Calamy, ii. 321; iii. 123; Mayor, 73; & Burrows.
Dod, Piers, subscribed 1 July, 1613.
Dod, Richard, B.A. of New Inn Hall, administration at Oxford 12 Jan., 1513. See Griffiths.

Dod, Richard, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 28 Feb., 1575-6, fellow 1581, M.A. 26 June, 1581, B.D. 15 Dec., 1590, perhaps rector of Gotham, Notts, 1590. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., x. 100.
Dod, Thomas, M.A. Cambridge, incorp. 16 July, 1600, perhaps canon of Chester and archdeacon of Richmond 1607, canon of Lichfield 1619, dean of Ripon, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Le Neve.
Dod, Thomas, B.A. from Sydney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1662, M.A. 1666; incorp. 13 July, 1669, perhaps rector of Denton, Hunts, 1667, and Connington 1699. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dod, William, M.A. Cambridge, sup. for incorporation 26 Oct., 1602.

Dodderidge, (Sir) John (Doderige, of Exeter Coll.), B.A. 16 Feb., 1576-7, created M.A. in Serjeants' Inn, London, 21 Feb., 1613-14, then a justice of the King's Bench, bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1585, serjt.-at-law 1604, knighted 5 July, 1607, King's serjeant 1607, M.P. Barnstaple 1588-9, Horsham 1604-11, solicitor-general 1604-7, justice of King's Bench 1612-28 (son of Richard Doderidge, of Barnstaple, merchant), died 13 Sept., 1628. See Foster's Judges and Barristers; Fasti, i. 355; Ath., ii. 425; & D.N.B.
Dodds, Robert (Doddys), chaplain, B.Can.L. 3 Feb., 1510-11.
Dodgson, See also Dochen and Dodson.
Dodhill, See Duddell.
Dodimer, Thomas, chaplain, sup. for B.A. & M.A. Oct., 1540. See O.H.S., i. 199.
Dodington, John, of Brasenose Coll. 1626, B.A. 17 June, 1629.
Dodson, See also Dochen and Dodgson.
Dodson, Elias, of Magdalen Hall 1580. See Dudson.
Dodson, John, chaplain, B.Can.L. 1 July, 1510.
Dodson, John, B.A. 8 July, 1521.
Dodsworth, Matthew, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1674 (incorp. 13 July, 1675), M.A. 1678, rector of Sessay, Yorks, 1690. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Dodwell, Henry, s. William, of Dublin, gent., incorp. 10 May, 1688, aged 46, from Hart Hall; fellow 1662, M.A. Trinity Coll., Dublin, Camden professor of history, 1688, until deprived in 1691, born in Dublin, Oct., 1641, died at Shottesbrooke 7th & buried there 9 June, 1711. See Fasti, ii. 404; Hearne, iii. 159, 176, 259; Rawlinson, xvii. 37; & D.N.B.
Doe, John, 1592. See Do.
Doe, Philip, s. Rich., of Kidlington, Oxon, privilegiatus 12 May, 1682.
Doelittle, William, B.A. from Christ Church 30 May, 1650.

Dogyon, George (Dogeson or Dogen), M.A., of Paris, incorp. in arts & as B.D. 8 Nov., 1542, preb. of Bristol 1542, precentor of Wells 1543, rector of Long Langton, Dorset, 1546, and of Welford, Berks, 1547, and vicar of Chew Magna, Somerset, 1552, died 14 Dec., 1552. See Le Neve, i. 171, 232; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dolbeare, John (Dolber, Dolbert, or Dolbard); B.A. 29 Jan., 1508-9, M.A. (sup. 14 June) 1512.
Dolbeare, John (Dolber), at Corpus Christi Coll. 1546, from Devon; fellow 1549, M.A. 4 July, 1553, rector of Duntsbourne Rouse, co. Gloucester, 1559. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Dolben, (Sir) William, created M.A. 8 Sept., 1665, then secretary to the Earl of Manchester (2nd son William last named), bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1655, bencher 1672, recorder of London 1676-8, and knighted 3 Feb., 1676, serjt.-at-law 1677, king's serjeant, a judge of the King's Bench 1678-83 & 1689, until his death 25 Jan., 1694, buried in the Temple church 29th. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & Fasti, ii. 285.
Dolby, Clement, created M.A. 20 Dec., 1670.
Doll, Francis, B.A. 17 June, 1510.
Dolliff, Francis, 'Bibliopola,' s. Joh., of Oxford (city); privilegiatus 5 Oct., 1681, aged 21.
Dolliff, John (Dollive), 'Bibliopola,' Oxon; privilegiatus 15 Aug., 1702.
Dolman, John, LL.D. of Cambridge, sup. for incorp. 15 Feb., 1513-14. See Dowman, page 419.

Dolman, John (Doleman or Dolmanne), B.A. 17 Dec., 1557, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1571, bencher 1588, treasurer 1598, as of Newbury, Berks (son of Thomas), licenced 28 July, 1585, to marry Mary Cornewall, widow. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Domelaw, John, M.A. Cambridge, incorp. 15 July, 1617, rector of Hambledon, Bucks, 1631. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. [Addenda: B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1612-13, M.A. 1616, B.D. 1624, D.D. from Corpus Christi 1636.]
Dominique, Charles, of St. John's Coll. 1704. See Docminique, page 409.
Domville, Silas, of New Inn Hall 1641. See Taylor.

Domville, William, B.A. from Merton Coll. 5 Feb., 1632-3; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 8 July, 1637, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1638, bencher 1658, as son and heir of Gilbert, of Dublin, arm. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Dona, William Albert, Count of, created D.C.L. 20 Dec., 1670, Swedish ambassador at the Court of St. James. See Fasti, ii. 324.
Donbarvin, Nathan, 1648. See Dunbavin.
Donhum, John, chaplain and student of canon law, disp. 24 June, 1506. See O.H.S., i. 46.
Donlee, See Dunlee.
Donne, See also Dunne.

Donne, Gabriel (Dunne), Cistercian; B.D. (sup. 26 Oct.) 1521, abbot of Buckfast, Devon, sinecure rector of Stepney, Middlesex, 1544, rector of Langtree, Devon, 1549, canon of St. Paul's 1541, until his death 5 Dec., 1558, buried in the cathedral. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Le Neve, ii. 408; O.H.S., i. 121; & D.N.B.
Donn, Gryffith (or Griffin), B.C.L. 18 Feb., 1527-8.
Donne, John (Doone or Don), B.A. 2 July, 1509.

Donne, John (Doone or Don), at Corpus Christi Coll. 14 Aug., 1523, from Exon diocese, fellow, secular chaplain, B.A. 31 Jan., 1525-6, Greek reader at Corpus Christi College, M.A. 14 July, 1528; of New Coll. & B.D. 13 July, 1538, 'afterwards dean of the chapel and well-dignified.' (Memo.: One John Dunne was preb. of Exeter 1541, and of St. Paul's 1543, vicar of Gamlingay, co. Cambridge, 1544, etc., etc.) See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Bloxam, ii. 126; Fasti, i. 79; & O.H.S., i. 317.
Donn, John, subscribed 21 Oct., 1614.
Donne, Thomas (Done), B.A. of Cambridge, ' sup. 14 June, 1544, for incorp. as M.A. abroad.' See O.H.S., i. 208.
Donne, Thomas (Dunne or Downe), of Christ Church 1547, B.A. 1547-8, M.A.; B.D. 12 Dec., 1561. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Donne-Lee, Edward and Henry, See Dunlee.
Donnell, David (Donell), B.C.L. 12 July, 1540.
Donnell, Nicholas, 1575. See Dunnell.

Donnell, Thomas, B.A. Cambridge 1541, incorp. 15 June, 1544, M.A. 13 Jan., 1544-5, incorp. at Cambridge 1546, preb. of a mediety of Oloughton alias Upton in Lichfield Cathedral 1551, vicar of Toppesfield 1551, and of Birdbrook, Essex, 1559. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Marriage Licences, ed. Foster, 411. [Addenda: B.D. Cambridge 1549.]
D'Orbee, Lewis, Baron; created M.A. 24 Sept., 1593. See Fasti, i. 266.
Dore, Robert (Doore), chaplain; 'disp. 7 March, 1505-6, B.Can.L. (sup. 28 June) 1506.' See O.H.S., i. 43.
Dorman, John, B.A. (sup. 4 May) 1520, secular chaplain, B.Can.L. 29 June, 1523.
Dorman, Thomas, B.Can.L. 25 Jan., 1505-6.
Dormer, Barnard, subscribed 2 July, 1613, of Gray's Inn 1616, as son and heir-apparent of William, of London, esquire. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Dormer, Charles, 2nd Earl of Carnarvon, created M.A. 12 April, 1648 (grandson of Philip, Earl of Pembroke), died 29 Nov., 1709. See Fasti, ii. 111.

Dormer, Philip, 5s. Robert, arm. Subscribed as of University Coll. 29 Dec., 1694; born 25 Nov., 1677, of Lincoln's Inn 1696, died s.p., brother of James 1695, and of Robert 1692. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Dormer, Robert (Derner), sup. for B.A. 26 April, 1569, of Gray's Inn 1567. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg. & Parliamentary Dictionary.
Dormer, Thomas, B.A. 17 July, 1525.
Dornell, John; B.C.L. 3 (? April) 1530.
Dorr, Abraham, a German of Hanow or Hanover, created D.Med. 9 Sept., 1680.
Dorington, Francis, created M.A. 1 Nov., 1642, perhaps father of the next named.
Dorrington, Marmaduke, M.A. Canterbury, incorp. 15 July, 1617. [Addenda: fellow Trinity Coll., Cambridge, M.A. 1595. See Al. West. 60.]

Dorrington, Theophilus, 'a reverend divine;' M.A. from Magdalen Coll. (by decree) 9 March, 1710-11, sometime a nonconformist preacher, rector of Hopton Castle, Salop, 1687, and of Wittersham, Kent, 1699, until his death 30 April, 1715. Rawlinson gives the following account of him: 'Son of Theophilus Dorrington, a tailor, born at Bow 11 Nov., 1654, educated at Merchant Taylors' School and at Leyden, became a preacher amongst the presbyterians, in 1678 he was elected one of the evening lecturers at a large room belonging to a coffee-house in Change Alley, entered Sydney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, and became a member of Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 16 June, 1686.' See Hearne, iii. 127; Rawlinson, ii. 45, v. 21, xvii. 47; Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; & D.N.B.
Dorvile, —, B.A. & student of Christ Church, died 15 Nov., 1648. See Gutch, i. 511.
Dorville, Frederick, of Aachen or Aix-la-Chapelle & Heidelburg; B.D. from Exeter Coll. 4 March, 1615-16, ' aquis granenis natione & Palatinus educatione.' See Fasti, i. 362; & O.H.S., x. 278.
Dorwood, George, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 3 May, 1636, M.A. 1 Feb., 1638-9.

D'othon, Hippocrates, D.Med. Montpelier, incorp. 12 June, 1609, died 3 Nov., 1611, buried in the church of St. Clement Danes. See Fasti, i. 335. [Addenda: a licentiate of college of physicians 1589. See Munk's Roll, i. 98.]
Dotten, Andrew (or Detton), of St. Alban Hall about 1572; B.A. 28 Feb., 1575-6.
Dottin, Thomas (Dotton), B.A. 22 March, 1522-3.
Doughty, Richard (or Dowgte), of Lincoln College; B.A. June, 1518, M.A. 22 March, 1521-2.

Doughtie, Thomas, B.A. from Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1656; M.A. 1660, incorp. 12 July, 1664, perhaps vicar of Romsey 1662, and of Bishop Stoke, Hants, 1666-98, canon of Windsor 1673, rector of Clewer, Berks, 1680, died 2 Dec., 1701, aged 65. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Douglas, George, a Scot, Aberdeen University, M.A.; incorp. 11 July, 1628, vicar of Stepney, Middlesex, 1633. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Douglas, George, s. (William), Marquis of Douglas; created D.C.L. 4 Feb., 1666-7, Earl of Dumbarton, so created 9 March, 1674-5, a major-general in the army, commander-in-chief Scotland 1685, died at St. Germain-en-Laye 20 March, 1692. See Fasti, ii. 296; & D.N.B.

Douglas, Henry (Dowglas), B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 13 Feb., 1612-13; incumbent of Pixley Chapel, annexed to Ledbury, co. Hereford, 1616, rector of Llanwarne, co. Hereford, 1617, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dounbell, James, See Dumbell.

Dove, Henry, fellow Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1658; B.A. 1661, M.A. 1665 (incorp. at Oxford 6 May, 1669), D.D. 1677, vicar of St. Bride, London, 1673, licenced 2 July, 1680, then a widower, to marry Rebecca Holworthy, of St. Margaret, Westminster, spinster, archdeacon of Richmond 1678, chaplain to Charles II., James II., King William, Queen Mary, and to his uncle Dr. Pearson, Bishop of Chester, died 11 March, 1694. See Fasti, ii. 310; Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; Alumni West., 150; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dove, John (Dooffe), Carmelite; B.D. 2 May, 1531-2.
Dove, Robert, B.C.L. 26 April, 1582.
Dovell, John (Dovall), B.A. 3 Feb., 1511-12, M.A. (sup. 8 Dec.) 1516, rector of Timberscombe 1517, of Old Cleeve 1520, died vicar of Queen or East Camel about 1541. See Fasti, i. 32.
Dovell, Robert (Davell or Dobell), LL.D., archdeacon of Northumberland 1531. See Dobell & Fasti, i. 69.

Dover, James, B.A. from Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge, 1688, M.A. 1692, incorp. 8 July, 1694, vicar of Limpenhoe and rector of Southwood, Norfolk, 1693, rector of Burlingham St. Peter 1697, and of Blofield, Norfolk, 1727-35. See Foster's Index.
Dowbin, John, B.Gram. 27 March, 1533.
Dowdon, John, 'musicus,' s. John, of Salisbury, ' Musici' privilegiatus 9 June, 1665, aged 25; administration at Oxford 17 Dec., 1689.
Dowding, Richard, B.A. (sup. May) 1533, vicar of Milbourne St. Andrew, Dorset, 1552. See Foster's Index.

Dow(e), Christopher, of Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 8 July, 1613, B.A. 1616 (incorp. as M.A. 10 July, 1621), B. & D.D., vicar of Battle, Sussex, 1629, rector of All Saints Hastings 1636. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Fasti, i. 399.

Dowell, John, M.A. from Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, incorp. 13 July, 1658 (? B.A. Christ Coll. 1648), vicar of Malton Mowbray, co. Leicester, 1660-90. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Fasti, ii. 215. [Addenda: B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1648, M.A. 1656.]

Dowland, John, B.Mus. 6 July, 1588, gentleman of the chapel royal temp. Queen Elizabeth and James I., supposed to have returned to Denmark with that king, and to have died there. See Fasti, i. 242; & D.N.B.
Dowles, John, subscribed 16 April, 1613.
Dowley, John (Dowly), subscribed 2 July, 1613; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 7 Nov., 1615; vicar of Alveston, co. Warwick, 1625, father of John and of Richard. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dowley, William, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 3 June, 1630, M.A. 14 May, 1633.

Dowman, John, LL.D. of Cambridge, sup. for incorp. 15 Feb., 1513-14, rector of Pocklington, Yorks, archdeacon of Suffolk (1526), preb. of Lichfield 1509-25, and canon residentiary of St. Paul's, died Nov., 1526, founded five scholarships and nine sizarships in St. John's Coll., Cambridge. See Cooper, i. 53.
Dowmell, Gregory, B.A. Cambridge, sup. for incorp. — Feb., 1577-8.
Downe, George (Dawen), B.C.L. 8 Feb., 1506-7, perhaps preb. of Moreton in Hereford 1529. See Le Neve, i. 513.
Downe, John, M.A.; B.D. (sup. 26 March) 1534, perhaps vicar of Felpham, Sussex, 1541. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Downe, John (or Downes), B.A. 26 June, 1543, after eight years' study, M.A. 18 March, 1543-4, one of these names vicar of Much Dewchurch, co. Hereford, 1546, and another rector of Throwleigh, Devon, 1550, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Downe, John, fellow of Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, B. & D.D., incorp. 10 July, 1600, vicar of Winsford, Somerset, 1602, rector of Instow, Devon, 1604, until his death in 1631, father of Henry 1633, and John 1637. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Fasti, i. 286; & D.N.B.
Down, Thomas, B.A. (sup. 16 June) 1514.

Downe, Thomas, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1602, incorp. as M.A. 9 July, 1605, perhaps vicar of Stratton, Cornwall, 1606, and of Buckland Bruere, Devon, 1611, and rector of Goodleigh, Devon, 1633. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Downes, Geoffrey, pensioner of Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 22 May, 1554, from Shrigley, Cheshire, B.A. 1557-8; fellow of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1558, M.A. 1561, B.D. (sup. — June) 1572, canon of Gloucester, deprived 1560, rector of Chadwell, Essex, 1568-75, and of St. Margaret's, Lothbury, 1572, deprived same year, vicar of Gillingham, Kent, 1573, rector of Bishopsbourne, Kent, 1576-95, of Little Thurrock, Essex, 1579, and of Brasted, Kent, 1580. See Cooper, ii. 182; Fasti, i. 190; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Downes, John, D.Med. Leyden 26 July, 1661, incorp. 7 Dec., 1661, fellow of the College of Physicians, London; of St. Martin's Ludgate, licensed 29 July, 1671, to marry Mrs. Christian Gale, of Putney, Surrey. See Fasti, ii. 251.
Downhall, Gregory, B.A. Cambridge; incorp. Feb., 1577-8, Greek scholar Pembroke Hall, Cambridge, a master in chancery. See Robinson, i. 1; & O.H.S., x. 364.

Downham, George, s. William, bishop of Chester; fellow Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1585, B.A. 1585 (incorp. 13 July, 1591), D.D., canon of Gloucester 1593, and of Chester 1594, rector of St. Margaret Lothbury 1596-1601, and of Great Munden, Herts, 1601, canon of St. Paul's 1598, and of Wells 1615, bishop of Londonderry 1616, died 17 April, 1634, buried in the cathedral. See Marriage Licences, ed. Foster, 416; Fasti, i. 255; Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; & D.N.B. [Addenda: M.A. 1588, B.D. 1595, D.D. 1601.]

Downing, Calibut (Downame), B.A. from Oriel Coll. 20 Nov., 1626; M.A. from Peter House, Cambridge, 1630, LL.D. 1637 (as Downing), (son of Calybute Downing, of Sherringham, co. Gloucester), rector of Ickford, Bucks, 1632, and of West Ilsley, Berks, 1632-7, vicar of Hackney, Middlesex, 1637-43, took the covenant and became one of the assembly of divines, left them and sided with the independents, one of the licencers of books of divinity 1643, died in 1644. See Ath., iii. 105; & D.N.B.
Downing, Richard (Downyng), B.A. from Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1576; incorp. 14 July, 1579, M.A. 6 Feb., 1579-80. See O.H.S., x. 364.
Dowse, (Sir) Gabriel (Douse), B.A. from Magdalen Hall 23 Feb., 1597-8; created M.A. 30 Aug., 1605, of Gray's Inn 1602, knighted 11 Nov., 1617. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg. & O.H.S., x. 237.
Dowse, John (Dowys), secular chaplain; B.C.L. 1 Aug., 1524.
Dowse, John (Douse), B.A. from Magdalen Hall 23 Feb., 1597-8.
Dowsett, James, of Christ Church 1713. See Doucett.

Dowsing, John, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1673, M.A. 1677; incorp. 10 July, 1683, rector of Ampthill, Beds, 1680, minor canon (9th) in St. Paul's 1688, rector of Stisted, Essex, 1704, of Monk's Eleigh, Suffolk, 1706, and of Cottenham, co. Cambridge, 1715. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dowsing, William (Dowsyng), B.A. — June, 1518; chantry priest Ware, Herts, 1536, and vicar 1558. See Newcourt.
Doyley, Norys (Doylie), arm. fil. n. m., demy Magdalen Coll., 1591-1601; B.A. 6 May, 1603. See Bloxam, iv. 233.
Doyne, Thomas, Eremite; B.D. (sup. 6 Feb.) 1511-12.
Dragley, William, chaplain; B.Can.L. 2 July, 1506, preb. of Southwell 1514-38. See Le Neve.

Drake, Mathew, B.A. from Broadgates Hall 25 Jan., 1610-11 (s. George, of Withycombe Rawleigh), rector of St. Stephen-juxta-Saltash, Cornwall, 1625, and of Musbury, Devon, 1630, died 1653, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., x. 269.

Drake, Nathan, s. John, of Pennegent, Yorks, yeoman, admitted sizar of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 27 Nov., 1676, aged 16, B.A. 1680, M.A. 1684, incorp. 14 July, 1684, master of the free school at Snaith 1681-9, official of the peculiar of Snaith 1684-5, vicar of Market Weighton 1689, of Sheffield, Yorks, 1694-1713, canon of York 1703, rector of Kirkby Overblows 1713, until his death April, 1729 (see Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Robinson's Snaith). Another of these names master of Pontefract School 1686, rector of South Lessingham, co. Lincoln, 1687, and canon of Southwell 1695, until his death 26 March, 1705, he was son of Samuel Drake, D.D., lately deceased, and admitted sizar of St. John's Coll. 27 April, 1681, aged 17, born at Pontefract, B.A. 1684, M.A. 1691, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Mayor.
Drake, Richard, s. Robert, of Norwich, innkeeper, admitted to Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge, Hilary term, 1688-9, aged 18; B.A. 1692, M.A. 1696, incorp. 14 July, 1712. Venn.
Drake, Robert (Draycke), B.A. (sup. 16 Feb.) 1524-5, one of these names vicar of Aylisbeare, Devon, 1545, another rector of Thundersley, Essex, 1550. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Drake, Robert, created B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 15 April, 1648, one of these names compounded for the first fruits of the rectory of West Monckton, Somerset, in Dec., 1657, ejected for nonconformity in 1662, perhaps son of George, of Aylesbeare, and buried at Pitminster 21 March, 1688-9. See Foster's Index Eccle.; Burrows, 40; & Calamy, iii. 203.
Drake, William, B.A. 16 June, 1512, M.A. 21 Jan., 1515-16. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Drake, William, gent. Exeter Coll., subscribed 7 Nov., 1655.

Drake, (Sir) William, bart., created M.A. 15 July, 1669, according to Cat. Grads., (son of Francis, of Esher, Surrey), chirographer to the House of Commons, knighted 14th and created a baronet 17 July, 1641, M.P. Agmondesham April-May, 1640, 1640 (L.P.), till secluded Dec., 1648 and 1661, until his death 28 Aug., 1669. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Draper, John, B. Mus. (sup. 30 Jan.) 1516-17.
Draper, John, Augustinian, B.D. (sup. 21 March) 1521-2; John Draper 'Prior,' vicar of Piddletown 1535, and rector of Ewerne Courtney, Dorset, 1536, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Draper, John, B.A. from Trinity Coll. 4 March, 1613-14. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Drapar, William, B.C.L. (sup. 27 June) 1506, 'sup. as secular chaplain for B.Can.L. Dec., 1530, and sup. as B.LL. for D.Can.L., Dec., 1530.' See O.H.S., i. 47.
Drax, Thomas, M.A. & B.D.; D.D. 1 July, 1510, rector of Lincoln College 1503-18, vice-chancellor 1511, canon of Lichfield 1504.
Drax, William, M.A. 5 March, 1515-16.

Draycott, Antony (Dracot or Dracox), B.A.; B.C.L. 3 Feb., 1510-11, chaplain, B.Can.L. 23 June, 1522, D.Can.L. 21 July, 1522, principal of White Hall (involved afterwards in Jesus College) and 'of Pirye Hall adjoyning,' canon of Lincoln 1539, deprived 1560, rector of North Wingfield, co. Derby, 1543, archdeacon of Stowe 1543, and of Huntingdon 1543, deprived 1560, chancellor to Dr. Longland, Bishop of Lincoln, and to Dr. Ralph Baynes, Bishop of Lichfield, in which offices he acted much against the Protestants, imprisoned in the Fleet shortly after the accession of Queen Elizabeth, rector of Draycot, co. Stafford, died 20 Jan., 1570-1. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Fasti, i. 61; & D.N.B.
Drewe, Edward, friar; B.A. (sup. in June) 1505.
Drew, Henry, B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 4 Feb., 1607-8, of the Middle Temple 1608, as 4th son of John, of Devizes, Wilts. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Drewett, William (Druett), B.A. 17 July, 1524.
Dring, Francis, B.A. from Balliol Coll., 6 Feb., 1623-4; M.A. 6 July, 1626. See O.H.S., xii. 426.
Driver, William, subscribed 28 Jan., 1613-14; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 30 June, 1617, M.A. 10 May, 1620, rector of Doddeswell, co. Gloucester, 1623. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dromant, Henry, B.A. 25 April, 1580.
Drome, Michael (Drowme, or Drum); B.A. 21 June, 1527, M.A. 10 March, 1530-1, B.D. 20 Sept., 1540, canon of Cardinal College, one of the six preachers of Canterbury. See Fasti, i. 84-85.
Drommyn, John (Drummyn), chaplain; B.A. 1 July, 1510.
Dromyn, Reginald (or Dromett), chaplain; B.A. (sup. 15 Feb.) 1507-8, M.A. 5 Feb., 1511-12, chaplain to archbishop of Dublin. See O.H.S., i. 58.

Drope, Edward, of Northants, 'cler. fil.' (Thomas). Magdalen Coll., 22 June, 1610, aged 11; chorister 1609, demy 1617, B.A. 15 Dec., 1618, fellow 1619, M.A. 14 June, 1621, B.D. 12 May, 1631, D.D. 17 June, 1661, ejected by the parliamentary visitors 1648, restored 1660, vice-president 1646, 1664, 1671, rector of Stratfield Turgis, Hants, and rector of Tubney, Berks, 1661, died 13 April, 1683, aged 84, brother of John 1601, and of Thomas 1604. See Gutch, i. 349; Bloxam, i. 33, v. 80; & Burrows.

Drope, John, of Northants, gent., of Magdalen Coll., 8 May, 1601, aged 13; chorister 1599, fellow 1608-29, B.A. 25 Jan., 1608-9, M.A. 23 May, 1612 (incorp. at Cambridge 1616), proctor 1618, B.D. 8 July, 1619, vice-president 1620, licensed to preach 26 April, 1623, rector of Grindon, co. Stafford, 1626, and of Norbury (diocese of Lichfield) 1628, until his death 29 Sept., 1629, altar tomb, Norbury, brother of Edward 1610, and of Thomas 1601. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & Bloxam, v. 4.
Drope, Robert, of Northants, 'cler. fil.,' of Magdalen Coll., 15 June, 1604, aged 10, chorister 1604-7. See Bloxam, i. 32.

Drope, Thomas, of Northants, 'cler. fil.,' of Magdalen Coll., 15 June, 1604, aged 12; chorister 1602, clerk 1609-10, B.A. 9 July, 1612, M.A. 20 May, 1615, chaplain 1617-30, B.D. 17 Dec., 1629, licensed to preach 1633, rector of Ardley, Oxon, 1629, vicar of Cumnor, Berks, 1633, buried there 27 Feb., 1644, brother of John, 1601, and of Edward 1610, and father of Francis and John 1642. See Ath., i. xxxix. note; Bloxam, ii. 47; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Drury, Edmund, B.A. 1547-8, perhaps of Lincoln's Inn 1552. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Drury, Henry, subscribed 7 May, 1613.

Drury, John (Drewrie), of Pulborough, Sussex, son of Humphrey, of Salop; B.C.L. 7 July, 1578, D.C.L. from Lincoln Coll. 17 March, 1584-5, said to have been a master in chancery, canon of Chichester 1582, rector of Pulborough, Sussex, 1589, and of Witney, Oxon, 1592, archdeacon of Oxford 1592, died at Chichester 9 June, 1614, father of Francis 1629. See Fasti, i. 228 & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Drury, William (Drewry), M.A. Cambridge, incorp. 10 July, 1593; B.D. from Queen's Coll. 27 June, 1600, rector of Leybourn, Kent, 1602, father of John 1626. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Drury, William, subscribed 7 May, 1613, perhaps of Gray's Inn 1611 (as son and heir of Anthony, of Besthorpe, Norfolk, knt.), and knighted 30 Sept., 1618, died 8 Nov., 1639. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Drusius, John, fil. Doctor John, M.A. Oxon; sometime a sojourner in Gloucester Hall; B.A. from Christ Church 26 Feb., 1605-6, 'admirably well skilled in the Hebrew, Chaldee, and Syriac tongue.' See Fasti, i. 304; & O.H.S., x. 376.
Drye, John (or Dryw), B.A. (disp. 28 April) 1507, B.D. 31 May, 1514.

Dryden, Jonathan, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1659, M.A. 1663, incorp. 6 April, 1665, rector of Cheriton, Kent, 1668, and of Scrayingham, Yorks, 1680, and of Londesborough 1680, canon of York 1683, rector of Keighley 1696, until his death 25 Aug., 1702, aged 63. See Le Neve, iii. 188; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Drysdale, Hugh, M.A. Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1679, D.D. by diploma 11 July, 1682, a vicar-choral of Ossory about 1660, chancellor 1666-8, and archdeacon of Ossory 1668-92, preacher in Kilkenny Cathedral, chaplain to James, Duke of Ormond, died in 1692, father'of Griffith last named, and of Hugh next named. See Fasti, ii. 385; & Cotton's Fasti. Eccl. Hib. ii.
Drysdale, John, B.A. Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1686 (as Driesdale), incorp. 11 July, 1690, created M.A. 12 July, 1690.
Ducane, John (Dukane), B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1623, incorp. 13 July, 1624, one of these names born 31 Jan., 1600, died 22 April, 1684. [Addenda: M.A. 1627.]
Ducke, Richard, B.A. (sup.) 1548-9, M.A. 27 Nov., 1553.

Ducke, William, B.A. 17 Feb., 1538-9; fellow Exeter Coll., 1541, M.A. 9 Oct., 1544 (as 'Dauke'), of Christ Church 1547, one of these names rector of Thornton-in-le-Moor, Cheshire, 1553. See O.H.S., i. 193; Boase, 36; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ducket, Thomas, 'Hortulanus e Coll. Exon.;' privilegiatus 26 June, 1624, aged 55.
Duckett, William (Dukytt), B.C.L. 2 July, 1525-6.
Ducket, William, created M.A. 31 Aug., 1636. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Duckfield, Daniel, fellow Christ Coll., Cambridge; B.A. 1686, M.A. 1690, incorp. 13 July, 1700.

Duckfield, John, B.A. from St. Catherine Hall, Cambridge, 1645; incorp. as M.A. 18 Jan., 1649-50, vicar of Layston, Herts, 1663, and of Aspeden, Herts, 1670. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. [Addenda: M.A. 1647.]

Duckworth, Charles, of Brasenose College 1611; B.A. 21 June, 1615, M.A. 9 July, 1618, vicar of Wrockwardine, Salop, 1617, rector of Dodlestone, Cheshire, 1635, canon of Chester 1635, until his death 8 Oct., 1673, father of William 1640. See Le Neve, iii. 271; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dudicote, William, of St. Edmund Hall 1602. See Dedicott, page 391.

Dudley, Ambrose, Earl of Warwick, K.G.; created M.A. 6 Sept., 1566, of the Inner Temple 1562, created M.A. Cambridge 10 Aug., 1564 (eldest son of the attainted John Dudley, Duke of Northumberland), restored in blood 7 March, 1558, created Baron Lisle 25 and Earl of Warwick 26 Dec., 1561, K.G. 1563, died 20 Feb., 1589-90. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.; Fasti, i. 177; & D.N.B.
Dudley, Edmund, s. John, speaker House of Commons 1504; M.P. Lewes 1491-2, co. Stafford 1504, beheaded 28 Aug., 1510. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary & Ath., i. 12.

Dudley, Richard, M.A.; proctor 1503, D.D. (sup. 24 Oct.), 1508, of Oriel College, principal of St. Mary Hall 1502-6, canon of St. Paul's 1505, of Lincoln 1508, and of York 1508, died about 1536. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dudley, Robert, Earl of Leicester, Queen Elizabeth's favourite; chancellor of the University 1564-85, died at Cornbury 4 Sept., 1588. See Cooper, ii. 30; & D.N.B.
Dudley, Robert (Dudlee), fellow Queen's Coll.; B.A. 5 April, 1566, M.A. 5 July, 1568 ('as Peter'), canon of York 1569. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dudley, William, bishop of Durham 1476, chancellor of the University 1483, died 29 Nov., 1483. See Dictionary of National Biography.
Dudson, See also Dodson.
Duel, See Dewell, page 400.

Duffield, John, B.A. Cambridge 1568, incorp. as B.D. 12 July, 1580, canon of St. Paul's 1579, rector of Lopham, Norfolk, 1577, and of Acle 1580, canon of St. Paul's 1580, etc., etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. [Addenda: M.A. 1572, D. D. 1579.]
Duffield, Ralph (Duffyld or Duffild), B.A. 17 March, 1534-5, as 'Robert.'
Dugdale, Christopher (Dugdaile), of Brasenose Coll., B.A. 17 Feb., 1564-5, rector of Calston, Wilts, 1569, probably father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Dugdale, James (Dugdell), B.A. 1545, master of University Coll. 1558, until deprived 1561, archdeacon of St. Alban's 1567, deprived 1560, rector of Higham, co. Leicester, 1586, perhaps vicar of Almsford, Somerset, 1590, until his death in 1594. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Dugdale, (Sir) John, s. Sir William Dugdale, garter king of arms; created M.A. 9 Sept., 1661, incorp. at Cambridge 1664, Windsor herald 1676, Norroy king of arms and knighted 1686, of Gray's Inn 1665, died 3 Aug., 1700. See Fasti, ii. 253.

Dugdale, (Sir) William, s. John; created M.A. 1 Nov., 1642, of Blythe, co. Warwick, author of 'The History of Warwickshire,' 'The Monasticon Anglicanum,' 'Baronage of England,' etc., of Gray's Inn 1660, Blanche Lyon, pursuivant of arms extraordinary 1638, Rouge Croix 1639, Chester herald 1644, compounded for his estate, Norroy king of arms 1660, and Garter 1677, knighted 25 May, 1677, and died 10 Feb., 1685, father of John 1661. See Fasti, ii. 13; & D.N.B.

Duke, Edward, of Cambridge, D.Med. from Gloucester Hall 9 Aug., 1660 (perhaps B.A. from Catharine Hall., Cambridge, 1636), 3rd son of George, of Wandsworth; hon. fellow College of Physicians 1664. See Munk's Roll, i. 336.
Duke, William, M.A. Cambridge, incorp. 11 July, 1611.

Dukeson, Richard, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1616, D.D. Cambridge 1637, incorp. 12 Aug., 1645, rector of Fiskerton, co. Lincoln, 1630, of Ledbury, co. Hereford, 1660-4, and of St. Clement Danes, London, 1634 (sequestered 1647 but restored), until his death 17 Sept., 1678. See Add. MS., 15,671, p. 145; Fasti, ii. 85,100; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. [Addenda: M.A. 1620, B.D. 1627, and D.D. 1637.]
Dulse, Leonard, B.Med. (sup. 8 April) 1530.
Dumarescq, Elias, of Guernsey, gent., privilegiatus 19 July, 1594, aged 12; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 25 Oct., 1599.
Dumarescq, Philip, of Guernsey, gent., privilegiatus 19 July, 1594, aged 14.
Dumbell, James; B.A. (sup. 16 Feb.) 1569-70.
Dunbar, Thomas; M.A. Aberdeen University, incorp. 20 Jan., 1706-7, vicar of Shalford 1689, rector of Faulkbourn 1690, and vicar of Kelvedon 1707, all in Essex. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Duncan, Peter, B.A. 27 April, 1574, M.A. 5 June, 1576, incorp. at Cambridge 1578, rector of Lidford 1580, vicar of Crediton 1584, and rector of Kenn 1595, all in Devon, father of the next named. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus & O.H.S., x. 368.

Dunch, (Sir) William, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 19 Feb., 1595-6; of Gray's Inn 1596 (as son and heir of Edmund, of Wittenham, Berks), M.P. Wallingford 1604-11, knighted 9 July, 1603, died 22 Jan., 1611-12, brother of Samuel and Walter, and father of William next named. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Duncombe, Michael, 'arm. fil. nat. max.,' B.A. from St. Alban Hall 7 July, 1615; of the Inner Temple 1618, and late of Clifford's Inn, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Duncombe, Thomas, B.A. from Peter House, Cambridge, 1604; incorp. as M.A. 8 July, 1614.
Dungan, John, created M.A. 1 Nov., 1642; perhaps of Gray's Inn 1623, as of Castleton, co. Kildare, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Dungan, Walter, created M.A. 1 Nov., 1642.
Dungan, William, of St. Mary Hall 1604. See Dongan.
Dunham, William, B.C.L. (sup. 10 Feb.) 1515-16.
Dunham, William, M.A.; bishop of Chester 1561-77, B. & D.D. (sup. 13 July) 1562.

Dunkon, John, B.A. from Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1623; incorp. as M.A. 15 July, 1628, rector of Stutton and Stoke St. Mary, Suffolk, 1637, of Bexwell, Norfolk, 1641, and of Rettenden, Essex, 1642. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. [Addenda: M.A. 1627, B.D. 1634.]

Dunkyn, William, B.A. from Clare Coll., Cambridge, 1619; incorp. as M.A. 15 July, 1628, vicar of St. Laurence, Isle of Thanet (Ramsgate), 1629, canon of Canterbury 1639. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus. [Addenda: M.A. 1623.]
Dunne, See also Done & Donne.
Dunn, Charles, B.A. from Christ Church 12 June, 1588, M.A. 9 July, 1591.
Dunn, Francis, B.A. from Exeter Coll. 3 March, 1613-14.

Dunne, (Sir) Patrick, D.Med., of Aberdeen, in Scotland, and of Valentia, in Dauphiny, and of Trinity Coll., Dublin, incorp. 19 Feb., 1677-8 (2s Charles, of Aberdeen, dyer), fellow Dublin College of Physicians, 'physician to the State' and physician in ordinary to James, Duke of Ormond, lord-lieutenent of Ireland 1677, several times president, and one of the founders of the Dublin Philosophical Society in 1683, knighted by the lords justices 1696, physiciangeneral of the army 1704, M.P. Killileagh, co. Down, 1692, Mullingar 1695, 1703, died 24 May, 1713. See Fasti, ii. 363; & D.N.B.
Dunning, See also Downing.

Dunscomb, Thomas, student Christ Church 1577, B.A. 23 Jan., 1580-1, M.A. 6 March, 1583-4, incorp. at Cambridge 1587, rector of St. Thomas Apostle, London, 1587, of Kingsnorth, Kent, 1591. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Dunstan, Antony (Dunstone, Dunston, or Downston), Benedictine, B.D. 1525, D.D. 29 July, 1538, prior of the students of Gloucester College 1526, abbot of Eynsham 1532, bishop of Llandaff 1545 (as 'Kitchin, alias Dunstan'), until his death 31 Oct., 1563. See Ath., ii. 796; Fasti, i. 70; & O.H.S., i. 140. [Addenda: perhaps B.A. Cambridge 1510. See Cooper, i. 238.]
Dunster, John, regular Augustinian canon, B.D. 22 June, 1530, one of these names vicar of Oborne, Dorset, 1547, another rector of Kingdon, Somerset. 1561. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Duppa, (Sir) Thomas, created D.C.L. 20 Dec., 1670 (only son of John Duppa, of Eardisley, co. Hereford, and possibly nephew of Brian, Bishop of Winchester), senior gentleman usher and daily waiter to his Majesty, usher of the black rod 1683, knighted at Windsor 6 May, 1683, died 25 April, 1694, buried in Westminster Abbey. See Fasti, ii. 325.
Dupper, Samuel, of Wellford, co. Gloucester, privilegiatus 29 Oct., 1680, aged 28; 'Banbury carrier.'
Durant, Francis, subscribed 4 Nov., 1614, B.A. from Exeter Coll. 22 April, 1618, M.A. 26 June, 1622.
Durant, Thomas, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1615, incorp. as M.A. 13 July, 1619.
Duredon, Abel, B.A. from Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1675; M.A. 1679, incorp. 12 July, 1681.
Durham, Richard, of Magdalen Hall 1626. See Durram.
Durham, Roger, secular chaplain; B.Can.L. (sup. 4 Feb.) 1527-8.
Durham, Samuel, Magdalen Hall, 1672. See Derham, page 397.
Durnford, Thomas, of Broadgates Hall 1615. See Dunford.
Duttone, Andrew, of St. Alban Hall in and before 1572; see also Dotten & Thomas 1577.
Dutton, James, subscribed 12 Dec., 1634.
Dutton, John, of Christ Church 1553; B.A. 26 Jan., 1554-5, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1567. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Dutton, Richard, of Hart Hall in and before 1568.
Dutton, Thomas (Dotton), B.A. 22 March, 1522-3. See also Dottin.
Duttun, Thomas, B.A. 20 July, 1533.
Dutton, William, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 10 Feb., 1600-1, M.A. 10 July, 1607. See O.H.S., xii. 224.
Duvall, Henry, B.A. 30 June, 1516.
Dwight, John, B.C.L. from Christ Church 17 Dec., 1661, 'potter,' father of the four of his name here mentioned—Plott's Oxon, 1677 edn., & Alumni West., 207. See also D.N.B.
Dyer, Birinus (Dier), B.A. 9 May, 1509.
Dyer, (Sir) Edward, of Sharpham Park, Somerset, M.P. Somerset 1588-9 & 1593, chancellor of the order of the garter and knighted 1596, died 11 May, 1607. See Ath., i. 740.
Dyer, Henry (Dier), secular chaplain; B.A. 21 June, 1527, M.A. 21 June, 1533.
Dyer, James, of Broadgates Hall, chief justice of Common Pleas 1559, died 24 March, 1582. See Ath., i. 480; Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; Foss; & D.N.B.
Dyer, John, B.A. (sup. 18 Nov.) 1506.
Dyer, John (Diar), B.A. 27 June, 1522, M.A. 6 Feb., 1525-6, one of these names rector of St. James, Westminster, 1531. See Newcourt.
Dyer, Nicholas, B.A. — Feb., 1525-6.
Dyer, Richard, B.A. 28 Feb., 1575-6, perhaps of the Middle Temple 1576, as son and heir of Laurence, of Wincanton, Somerset, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Dyer, Robert, B.A. 9 Nov., 1525.
Dyer, Thomas (Dyar), B.A. 22 April, 1574, perhaps rector of Fiddington, Somerset, 1577-87. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Dygon, John, Benedictine. B.Mus. (sup. 28 March) 1512. See Grove's Dictionary of Music, iv. 625; & D.N.B.
Dygon, John, B.C.L. 13 July, 1533.

Dyke, Daniel (Dike), B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1596, M.A. from Sydney Sussex Coll. 1599, and fellow 1606, B.D., incorp. as M.A. 15 July, 1602, puritan divine, born at Hemel Hempstead, Herts, minister of Coggeshall, Essex; one William Dyke compounded for the first-fruits of Hemel Hempstead, Herts, 18 Jan., 1594-5. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus; Fasti, i. 298; & D.N.B. [Addenda: B.D. 1506.]
Dyke, Richard, of co. Hereford; M.A. and chaplain of New Coll., died in 1604. See Gutch, i. 219.
Dymocke, Robert, (Dimmock, Dymmok or Democke), of Brasenose College 1551; B.A. 1 Feb., 1553-4, M.A. 25 Feb., 1555-6, fellow in 1565, then 'mil. fil.' (Edward), died in 1580.
Dyneley, See also Dingley.
Dyneley, John, B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1609; incorp. as M.A. 8 July, 1614. [Addenda: M.A. 1613.]
Dynham, Edward, D.Med. Montpelier, 19 March, 1639, incorp. 3 April, 1641, a Londoner born. See Fasti, ii. 4.
Dynham, George (Dinham), B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 26 May, 1642; see John Denham, of Magdalen Coll. 1672, page 393.
Dyos, See also Deyos.
Dyott, John (Diett or Diot), of Corpus Christi Coll. 1524, from Dorset; fellow 1525, B.A. 31 Jan., 1525-6. See Fasti, i. 45.

Dyott, (Sir) Richard, (s. Anthony, of Lichfield), subscribed 12 Oct., 1604, B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 9 Dec., 1607, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1615, recorder of Stafford, M. P. Stafford 1621-2 & 1624-5, Lichfield 1625, 1626, 1628-9, & April to May, 1640, knighted at Dublin 13 Sept., 1635, privy councillor to Charles I. in the Council at York, high steward of Lichfield and chancellor of the county palatine of Durham, died 8 March, 1659, aged 69, father of Anthony and brother of Robert. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & Parliamentary Dictionary.
Dyrham, See Derham, page 397, & Durham, page 435.
Dyson, Richard, B.A. 6 May, 1534.

Dyve, (Sir) Lewis, subscribed 21 Feb., 1613-14 (son of Sir John, of Bromham, Beds), a royalist, knighted 19 April, 1620, M.P. Bridport 1625 & 1626, Melcombe Regis 1628-9, colonel of the 11th regiment in the king's army, taken prisoner several times during the Civil Wars, died 17 April, 1669. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; D.N.B.; see also Dive.
Eales, John,, of St. Alban Hall., 1607. See Eles.
Eames, See also Emes.
Eames, Daniel, of St. Mary Hall, B.A. 26 June, 1585; M.A. from St. John's Coll. 23 March, 1587-8. See O.H.S. xii. 130.
Eande, Richard, of Brasenose Coll, 1638. See Eaude.
Eares, Samuel, pleb., subscribed, 3 March, 1639-40.
Earle, See also Erle.

Earle, Ralph, of Pembroke Hall.Cambridge, M.A. per Literas Regias, 1675; incorp. 13 July, 1675, perhaps a student of Lincoln's Inn, 1676, as son and heir of John, late of Heydon, Norfolk, esq., deceased. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Earle, Thomas (Erle), at Corpus Christi Coll., 1532, from Devon; B.A. (sup. June), 1535, died 1538. See Gutch, i. 412.
Earnshaw, Edward, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 1 June, 1716.
Earth, See Erth.
Eason, Nathaniel, of All Souls' Coll., 1619. See Eston.
Easte, Francis, subscribed 15 Oct., 1634.
East, John, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 17 June, 1624.

East, William (Est), Benedictine, B.D. 3 July, 1539, canon of Windsor 1554, rector of St. Mary Magdalen, Milk Street, London, 1554, (1st) archdeacon of St. Albans, and rector of St. Albans, Herts, resigned both in 1556. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Easton, See also Eston.
Eastwick, See Estwick.
Eaton, Christopher, of Oxford city, "Brewer," privilegiatus, 4 Aug., 1704. Will at Oxford proved 30 Oct. 1719.
Eaton, Guy, 1535., See Etton.

Eaton, John, B.A., Cambridge, 24 years in theology, incorp. 7 July, 1572, B.D. 16 July, 1572; one of these names vicar of Powick, co. Worcester, 1561, and another rector of St. Leonard, Shoreditch, 1563. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. x. 363.
Eaton, Ralph (Elton), B.A. 22 Nov., 1571, M.A. 1 Feb., 1575-6, rector of Wick Rissington, co. Gloucester. 1575, and of Kemerton, co. Gloucester, 1582. See Foster's Index Eccl. See also Eyton.
Eaton, Richard, B.A. from Brasenose Coll., 12 June, 1611, M.A. 7 July, 1614; one of these names vicar of Audlem, Cheshire, 1642. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Eaton, Richard, subscribed 23 April, 1613; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 10 Feb., 1615-16.
Eaton, Robert, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 6 July, 1577, M.A. 26 May, 1587, rector of Mobberley, Cheshire, 1595-1621; father of Samuel 1602. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. xii. 69.

Eaton, Robert, B.A. Trinity Coll., Dublin, 9 Feb., 1642-3, M.A. 7 March, 1645-6, incorp. 4 July, 1648, and fellow of Brasenose Coll., in place of Thomas Church ejected; one of these names rector of Pangbourne, Berks, 1647, and vicar of Cuddesdon. Oxon, 1648. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Burrows, 175.

Eaton, Robert, 7 years of Cambridge, created M.A. 15 July, 1653, as of All Souls' Coll.; settled in Essex, removed to Walton, co. Lancaster, ejected 1660, chaplain to Lord Delamere, died in Manchester 1 Aug., 1701. See Calamy, ii. 380.

Eaton, Samuel, s. Richard, vicar of Gt. Budworth, Cheshire, beneficed at West Kirby, Cheshire, suspended 1631, fled to Holland, was in New England 1637-40, chaplain to the parliamentary garrison at Chester, nonconformist preacher at Dukinfield, Cheshire, before 1645 until his death, 9 Jan., 1664-5, aged 68. See Ath. iii. 672; Calamy, ii. 361; Earwaker's East Cheshire, ii. 28-35; & D.N.B.
Eaton, Thomas, subscribed 18 June, 1613. perhaps a student of Lincoln's Inn 1615, as son and heir of Randle, of Flints, gen. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Eaton, William, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 4 Nov., 1710. See also Eyton.
Ebden, John (or Hebden), B.A. from Magdalen Hall 26 May, 1585, M.A. 3 July, 1592, canon of S. Paul's 1596, dead before 22 March, 1597-8. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 3.
Eccleston, Thomas (Ekylston), Benedictine; supl. for B.Can.L. 4 April, 1506.
Eckersall, Peter, B.A. from Emmanuel Coll., *Cambridge; incorp. 13 July, 1591. [Addenda: B.A. from Emmanuel 1588-9.]
Eddowe, Thomas (Edowe or Edoo), B.A. 1 Aug. 1541, M.A. 1545, B.Med. (sup.) 1548.
Ede, Richard, regular canon; B.Mus. (sup. 16 Feb.), 1506-7; one of these names last Abbot of Burtonon-Trent. See Fasti, i. 33; & O.H.S. i. 51.
Edelen, Christopher, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1652-3, M.A. from Sydney Coll. 1656, incorp. 14 July, 1657.
Edelinge, William, of Hart Hall, in and before 1568. See also Edlyn.
Eden, See also Edon.
Edes, See also Eedes.
Edes, William (Edys), Benedictine, B.D. (sup. 12 June), 1516.

Edgar, Ezeckiel, M.A. Cambridge, incorp. 12 July, * 1603, rector of Hawstead, Suffolk, 1608, and perhaps rector of Great Stanmore, Middlesex, 1662, until his death in the following year. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,670, p. 106. [Addenda: B.A. from Emmanuel 1598-9, M.A. 1602.]
Edgcombe, Joseph, Piers, of Mount admitted to Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, 7 Nov., 1646; B.A. from New Inn Hall 21 Nov., 1650.

Edgcombe, Piers, of Mount Edgcombe, created D.C.L. 21 March, 1643-4 (son of Sir Richard), M.P. Newport (Cornwall) 1628-9, Camelford AprilMay, 1640, and the Long Parliament, until disabled in Jan., 1644, M.P. Newport about Feb., 1662, until his death, 6 Jan., 1666. Wood states that he sat in the parliament at Oxford, "for which he also suffered in his estate;" father of Francis and of the next-named. See Fasti, ii. 66.
Edge, Giles, B.A. Cambridge, perhaps supplic. for incorporation 23 July, 1574. See O.H.S. x. 363 & xii. 45.

Edgeley, George (Cantab), created D.D. 22 Feb., * 1643-4, prebendary of Chichester 1629, vicar of Donnington 1630, and of Lymister 1634, rector of Nuthurst (all in Sussex) 1642-7. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Fasti, ii. 69. [Addenda: B.A. from Trinity 1623-4, M.A. 1627.]
Edguard, David, of Oxford, and then of Cambridge (circa 1532). See Cooper, i. 46.
Edgworth, John, B.A. (sup. 28 Jan.), 1518-19.
Edlin, See also Edelen.

Edlin, Philip, B.A. from Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge*, 1617, incorporated as M.A. 10 July, 1617, rector of North Cray, Kent, 1628, of Alverstoke, Hants, 1631, and of St. John Zachary, London, 1635, of Bushey, Herts, 1643. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & Add. MS. 15,670, p. 60. [Addenda: B.A. from Emmanuel 1617-18, M.A. 1621, B.D. 1628.]
Edlin, Samuel, fellow Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; *M.A. 1660, incorporated 13 July, 1669, rector of Silchester, Hants, 1667. See Foster's Index Eccl. [Addenda: B.A. from Magdalen 1656-7.]
Edlyn, William, of Hart Hall, in or before 1572. See also Edelinge.
Edmonds, (—) M.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, created D.D. 20 Dec., 1642.
Edmonds, Ambrose (Edmunds), fellow of New Coll., 1555-60, from Winchester; B.A. 12 Dec., 1558. See O.H.S. i.
Edmonds, Henry, of St. John's Coll., in and before 1566; one of these names vicar of Watford, Herts. 1573. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edmonds, Henry, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 30 Oct., 1616, M.A. 9 July, 1619. See O.H.S. xii. 349.
Edmonds, John (Edmunds), B.A. (sup. May), 1566, perhaps of Brasenose Coll.
Edmonds, Nicholas, subscribed 5 June, 1613.

Edmonds, Richard (Edmunds), fellow of New Coll., 1548-58, from Winslow, Bucks; B.C.L. 8 July, 1556, rector of Wavendon, Bucks, 1571, of Shenley 1574; another of these names rector of Ludgarshall 1611. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. i.
Edmondes, Richard, of co. Hereford, pleb. St. Mary Hall, 11 Oct., 1583, aged "8"; B.A. 4 Feb., 1591-2.
Edmonds, Silvester, student of Christ Church, 1571, B.A. 19 Feb., 1573-4, M.A. 27 June, 1577. See O.H.S. xii. 39.

Edmonds, Thomas, born in Surrey, chorister Magdalen Coll., 1553, clerk 1559, fellow 1564-9, from co. Lincoln; B.A. 26 April, 1566, B.D. 21 March, 1589-90; one of these names rector of St. Mary Magdalen, Milk Street, 1571, and of Llanbedr, co. Brecon, 1582, prebendary of Llandaff 1584. See Foster's Index Eccl.; O.H.S. i. 232, 259; & Bloxam, i. 15, ii. 40.

Edmonds, Thomas (Edmunds), B.A. from University Coll. 30 Jan., 1582-3, M.A. 7 July, 1585, B.D. 10 July, 1601; one of these names rector of Little Nazing, alias Little Snoring, Norfolk, 1585, of All Hallows, Bread Street, London, 1585, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. xii. 110.
Edmonds, Thomas, gent. New Coll., (subs.) 7 Nov., 1655, B.A. 20 June, 1659, M.A. 16 May, 1663, rector of Tingewick, Bucks, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edmonds, William, B.A. 9 Feb., 1574-5, M.A. 6 July, 1577; one of these names vicar of Rickmansworth, Herts, 1590-1621, and of Taplow, Bucks, 1615-23. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edmonds, William, of Salop, pleb., privilegiatus 17 Jan., 1600-1, aged 33.
Edon, See also Eden.
Edon, William (Eden or Eydon), B.A. 18 July, 1524, M.A. 15 April, 1529.
Edon, William, student residing in the town in July, 1562, tutor Nicholas Balche of Magdalen Coll.
Edwardes, Christopher, B.A. (sup. 20 June), 1510.
Edwards, David (Eduardys), at Corpus Christi Coll., 1517, from Northampton; B.A. 16 Dec., 1522, fellow 1522, M.A. 4 July, 1525. See Thomas, 830, & O.H.S. i.
Edwards, David, B.A. 30 Jan., 1562-3.
Edwards, Elias, B.A. from Hart Hall 9 July, 1600, rector of Liddington, Wilts, 1625; father of Edmond, 1623. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edwards, George, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 29 Jan., 1601-2.

Edwards, Humfry (Edwardis), B.A.; M.A. (sup.) 1548, B.D. 22 Nov., 1554; one of these names rector of Llandrillo, co. Merioneth, rector of St. Mary Wolnoth, London, 1549, of Caerwys, Flints, 1550, archdeacon of St. Asaph 1554, ejected temp. Q. Eliz. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edwards, Israel, M.A., Cambridge, incorp. 10 July, *1610, vicar of Gt. Bentley, Essex, 1615, and rector of East Mersea 1615. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edwards, John, B.C.L. 28 Feb., 1518-19.
Edwards, John (Edwardys), B.A. 23 June, 1524, M.A. 14 July, 1528, "admitted B.Med. and to practice 6 July, 1532," D. Med. (sup. 26 March), 1534.
Edwardes, John, of Hart Hall. B.A. 17 Dec., 1569.
Edwards, John, student of Christ Church, 1569. B.A. 26 Feb., 1571-2, M.A. 5 May, 1575; one of these names rector of Llanwyddelan, co. Montgomery, 1582. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. xii. 13.
Edwards, John, B.A. from Christ Church 30 Oct., 1598, M.A. 20 June, 1601; one of these names rector of Langstone, co. Monmouth, 1608. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. xii. 211.

Edwards, John, B.A. from Pembroke Coll., Cambridge*, 1611, incorp. 20 May, 1617; one of these names rector of (part of) Pakefield, Suffolk, 1617, and rector of Oxwick, Norfolk, 1623. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Edwards, John, fellow Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A. 1680, M.A. 1684, incorp. 26 June, 1710, vicar of Nannerch and Rhuddlan, Flints, 1686, rector of Newtown, co. Montgomery, 1688, sinecure rector of Llansantffiaid and rector of Llanfihangel-yn-gwnfa, co. Montgomery, 1691, canon of St. Asaph 1705. See Thomas, 760, 764; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Edwards, John, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 14 Oct., 1717. See also David.
Edwards, Philip, secular chaplain, B.Can. L. 5 Feb., 1531-2; one of these names rector of Chilton Egg, Berks, 1540, vicar of Ellesmere, Salop, 1547. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edwards, Richard, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 16 March, 1593-4.
Edwards, Richard, B.A. from Pembroke Coll. 28 April, 1629, M.A. 27 Jan., 1631-2.
Edwards, Richard, "Chyrurgus, s. Denis, of university Oxon, gent. Privilegiatus 23 Jan., 1645-6, aged 36.

Edwards, Samuel, s. Samuel, of Elmden, Essex, gent., adm. sizar St. John's College., Cambridge, 5 March, 1659-60, aged 14; B.A. 1663-4, M.A. 1667, incorp. 13 July, 1669; one of these names beneficed in Suffolk, rector of Great Saxham 1676, vicar of Eye 1682, and rector of Troston 1686. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Mayor, 144.
Edwardes, Thomas, of Magdalen Hall, student residing in the town in July, 1562; tutor, — Hammon, of Magdalen Coll.

Edwards, Thomas, admitted to Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 15 June, 1575, B.A. 1578-9, M.A. 1582, incorp. 12 July, 1623, 'a zealous puritan, author of "Gangraena."' See Allibone, i. 547; Fasti, i. 413; & D.N.B.
Edwards, Thomas, B.A. from New Coll. 26 March, 1582.
Edwards, Thomas, B.A. from Hart Hall. 3 May, 1620, M.A. 25 June, 1623.
Edwards, Thomas, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 9 June, 1638, possibly of Lincoln's Inn 1636, as of Westwood, co. Worcester, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Edwards, Thomas, B.A. from New Coll. 12 Nov., 1657, M.A. 26 March, 1659.
Edwards, Thomas, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1673, M.A. 1677, incorp. 9 July, 1685; one of these names vicar of Newnham, Herts, 1680. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Rawl. iv. 86.

Edwards, Thomas, s. William, of Taplow, Bucks, cler., admitted to Caius Coll., Cambridge, 15 July, 1674, aged 19; B.A. 1678, M.A. 1683; incorp. from Christ Church 10 July, 1685 (Wm. Edwards instituted to rectory of Taplow 1615 and 1623). See Venn, 279.
Edwards, Timothy, B.A. from Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 1705-6, M.A. 1709, incorp. 11 July, 1709, vicar of Okehampton, Devon, 1717. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Edwards, William (Edwardys), M.A.; fellow of Exeter Coll. 1483, B D. (sup. 26 May), 1506, perhaps canon of Hereford 1513, etc. See Le Neve, i. 525.
Edwards, William, B.A. 7 April, 1565.
Edwards, William, B.A. 3 April, 1574.
Edwards, William, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 26 Jan., 1637-8, M.A. 17 April, 1641 (as Edmunds).
Edwards, Zachariah, "Pharmacopola", 13 Oct., 1674, Admon. at Oxford, 10 Feb. 1704; father of Henry 1689.

Eede(s), Francis, gent. Christ Church, (subs.) 18 March, 1656-7, B.A. 17 Jan., 1659-60, M.A. 1662, B. and D. Med. 1674, as Eedes; fellow college of physicians 1677, died in 1683. See Munk's Roll, i. 396; Alumni West. 145.
Eede, William, B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1660-1, M.A. 1664, incorp. 12 July, 1664, rector of Horsheath, co. Cambridge, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Eedes, See also Edes.

Eedes, John, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, *1610; incorp. as M.A. 13 July, 1619, perhaps rector of Lawford, Essex, 1616-59; licenced 13 Dec., 1616, to marry Thomazine, daughter of late Baldwin Dereham, deceased. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.
Eedes, John, B.A. from Katherine Hall., Cambridge*, 1650, incorp. as M.A. 10 July, 1655, vicar of Lillington, co. Warwick, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Eedes, Toby, B.A. from Christ Church 17 June, 1613, M.A. 20 June, 1616. See O.H.S. xii. 320.
Eedes, William (Edes), B.A. 9 Feb., 1579-80.
Effard, Nicholas, B.A. (sup. 18 April) 1575.

Egerton, John, of co. Chester, arm. Brasenose Coll., 17 Oct., 1589, aged 10; B.A. 4 July, 1594, created M.A. 30 Aug., 1605, M.P. Callington 1597, Salop 1601, a baron of the Exchequer of Chester 1599-1605, K.B. 24 July, 1603; a student of Lincoln's Inn 1595 (as son of Sir Thomas, master of the rolls), Earl of Bridgwater, so created 27 May, 1617, etc.; died 4 Dec., 1649; father of John, 2nd Earl, M.A. 1663. See D.N.B. & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Egerton, John, 2nd Earl of Bridgwater, M.A. by diploma 24 May, 1663, high steward of the University 1663-86, a student of Gray's Inn 1633, etc.; died 26 Oct., 1686. See D.N.B. & Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Egerton, Ralph, B.A. from Wadham Coll. 1699. See Eggerdon.
Egerton, Richard, 1526. See Hectherton.

Egerton, Stephen, B.A. from Peter House, Cambridge, 1575-6, M.A. 1579, incorp. 9 July, 1583, admitted of Gray's Inn 1588, preacher at St. Ann's, Blackfriars, in London, buried there 7 May, 1622; made his will 12 April preceding, and proved 3 June. See Fasti, i. 224; Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.; Waters' Chesters of Chicheley, i. 281; & D.N.B.

Egerton, Thomas, Baron Ellesmere, chancellor of the university 1610-1616 (natural son of Sir Richard, of Ridley, Cheshire); of Brasenose Coll. 1556, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1572, treasurer 1588, solicitor-general of England 1581-92, attorneygeneral 1592-4, chamberlain of Chester 1593 and knighted, master of the rolls 1594-1603, lord keeper 1596, created Baron Ellesmere 19 July, 1603, and lord chancellor on 24th, created Viscount Brackley 7 Nov., 1616, died 15 March, 1617. See Ath. ii. 197; Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 313; & D.N.B.
Egerton, Thomas, B.A. from Peter House, Cambridge*, 1567-8, incorp. as M.A. 9 July, 1583, perhaps bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1572. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Egleston, William, B.A. from Trinity Coll., *Cambridge, 1586; incorp. as M.A. 13 July, 1591. [Addenda: M.A. from Trinity 1590.]
Egleton, Francis, "apothecary, s. Henry, of Wotton Underwood, Bucks, privilegiatus 14 Sept., 1666, aged 27; will at Oxford proved 26 May, 1677; brother of William.
Eglionbye, Edward, of Queen's Coll.1631. See Aglionby, page 10.
Eier, William (Eirus), D.Med. Leyden; incorp. 8 July, 1608. See Fasti, i. 328; & Munk's Roll, i. 178.
Einsworth, Samuel, of Oriel Coll., 17 Feb., 1631-2. See Ainsworth, page 10.

Ekins, Alexander, B.A. from Clare Hall., Cambridge, 1675-6, M.A. 1679, incorp. 14 July, 1684, (probably son of Alexander, of Weston Favell, Northants), baptised 11 Nov., 1656, vicar of Weekley, Northants, 1682, rector of Barton Seagrave, Northants, 1686; another of these names rector of Orlingbury, Northants, 1656. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Eland, John, student residing in the town in July, 1562. Tutor, — Godall. See O.H.S. i. 219.
Elborow, Henry, B.A. from St. John's Coll., *Cambridge, 1614, incorp. as M.A. 15 July, 1617. [Addenda: M.A. from St. John's 1618.]
Elcocke, Alexander, B.A. (supl.) 1547-8, of Christ Church, 1550, M.A. supl. 1551-2.
Elder, Arthur, M.A. Cambridge, incorp. 1556. See Yeldard or Yelder.
Elder, John, a Scot, M.A. Cambridge, incorp. 30 July, 1560; studied 12 years in the Universities of St. Andrews, Aberdeen, and Glasgow. See Ath. Ox. i. 718; Cooper, i. 208; & D.N.B.
Eldridge, Joseph, fellow of New Coll.; B.C.L. 18 April, 1634; died 5 Nov., 1645, buried in the College chapel. See Gutch, i. 232.
Elithirst, Henry, B.A. 15 Dec., 1511.
Elkins, George, B.A. 17 April, 1559, "a priest with cure of souls."
Elkes, Richard, B.A. 20 June, 1570, rector of West Ilsley, Berks, 1576. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Elletson, George (Eletson), B.A. 5 April, 1543, M.A. (sup. Oct.) 1544, vicar of Bridekirk, Cumberland, 1554. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Elletson, Laurence, s. Robert, of Furness Fell, co. Lanc., pensioner of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 23 Feb., 1663-4, aged 17; B.A. 1667-8, M.A. 1671, incorp. 11 June, 1671, vicar of Winterton, co. Lincoln, 1671 (as Ellerton), and of Aukborough, Yorks, 1685-6. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & Mayor, 162.
Ellingworth, John, of Balliol Coll., B.A. 20 May, 1530.
EliottEdward, B.A. (sup. 10 June) 1516.

Eliot, Edward, B.A. from Exeter Coll. 31 Jan., 1605-6; one of these names rector of St. Edmund-onthe-Bridge, Exeter, 1611, vicar of Tavistock, Devon, 1612, rector of Newton Ferrers, Devon, 1632, and perhaps father of Joseph and Peter. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Eliot, Edward, B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, 1696-7, M.A. 1700, incorp. 13 July, 1703, vicar of Buttermere, Wilts, 1700-22, rector of Dunsfold, Surrey, 1722-39. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Eliott George (Elyott), B.A. (disp.) 1543-4.
Eliott, John (Eliott, Elyott or Elyottys), B.A. 21 June, 1529.

Eliot, John, of Catherine Hall., Cambridge, D.Med. per literas Regias 1681, incorp. 11 July, 1683, a fellow College Physicians 1687, an adherent of James II., impeached of high treason, but not tried. See Munk's Roll, i. 474; & D.N.B.
Eliott, Nicholas (Elyot), B.A. 25 Feb., 1529-30, M.A. 3 Feb., 1532-3.
Elliot, Robert (Elyatt), B.C.L. (sup. 23 May), 1528; one of these names vicar of Moreton Magna, Southampton, 1545, rector of Trent 1554. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Eliot, Roger, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 20 March, 1588-9, M.A. 19 Nov., 1591; one of these names canon of Lichfield 1600; perhaps father of Raphael 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. xii. 153.
Eliot, Thomas (Eliett or Eyllyott), of St. Mary Hall, B.A. June 1518; B.C.L. 1 Aug. 1523. See Lansdowne MS. 980, f. 53.
Ellis, Benjamin, B.A. Cambridge, incorp. 9 July, 1622.
Ellis, Christopher, (Elys); B.A. (disp. 28 Jan.) 1510-11.
Ellis, Edward, B.A. Cambridge 1565, incorp. as M.A. *15 July, 1572.
Ellis, George, s. Henry, of Haly, Oxon, pleb. "serv." 20 Jan., 1625-6, aged 30.
Ellis, George, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1674, M.A. 1680, incorp. 13 July, 1680, rector of Over Worton, Oxon, 1683. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ellis, John, B.C.L. from Jesus Coll., 5 April, 1595.
Ellis, John (Eilys), B.A. from Christ Church 29 March, 1599.
Ellis, John, of St. Alban Hall 1607. See Eles.
Ellis, John, B.A. from Christ Church 17 Nov., 1631.

Ellis, John, s. John, of Raveningham, Norfolk, gent. *Admitted to Caius Coll., Cambridge, 15 Feb., 1647-8, aged 14; incorp. as M.A. 9 July, 1661; one of these names vicar of Fritwell, Oxon, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Venn 226.
Ellis, Nicholas (Ellysh), secular chaplain, B.Can.L. (sup. Dec.) 1530, B.C.L. May, 1531, incorp. at Cambridge 1537.
Ellis, Richard, subscribed 2 Nov., 1621.
Ellis, Robert (Ellys), student residing in the town in July, 1562. Tutor, Adam Broke of Magdalen Coll. See O.H.S. i. 246.
Ellis, Robert (Ellys), of Devon, scholar of Wadham Coll., B.A. 25 Feb., 1616-17.
Ellis, William (Elys or Elise), B.A. Jan., 1528-9, B.Can.L. July, 1532, B.C.L. 8 July, 1532.

Ellis, William, B.Mus. 5 July, 1639, sometime organist of Eton College, and of St. John's College, Oxford; buried in the churchyard of St. Mary Magdalen, in the north suburb of Oxford, in 1679. See Ath. i. xxvin. & Fasti, i. 506.
Ellison, George (Elyson), B.A. 22 March, 1542-3, M.A. 1545, Master of University Coll. 1551-7.
Elliston, John, B.A. 3 March, 1575-6.
Elly, See also Ely.
Elmer, See also Aylmer, page 48.
Elmer, Richard, (Elmore or Aylmer), student Christ Church, 1572, B.A. 17 Dec., 1574. See O.H.S. xii. 44.
Elmes, John, B.A. 21 Jan., 1593-4, late New Coll.

Elrington, Edward, of Hart Hall, in and before 1568, possibly a student of Lincoln's Inn 1573, as of Middlesex; and brother of the next-named. See Harl. Society, xiii. 49; Foster's Inns of Court Reg.; & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Elrington, Thomas, of Hart Hall, in and before 1568; one of these names (perhaps his father) bar.at-law, of Lincoln's Inn, 1548. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Else, John, Dominican, B.D. (disp. 23 Oct.), 1528.
Elsing, see Elsyng.
Elsley, William, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1692, M.A. 1698, incorp. 13 July, 1703, rector of Ryther, Yorks, 1704, canon of York 1721. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Elson, William, gent. St. John's Coll., subs. 7 Nov., 1655 (s. William, of Barnham and Oving, Sussex); born 1637; a common councillor of Chichester, and M.P. 1695-8, 1700, until his death 23 Oct., 1705. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; & Hearne, i. 58.
Elston, John, of Magdalen Coll., B.A. 27 June, 1506, M.A. 18 June, 1511 (as Elton).
Elston, Thomas, B.A. 19 March, 1508-9.
Elsworthy, George (Ellisworth), B.A. (sup. 6 April), 1508, M.A. 1 Dec., 1512; has a cure in the country; rector of Exford, Somerset, 1522. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. i. 59.

Elton, Edward (alias Baker), secular chaplain. B.C.L. 21 July, 1522, B.Can.L. 18 Feb., 1527-8 (as "Edward Baker"), late of Ledbury, co. Hereford, alias of Hynxsey Hall, in the University of Oxford; scholar alias clerk 1514. See Letters and Papers For. and Dom. Henry VIII. i. 845; & O.H.S. i. 110.

Elton, John, alias Baker, LL.D., founded a fellowship at Brasenose by deed dated 20 May, 1529; (s. William of Ledbury), rector of Marlborough St. Peter, Wilts, 1510-13, vicar of Sherston 1511-15, canon of Salisbury 1512, warden of St. Katherine hospital, Ledbury, 1513, rector of Mildenhall, Wilts, 1514, canon of Hereford 1515, rector of Netherhall portion of Ledbury 1517, vicar of Cricklade, Wilts, 1526, rector of Childrey, Berks, 1529, vicar of Yetminster, Dorset, 1534, chancellor of Hereford cathedral 1539, also rector of Welford, Berks, and vicar of Bishops Norton, co. Lincoln; will dated 17 April, 1547, proved 8 Nov. following. See Mayo and Elton Families, 7-12.
Elwood, John (Elwode or Elwalde), B.A. 23 May, 1533; one of these names vicar of Burnall, co. Cambridge, 1554. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ely, See also Elly.
Elye, George, B.A. 25 June, 1566, M.A. 3 July, 1569, vicar of Tenterden, Kent, 1571. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ely, John (Eley), canon, B.A. 15 Dec., 1512.
Ely, John (Eley), B.A. (disp. July) 1539, vicar of Pucklechurch, co. Gloucester, 1541.
Ely, John, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 3 March, 1613-14.
Ely, Nathaniel (Eeley), M.A. Cambridge, incorporated 9 July, 1605.*. [Addenda: B.A. from St. John's 1593-4, M.A. 1597.]
Ely, Robert, B.A. from Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, *1586, incorporated as M.A. 10 July, 1593, vicar of Charing, Kent, 1595. See Foster's Index Eccl. [Addenda: M.A. 1590.]

Ely, William (or Elly), of Brasenose Coll., B.A. (sup. July), 1546, M.A. (sup.) 1548-9, B.D. (sup. 21 June), 1557, one of the clerks of the market 1552, second president of St. John's Coll. 1559-63, rector of Crick, Northants, 1560; died in prison at Hereford 1609, "having been seized upon for a seminary"; brother of Humphrey. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Fasti, i. 153; & D.N.B.
Emerson, Thomas, chaplain, M.A. (sup. 22 Jan.), 1505-6.

Emilie, Theophilus (Emlie), B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 21 Oct., 1605 (s. Denis); baptised at Helmdon 30 Sept., 1583, vicar of Matherne, co. Glamorgan, 1608, vicar of Bromyard, co. Hereford, 1620. See Foster's Index Ecc.
Emot, Richard, of Brasenose Coll., scholar in music 20 years, sup. for B.Mus. 1619.

Emot, William (Emmot), of co. Lancaster; fellow of Brasenose Coll, 1556, B.A. 14 Oct., 1556, M.A. 15 Jan., 1559-60, sup. for B.D. 26 April, 1571, vicar of Eynsham 1569, until his death 10 Feb., 1584-5. See Hearne, iii. 84; Fasti, i. 386; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Empson, John, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 14 Feb., 1595-6, M.A. 5 July, 1599. See O.H.S. xii. 194.
Engest, Richard (Yngeste), B.A. 22 March, 1522-3, M.A. 1 July, 1527, B.D. 5 March, 1535-6, principal of Magdalen Hall 1537-41.

England, Charles, I, King of, signs 28 Aug., 1616, "Carolus P." with legend "si vis omnia subjicere subjice te rationi," crowned at Westminster 2 Feb., 1626, found guilty of high treason by a "High Court of Justice" appointed by parliament, and beheaded at Whitehall 30 Jan., 1649. See Fasti, i. 369; & Foster's Peerage.

England, Charles II, King of, M.A. Cambridge 21 March, 1641-2, incorporated at Oxford 1 Nov., 1642, crowned at Westminster 23 April, 1661, died s.p.l. 6 Feb., 1685. See Fasti, ii. 9; & Foster's Peerage.

England, George, I, King of, created D.C.L. 25 Feb., 1680-1, then Prince Elector of Hanover, Duke of Brunswick Lunenberg, etc.; "came to England to pay his respects to Anne, daughter of James, Duke of York" (though she became wife of George, Prince of Denmark, in 1683, and Queen of England 1702-14), crowned in Westminster Abbey 20 Oct., 1714, died 22 Nov., 1726. See Fasti, ii. 377; & Foster's Peerage.

England, James, II, King of, created M.A. 1 Nov., 1642, then Duke of York, and aged 9 years; crowned at Westminster 23 April, 1685, abdicated 23 Dec., 1688, and died at St. Germains 6 Sept. 1701. See Foster's Peerage; & Fasti, ii. 13.

England, William III, King of, created D.C.L. 20 Dec., 1670, then Prince of Orange, etc., proclaimed King 13 Feb., 1688-9, crowned in Westminster Abbey 11 April, 1689, died 8 March, 1702. See Wood's MS. in Mus. Ashm. 8566; Fasti, ii. 323.
England, John, of Magdalen Hall, 1607. See Ingland.
England, Richard, B.A. 2 Oct., 1540.
England, William (Englond or Inglonde), B.Can.L. 21 July, 1522.
English, James, "Scotto Brittannus, created M.A. 19 Aug., 1669, vicar of Long Bennington, co. Lincoln, 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.
English, John, fellow of Brasenose Coll., B.A. 16 Jan., 1569-70; one of these names beneficed in Lincolnshire. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ent, (Sir) George (son of Josias, of Belgium), born at Sandwich, Kent, 6 Nov., 1604, admitted to Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 8 July, 1624; B.A. 1627-8, M.A. 1631, D.Med. Padua 28 April, 1636, incorporated 7 Nov., 1638, fellow of College of Physicians 1639, president 1670-75, 1682, and 1684, knighted by Charles II. in the Harveian Museum 15 April, 1665, F.R.S. and of the first council; died 13 Oct., 1689, buried in the church of St. Lawrence Jewry. See Fasti, i. 504; Munk's Roll, i. 223; & D.N.B.
Erasmus, Desiderius, of St. Mary Hall; B. and D.D., Cambridge, 1506; died at Basle 12 July, 1536. See Ath. i. 97; & Lansdowne MS. 978, f. 220.
Erbury, See Earbury.
Erdall, John, B.A., from Magdalen Coll. 16 Nov., 1601, vicar of Thorpe St. Peter, co. Lincoln, 1611. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Erdeswicke, Sampson, s. Hugh, of Sandon, co. Stafford, gent. commoner of Brasenose Coll., 1553; author of a short view of Staffordshire, &c.; died 11 April, 1603. See Ath. i. 736; & D.N.B.
Erffeild, Antony, M.A. Cambridge, incorporated 11 July, 1598.
Erington, Richard (Eryngton or Herynton), B.A. (sup. 16 Nov.) 1510; one of these names vicar of Wookey, Somerset, and of South Stoke 1527. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Erington, Thomas, sup. for B.A. 5 May, 1515.
Erle, See also Earle.
Erle, Christopher, created D.C.L. 9 Sept., 1651, M.P. for Essex 1653; son of Christopher, last-named, born 1624.
Erpenius, Thomas, "restorer of the Arabic tongue", 1606. See Fasti, i. 319.
Ersden, John, at Corpus Christi Coll. 1548, from Kent, B.A. 12 March, 1553-4 (as Ersdall), fellow 1557, M.A. 30 June, 1558.

Erskine, (—) created M.A. 30 Aug., 1605; a Scottish Noble. Memo. John, Lord Erskine, son and heir of John Earl of Mar., was admitted to Gray's Inn 22 May, 1603. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg. & Fasti, i. 315.

Erskine, William, M.A. St. Andrews, incorporated Oct., 1632 (and at Cambridge in 1634); perhaps B.D. from Magdalen Coll., and created D.D. 20 Dec., 1642, sinecure rector of Cwm, Flints, 1644-53, prebendary of Llanefydd, in St. Asaph cathedral, 1644. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Thomas, 249.
Erthe, William (Earthe), B.A. from Balliol Coll., 29 Nov., 1574, M.A. 23 June, 1576, rector of Langtree, Devon, 1586, and vicar of Paignton 1583. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Essex, John, Benedictine, B.D. 9 May, 1515, "lord abbot of St. Austin's at Canterbury." See Dugdale's Monasticon; & Fasti, i. 43.
Essex, Thomas (Eseckys), Benedictine, B.D. (sup. 27 May), 1522, perhaps prebendary of Westminster 1540.

Essex, William, of Berks, arm. Christ Church, 13 July, 1587, aged 12; perhaps Sir William Essex, of Bewcot, Berks, bart., so created 25 Nov., 1612, taken prisoner at battle of Edgehill; one of these names M.P. for Arundel 1597-98, Stafford 1601. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Estcourte, Edmond, of co. Gloucester, gent. Magdalen Coll., 29 April, 1586, aged 13; a student of Gray's Inn, M.A. 1587, as of Tetbury, co. Gloucester. See A. T. Lee's Tetbury, 196, 208; Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Estcourt, Sir Thomas, Kt, created D.C.L. 6 Aug., 1677, of Cherston Pinkney, Wilts, one of the masters in chancery 1652 and 1660-83, a bencher of Lincoln's Inn, knighted 2 March, 1660-1 (son of Edmund Escourt, of Newton, Wilts); buried under Lincoln's Inn chapel (Le Neve); father of the lastnamed.
Esterfield, John (Esterfyld), M.A.; B. and D.D. (sup, 3 June), 1511, rector of Bradwell-by-the-Sea, Essex. 1504. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Estmonds, Thomas, gent. Exeter Coll., subs. 15 June, 1657.
Eston, See also Easton.
Eston, Antony (or Etton), Dominican, B.D. 6 July, 1509, "had studied at Ferrara, Louvain and Oxford." See Fasti, i. 28; & O.H.S. i. 65.

Eston, John, s. Nicholas, of Beeston, Norfolk, M.A. *and schoolmaster, admitted to Caius Coll., Cambridge, 24 June, 1613, aged 15; B.A. 1617, M.A. incorporated 10 July, 1627, rector of West Raynham, St. Margaret's, Norfolk, 1638. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Eston, John, of Magdalen Hall. See Easton.

Eston, John, B.A., from Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1644, M.A. 17 March, 1647-8, incorporated 14 Oct., 1648, fellow of Oriel Coll. this year by the visitors, delegate of the visitors 1649, perhaps rector of Pertenhall, Beds, 1660, until his death, buried 2 Jan., 1689-90. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Burrows, 537.
Eston, Nicolas, B.A. (sup. Nov.) 1530.
Eston, Richard, secular chaplain, B.A. 22 March, 1522-3, M.A. 26 June, 1526, rector of Ideford, Devon, 1545, vicar of St. Gerrans, Cornwall, 1548. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Estwicke, John, B.A. from Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1655, as Eastwicke; incorporated 22 Feb., 1658-9, M.A. from University Coll. 18 March following.
Estwick, William, B.A. from All Souls' Coll., 9 Dec., 1647.
Etheridge, William, M.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, incorporated 11 July, 1676; vicar of Eaton, Beds, 1677, and of Chesham Bois, Bucks, 1677. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Etkins, James (or Atkins), rector of Winifrith, Dorset, bishop of Moray in Scotland 1677, and of Galloway 1680, until his death 28 Oct., 1687. See Ath. iv. 870; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Etton, Anthony (or Eaton), 1510. See Eston.

Etton, Guy (Eton), minorite; B.D. 29 Jan., 1534-5, archdeacon of Gloucester until the accession of Queen Mary (when he became exiled at Strasburgh), and again 1559-71, rector of Horton, co. Gloucester, 1562, vicar of St. Leonard's Shoreditch 1576, until his death, about 1577. See Lansdowne MS. 981, f. 167; Fasti, i. 98; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Ettricke, Walter, commoner of Trinity Coll., 1648, in which year he was expelled; (3s. William, of Barford, Dorset); collector of customs at Stockton and Sunderland, register of the Court of Admiralty 1661; purchased the estate of High Barnes, co. Durham; born 26 April, 1628; died at Bath 2 Aug., 1700. See Burrows, 551.
Etwall, Thomas (Ettewell), created B.A. from Magdalen Hall 15 April, 1648, vicar of Bishops Cannings, Wilts, 1652, rector of Ogbourne St. George, Wilts, 1658. See Burrows, 39; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Eure, See also Ewre.

Eure, Sir Sampson (Evers), serjeant-at-law, created D.C.L. 7 Feb., 1642-3; of Gatley Park, co. Hereford, bar.-at-law, of Gray's Inn, 1617, bencher 1638 (3rd son of Sir Francis), king's serjeant 1640, attorney-general in Wales, knighted 7 Aug., 1641, M.P. for Beaumaris 1621-2, Leominster 1640 (l.p.) till disabled 22 Jan., 1644, speaker of the parliament at Oxford, was a sufferer in the royal cause; died in 1659. See Foster's Judges and Barristers, & Fasti, ii. 45.

Eure, William, of co. York, arm. Queen's Coll., 25 May, 1593 aged 13; one of these names seated at Ingleby, Yorks (3s. William 2nd Lord Eure), M.P. for Scarborough 1601, knighted before 1612. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Eustace, John, secular chaplain. B.A. 15 Feb., 1519-20.
Eustace, Oliver (Ewstas), B.Can.L. 18 Feb., 1527-8; probate at Oxford 14 July, 1528, as Heustes.
Eustace, Robert, chaplain of Brasenose Coll., B.A. 4 July, 1513, M.A. 27 June, 1516.
Eustance, Wm, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 1713. See Estance.

Evans, David, fellow of Brasenose Coll., B.A. 11 June, 1570, in orders, perhaps vicar of Lampeter als. Llanbedr 1571, and of Llandyssil 1575, and rector of Kellan (all in co. Cardigan) 1578, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Evans, Edward, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 23 Feb., 1619-20.
Evans, Evan, of Christ Church, 1696. See Jevans.

Evans, George, B.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge *(incorporated as M.A. 11 July, 1654), D.D. 1665, vicar of New Windsor, and rector of St. Benet Fink, London, 1663, rector of Hitcham, Bucks, 1667, canon of Windsor 1660, until his death 2 March, 1701-2; perhaps father of George 1678. See Lansdowne MS. 987, f. 221; Foster's Index Eccl.; & D.N.B.
Evans, George, fellow of Jesus Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1678, M.A. 1683, incorporated 13 July, 1703; perhaps rector of St. Benetfink, London, 1693. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Evans, Griffin, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 6 June, 1607.
Evans, Griffith, subscribed 16 April, 1613; B.A. from Hart Hall 11 June, 1616.
Evans, Henry, scholar of Corpus Christi Coll., 1564, from Gloucester, fellow 1566, B.A. 15 Oct., 1568, M.A. 14 June, 1572, B.D. 8 July, 1581.
Evans, Henry, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 26 March, 1596, possibly a student of the Inner Temple 1597, as of Iwerne Minster, Dorset, gen. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Evans, Henry, subscribed 3 June, 1614; B.A. from Oriel Coll. 10 May, 1617, M.A. 7 May, 1620.
Evans, Henry, B.A. from Christ Church 8 Dec., 1633, M.A. from Hart Hall, 20 June, 1636; one of these names rector of Llanfachell, co. Anglesey, 1646. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Evan, Hugh ap, B.C.L. 26 June, 1507.

Evans, Hugh, of Brasenose Coll., B.A. (sup.) 1548-9, M.A. 15 July, 1553, prebendary of St. Paul's 1558, dean of St. Asaph 1560, sinecure rector of Cwm, Flintshire, 1566-74, vicar of Northop, Flintshire, 1571-7, rector of Cerrig-y-Druidion 1582-7, vicar of Henllan, co. Denbigh, 1582, until his death 17 Dec., 1587, aged 64. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Thomas, 242.
Evanse, Hugh (Evance), of Brasenose Coll., B.A. 20 Feb. 1570-1, M.A. 18 May, 1574, "minister word of God."
Evans, Humphrey (Evons), student residing in the town in July, 1562; tutor, Geo. Capinhurst of Magdalen Coll. See O.H.S. i. 220.
Evans, John, B.A.; M.A. 9 May, 1555.
Evans, John (Evons), student residing in the town in July, 1562. Tutor, — Gwynne, of New Coll.
Evans, John (Evons), fellow of Oriel Coll. in July, 1562. Tutor, Ds. John Jackman. See O.H.S. i. 242.
Evans, John, of Balliol Coll., sup. for B.A. 14 July, 1585, M.A. 5 July, 1594. See O.H.S. xii. 132.
Evans, John, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 8 July, 1595.
Evans, John, astrologer. See Ath. ii. 552.
Evans, John, B.A. from Hart Hall, 28 June, 1620.
Evans, John, B.A. from Exeter Coll. 31 Oct., 1629, M.A. 7 June, 1632, perhaps rector of West Ogwell, Devon, 1632. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Evans, John, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 19 April, 1681, M.A. 3 July, 1684, B. and D.D. 25 June, 1695, perhaps chaplain H.E.I.C.S. Bengal and Madras, rector of Llanaelhaiarn, co. Carnarvon, bishop of Bangor 1702-15, and of Meath 1716, until his death in Dublin 2 March, 1723-4. See Cotton's Fasti Ecc. Hib. iii. 121; Foster's Index Eccl.; & D.N.B.

Evans, Lewis, student of Christ Church, 1552, B.A. 1 Feb., 1553-4, M.A. 17 Dec., 1556, B.D. (sup. Feb.) 1561-2, rector of Waterstock, Oxon, 1561, and of Ducklington, Oxon, 1568, etc. See Ath. i. 411; D.N.B.; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Evans, Lewis, B.A. from Hart Hall 18 Feb., 1573-4.
Evans, Lewis, (apparently erased in subscription book), 17 Dec., 1619.
Evans, Mathew, B.C.L. 26 May, 1542.
Evans, Maurice (Evance), B.A. from Brasenose Coll., 1 Feb. 1576-7, M.A. 4 July, 1583, as "Martin." See O.H.S. xii. 64.
Evans, Nicolas, B.A. (sup. 26 June), 1521.
Evance, Richard, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 14 Feb., 1576-7, M.A. 27 June, 1579. See O.H.S. xii. 65.
Evans, Richard, B.A. from Oriel Coll. 19 March, 1618-19.
Evans, Ritharch, of Hart Hall, in and before 1568.
Evan, Robert (Enan), chaplain. B.A. (sup. 1 July), 1511.

Evan, Robert, B.A.; B.Can.L. 17 May, 1534; B.D. Cambridge, 1544, rector of Llantrissant, Anglesey, 1526-34, dean of Bangor 1534, and again 1557, rector of Llaneingan in Llyn 1534-5, vicar of Terrington St. John, Norfolk, 1540, and of Llanllechid, co. Carnarvon, 1555; first master of Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1542-6; son of Evan ap Riaulte, etc., died about March, 1570. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Cooper, i. 284; Fasti, i. 97.
Evans, Robert (Evons), student residing in the town in July, 1562; tutor, Davie Hwes, of White Hall. See O.H.S. i. 250.
Evans, Robert, of Queen's Coll., in and before 1564.
Evans, Robert, subscribed 10 April, 1635; possibly a student of the Inner Temple 1633, as son and heir of Edward, of Crickett, Salop. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Evans, Rowland, student residing in the town in July, 1562.
Evans, Thomas, B.C.L. (sup. 24 Jan.), 1520-1.
Evans, Thomas, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1580, incorporated 11 July, 1585.*
Evans, Thomas, of Jesus Coll., sup. for B.A. 15 April, 1586. See O.H.S. xii. 138.
Evans, Thomas, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 2 March, 1593-4.
Evans, Thomas, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 14 July, 1596.
Evans, Thomas, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 27 Feb., 1610-11.
Evans, Thomas, s. Th., of co. Glamorgan, pp. Jesus Coll., subscribed 8 April, 1693, aged 17; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 9 March, 1698-9, M.A. 1701.
Evans, William, B.C.L. 18 July, 1552; one William Jevan vicar of Granston, co. Pembroke, 1559, and of Llandeloy, co. Pembroke, 1560. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Evans, William, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 21 Feb., 1600-1.
Evarill, Edward, B.A. from Peter House, Cambridge, 1616, incorporated as M.A. 14 July, 1618.*. [Addenda: M.A. 1620.]
