Plough, John (Plouwgh), s. Christopher, of Nottingham; B.C.L. supd. 1543-4, vicar of Sarratt, Herts, 1544, rector of St. Peter, Nottingham, fled beyond sea on the accession of Q. Mary, vicar of East Ham, Essex, 1560, rector of Long Bredy, Dorset, 1560. See Ath. i. 301; & Lansdowne MS. 980, f. 265.

Plowden, Edmund (Plaudon), studied at Cambridge 3 years, and 4 at Oxford, supd. Michaelmas term 1552 for leave to practice surgery and medicine (s. Humphrey, of Plowden, Salop), triple Lent reader Middle Temple 1561 (called to be serjeantat-law 1558), the eminent law reporter; M.P. Wallingford Oct.-Dec., 1553, Reading Nov., 1554 —Jan., 1555, and Wootton Bassett Oct.-Dec., 1555; died 6 Feb., 1584, buried in the Temple church; father of Francis. See Ath. i. pp. lxxix, 503; Cooper, i. 501; Foster's Judges and Barristers; Foley's Records, iv. 537; Woolrych's Serjeants, i. 102; Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; & Lansdowne MS. 982, f. 63.
Plumleigh, George, of Exeter Coll., subscribed 19 May, 1637 (erased); probably identical with former entry.
Plumme, Francis, (1648). See Pumme.

Plumme, Robert, B.A. from Catherine Hall 1641-2, and M.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1645; incorporated 13 Dec., 1649 (? B.D. Cambridge 1662), fellow Magdalen Coll., Oxford, 1648, by the visitors', vice-president 1653. See Burrows, 518; & Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Plummer, Henry, B.A. 3 June, 1522.
Plumpton, John, student Christ Church 1568, from Westminster school, B.A. 26 Feb., 1571-2, M.A. 30 June, 1575; one of these names rector of Butterleigh, Devon, 1591. See Al. West. 47.

Plum(p)tre, Huntingdon, admitted St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 8 April, 1620; B.A. from Trinity Hall 1622-3, M.A. 1626; D.Med. from St. John's Coll. (Regiis Literis) 1631; incorporated 25 April, 1656 (s. Henry, of Nottingham); baptised 5 Feb., 1601; died in June, 1660. See Foster's Graduati Cantab. & Fasti, ii. 194.
Plumptree, Jeremiah (Plomtrie), of Beds, pleb. privilegiatus 10 Dec., 1585, aged 18.

Plum(p)tre, John, at Corpus Christi Coll. 1522, from Nottingham, B.A. 31 Jan., 1525-6; perhaps son of Henry, of Nottingham; born 5 Jan., 1504; died in July, 1552, buried in the north choir of St. Mary's. See O.H.S. i. 342.
Plunket, Patrick, 7th Baron of Dunsany (son of Christopher, baron). See Ath. i. 505.]
Plunkett, Richard, secular chaplain, B.C.L. 27 Nov., 1535.
Plunkett, Robert, admon. at Oxford 15 Feb., 1504.

Pocklington, Oliver, M.A. of Cambridge; created D.Med. (Oxford) 25 April, 1656, incorporated at Cambridge 1656 (s. John, D.D.); rector of Bringhorn, Hunts, 1663, until his death 9 May, 1681. See Fasti, ii. 197; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Pococke, Hugh, B.A. from Merton Coll. 19 July, 1655, student of Gray's Inn 1653, as of Woolley Farm, Berks. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Pocock, Laurence, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 4 Feb., 1607-8, M.A. 4 June, 1611, rector of Brightwaltham), Berks, 1626; father of the next, and of Robert 1643, and of Zachariah 1638. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pococke, Richard, pleb. Hart Hall, subscribed 7 Nov., 1655, B.C.L. from New Coll. 1664, rector of Colmer, Hants, 1660; father of Philip and Richard 1684. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pocock, Samuel, "stationarius," privilegiatus 25 Feb., 1660-1, subscribes "bookseller."
Pocock, Thomas, s. John, of Hammersmith, Middlesex, privilegiatus 18 Jan., 1683-4, aged 30; "servus Dni. Sherrard, LL.B. & soc. St. Johns."

Poindexter, John, M.A.; fellow Exeter Coll., 1635, until expelled 1648, lieut. Bailly of Jersey; possibly B.A. from Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1629-30, M.A. 1633; died 1691, aged 83. See Boase, 65; & Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Poingdestre, Thomas (Poyngdestre), created B.D. from Exeter Coll. 31 Aug., 1636.
Pointer, See also Poynter.
Pointer, Samuel, of Wadham Coll. 1665. See Poynter.
Polden, Robert, subscribed 14 Oct., 1597; B.A. from New Coll. 24 April, 1601, M.A. 17 Jan., 1604-5, B.D. July, 1612.
Pole, See also Poole and Powle, or Powell.
Pole, John (or Powle), B.A. 9 May, 1520, M.A. supd. 11 July, 1521; one John Pool, vicar of Hartley Wintney, Hants, 1545. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pole, Matthew (or Poole), s. Francis, of York, admitted to Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 2 July, 1645, B.A. 1648-9, M.A. 1652; incorporated 14 July, 1657, rector of St. Michael-le-Querne, London, 1648, until ejected for nonconformity 1662, retired to Amsterdam for the free exercise of his religion, and died there Oct., 1679. See Calamy, i. 167; & Fasti, ii. 205.
Pole, Stephen or Powle, See Powell.
Polhelme, Winand à, Lord in Rozenhall, councillor to the Prince of Hesse, and steward of the palace, diplomated M.A. 14 Oct., 1636. See Fasti, i. 495.
Polkyn, John (or Palkyn), B.A. sup. 11 Dec., 1527.
Pollard, James, B.A. 17 Feb., 1538-9, M.A. 12 March, 1541-2, rector of Marsh Baldon 1542, vicar of Steeple Barton 1551, and of Dunstew (all Oxon 1554. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pollarde, John (or Pollar), regular canon; B.Can.L. 17 Jan., 1506-7, B.D. disp. 19 March, 1511-12.

Pollard, John, B.A. 3 Feb., 1522-3, M.A. 27 June, 1526, proctor 1531-2, and 1535-6, vicar of Minety and archdeacon of Wiltshire 1539, until deprived 1544, prebendary of Brecon 1542-51, archdeacon of Cornwall 1543-5, rector of Portishead, Somerset, 1543-50, archdeacon of Barnstaple 1544, until deprived 1554, rector of Ermington mediety 1545-54, of Widdicombe (or Withycombe) 1549-60, and of Newton Ferrers (all Devon) 1553-6, rector of Wheatfield, Oxon, 1553-77, canon of Sarum 1556-9, archdeacon of Totnes 1558. See Fasti, i. 74; Lansdowne MS. 980, f. 153; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Pollard, Richard, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1579-80; M.A. 1583, incorporated 9 July, 1583; perhaps rector of Doddington, co. Lincoln, 1605-8. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pollet, Edmund, B.A. sup. 24 April, 1526.
Pollexfen, William (Polexfen), "ser." Hart Hall, subscribed 23 July, 1656.
Pomerell, William (Pomrell or Poumrell), B.A. from New Coll. 30 Oct., 1557. See Ath. ii. 120.
Pomery, George, subscribed 21 June, 1616; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 17 Feb., 1619-20.
Ponde, See also Pounde.
Ponde, William, Augustinian friar, B.D. supd. June, 1535.
Ponsonby, Thomas, B.A. from Queen's Coll. 6 June, 1681.
Pontanus, Felix, "supd. June, 1540, for leave to practice medicine." See O.H.S. i. 197.
Ponten, Antony, subscribed 9 Dec., 1614; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 25 Feb., 1616-17.
Pontesbury, Ralph (Ponsbury, or Pentesbury), B.A. 30 June, 1516, M.A. supd. 6 Nov., 1519; perhaps rector of Finmere, Oxon, 1542, as Ponsbury. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Poole, Edward (or Pole), chaplain, B. Gram., supd. April, 1515.
Poole, Edward, 1600-1. See Peele.
Poole, Hugh, M.A. from All Souls' Coll., disp. 7 March, 1505-6; perhaps rector of Chedzoy, Somerset, 1524. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Poole, Hugh, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1670-1, M.A. 1674; incorporated 12 July, 1681.
Poole, John (Pole), B.A. from St. Alban Hall 6 June, 1608.
Poole, Matthew, B.C.L. disp. — June, 1534-5.
Poole, Matthew, 1645. See Pole.
Poole, Nicolas (Paule or Powle), B.A. from Laurens Hall 12 Feb., 1508-9, M.A. — June, 1518.

Poole, Oliver (Pole or Polle), of Nevill's Inn; B.Can.L. 11 June, 1509, principal of Greek Hall, was "of noble race;" perhaps archdeacon of Lewes 1516, and rector of St. Mabyn, Cornwall, 1515, until his death 1534. See Fasti, i. 27; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Poole, Richard (Pole), B.A. from Gloucester Hall 20 April, 1638.
Poole, Robert, B.C.L. supd. 5 Feb., 1505-6.
Poole, Thomas (Pole), B.A. 5 Feb., 1534-5.
Poole, Thomas (Pole), B.A. 31 Jan., 1535-6.
Poole, Thomas, subscribed 11 Nov., 1614; B.A. from University Coll. 23 June, 1618, M.A. supd. 16 April, 1621; incorporated at Cambridge 1624.

Poole, William, subscribed 30 April, 1613; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 6 July, 1615, M.A. 16 May, 1618; one of these names rector of Duntsbourne Abbots 1620-34, of Elkstone 1625, and of North Cerney 1634, all co. Gloucester; see also following entries another rector of Turnerspuddle, Dorset, 1640. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pooley, Giles, scholar Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1668, B.A. 1670, fellow 1672, M.A. 1673; incorporated 1 March, 1680-1, D.D.; rector of Haselbury Bryan, Dorset, 1680, vicar of Weare 1682, and rector of Wrington, Somerset, 1692-1709; licenced (V.G.) to marry Sarah Carpender, of Watford, Herts, spinster; vicar of St. Leonard, Shoreditch, 1708-20. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Rawl. iii. 138, iv. 286.

Pooley, John, chaplain to James, duke of Ormond, and dean of Kilkenny, 1674; D.D. by diploma 11 July, 1682; a native of Ipswich, Suffolk, scholar of Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1667, fellow 1670, D.D. 1692; canon of Christ Church, Dublin, 1674, dean of Ossory 1675, bishop of Cloyne 1697, and of Raphoe 1702, until his death 16 Oct., 1712; buried in St. Michans. See Cotton's Fasti Ecc. Hib.
Poore, William, B.A. from Hart Hall 6 Feb., 1629-30.
Pope, Barnard, B.A. of Cambridge 1508, sup. 8 March, 1508-9, for incorporation. See O.H.S. i. 64.
Pope, John, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 14 June, 1642; possibly brother of Thomas 1639.
Pope, Josiah, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 11 June, 1570, M.A. 28 June, 1574, perhaps vicar of Monkton, Kent, 1596. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Pope, Maurice, B.A. supd. 17 Jan., 1530-1.
Pope, Robert, secular chaplain, B.Can.L. supd. Dec., 1533, B.C.L. supd. 10 March, 1535-6.
Pope, Thomas (Poppe), B.A. 9 Dec., 1512, vicar of Kilton, Somerset, 1519. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pope, (Sir) Thomas, founder of Trinity Coll. 1555; of Tittenhanger, Herts, and Thorpe Underwood (s. William, of Deddington, Oxon); born there 1508, knighted 1536; died in Clerkenwell 29 Jan., 1558-9, removed to Trinity Coll. chapel 1567.]
Pope, Thomas, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 9 Dec., 1572, M.A. 23 June, 1576; student in Gloucester Hall; will at Oxford 2 April, 1578.

Pope, Thomas, subscribed 1 July, 1614; perhaps the 3rd Earl of Downe; born 1598 (2s. William, 1st baron), knighted 3 Aug., 1625; died 11 Jan., 1667-8; brother of William 1614, and father of Thomas 1658.

Pope, William, subscribed 1 July, 1614; perhaps 1s. William, Earl of Downe; baptised 17 Oct., 1596, knighted 28 Aug., 1616, M.P. Oxon 1621-2; buried at Wroxton, Oxon, 29 Aug., 1624; brother of Thomas 1614, father of Thomas 1639.

Popham, Sir Francis, created M.A. from Christ Church 7 May, 1662; of Littlecote, Wilts (s. Alexander 1621), knight of the bath, at the coronation of Charles II. April, 1661, M.P. Bath Nov., 1669, until his death 28 Aug., 1674. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Popham, (Sir) John, 2s. Edward (or rather of Alexander), of Huntworth, Somerset; of Balliol Coll.; autumn reader Middle Temple 1568, treasurer 1580, serjeant-at-law 1579, recorder of Bristol, M.P. Lyme Regis 1558, Bristol 1571, 1572-83, speaker house of commons 1577-85, solicitor-general 1579-81, attorney-general 1581-92, chief justice of the king's bench, knighted 1592; died 10 June, 1607; buried in Wellington church, Somerset; father of Francis 1588. See Ath. ii. 20; Foss's Judges; & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Popham, Thomas, B.A. 27 Jan., 1521-2.
Popple, Nathaniel, fellow Sydney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1702-3, M.A. 1706 (incorporated 14 July, 1712), B.D. 1713.
Portbury, See also Potbury.
Porte, Richard, M.A. of Cambridge; incorporated 2 April, 1528, B.D. sup. 28 June, 1530, vicar of Avebury, Wilts, 1530, and of Ashton Keynes, Wilts, 1542. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Porter, George, (incorporated 16 July, 1600) fellow of "Queen's" Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1595-6, M.A. 1599, LL.D. 1611; regius professor of civil law. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Porter, Henry, B. Mus. from Christ Church, supd. 4 July, 1600; his son Walter became gentleman of the chapel royal of Chas. I., and master of the choristers at Westminster. See Fasti, i. 284.
Porter, John, secular chaplain, B.Can.L. — July, 1525.
Porter, John, B.A. 31 Dec., 1542. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Porter, Richard, of Hart Hall, in and before 1568; B.A. supd., 10 July, 1570.
Porter, Robert (Portar), B.A. supd. 17 March, 1505-6, M.A. 4 May, 1510.

Porter, Roger, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 20 June, 1576, M.A. (29) March, 1579-80, B.D. 14 April, 1600, licenced to preach 20 Oct., 1604, rector of Hartwell, Bucks, 1601; father of Roger 1631; see Richard 1584. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Porter, Thomas, B.A. June, 1518.

Porter, William, warden of New Coll. 1494; B.D. —, D.D. supd. 31 May, 1511; chauntor or precentor of Hereford, a benefactor to Brasenose Coll. 1531. See Lansdowne MS. 978, f. 175; Gutch, i. 357; & Fasti, i. 34.
Porter, William, B.A. from Queen's Coll. 18 Dec., 1579, M.A. 30 June, 1582; vicar of Appleby St. Michael, Westmorland, 1583. See O.H.S. xii. 85.
Portington, Thomas (or Portinton), B.A. 11 March, 1554-5; rector of Winterton, Norfolk, 1562, of Linwood 1576, and of Nettleton, co. Lincoln, 1580. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Portlock, Benjamin, fellow Trinity Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1685-6, M.A. 1689, incorporated 9 Nov., 1695, D.C.L. by diploma 18 May, 1702.
Portman, Leonard, B.A. 5 July, 1527, rector of Great Wishford, Wilts, 1531, vicar of East or Queen Camel, Somerset, 1541. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Porton, Isaac, of Christ Church 1567, B.A. 26 Jan., 1569-70.

Portrey, Alexander, arm. fil. n.m. B.A. from Christ Church 16 Dec., 1620; incorporated at Cambridge 1621; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1631, as son and heir of Reginald, of Fonmon, co. Glamorgan. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Portridge, John, of Magdalen Hall 1647. See Partridge.
Portugal, Peter, of. See Lusitania, page 950.

Pory, John (Porie), admitted of Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, about 1520 (from Thrapston, Northants), B.A. 1523-4, M.A. 1527, fellow and B.D. 1535, D.D. 1559 (sup. 11 Nov., 1566, for incorporation), master of his college 1557-69, vicechancellor 1558, fellow of the collegiate church of Stoke-by-Clare, Suffolk, rector of Bunwell, Norfolk, 1549-63, vicar of Norwich St. Stephen 1556, rector of Landbeach, co. Cambridge, 1558-70, canon of Ely 1560, rector of Pulham St. Mary, Norfolk, 1560, rector of Lambeth 1563, canon of Canterbury 1564-7, and of Westminster 1568-70; died in 1573, made his will May, 1570. See Lansdowne MS. 981, f. 72; Cooper, i. 320; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Porye, John, of Caius Coll., Cambridge, 1587, B.A. 1591-2, M.A. 1595; incorporated 18 April, 1610; traveller and geographer, secretary to the colony of Virginia 1619-21, etc.; died before Oct., 1635. See Venn, 64; Fasti, i. 175; and Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Pory, Robert, s. Robert; B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1628-9, M.A. 1632, B.D. 1639, D.D. per Literas Regias 2 Aug., 1660, incorporated 10 July, 1663, chaplain to Dr. Juxon, archbishop of Canterbury, archdeacon of Middlesex 1660, rector of St. Margarets, New Fish Street, London, 1640, and of Thorley, Herts, 1640, until ejected, restored 1660; licenced 21 Sept., 1640, to marry Elizabeth, daughter of Thomas Juxon, of Chichester; canon residentiary of St. Paul's 1660, and canon of Chichester 1660, rector of St. Botolph, Bishopsgate, 1660-2, of Hollingbourn, Kent, 1661, of Much Hadham, Herts, 1662, and of Lambeth 1662; died 22 Nov., 1668. See Lansdowne MS. 986, f. 122; Foster's Index Eccl.; London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; Fasti, ii. 267; & St. Paul's S. R. 34.
Poskins, John (Poskyns), B.A. 19 June, 1507, M.A. supd. 8 July, 1510.
Post, Leonard, subscribed 16 April, 1613.
Poston, John, created M.A. from Christ Church 17 Oct., 1643.
Potbury, George, 1661 and 1697. See Portbury.
Poterell, Thomas, secular chaplain, B.C.L. 2 July, 1526.
Potkin, Cuthbert (Potkyn), B.C.L. 26 Feb., 1528-9.

Potkin, Peter, B.C.L. —, B.Can.L. from New Inn Hall 8 Feb., 1506-7, D.C.L. 1 July, 1510, an advocate in the court of arches circa 1511, rector of St. Mary Bothawe 1506, rector of Monks Eleigh, Suffolk, 1514-16, rector of St. Leonard, Eastcheap, 1516, until his death 1 May, 1520. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Coote's Civilians, 11; & Fasti, i. 30.
Potkin, Thomas (Potkyn), B.C.L. 18 Feb., 1527-8, B.Can.L. 19 July, 1531.
Potman, Richard, chaplain, D.C.L. and B.Can.L. supd. 29 June, 1510.
Pott, See also Potts.
Pott, Caleb, B.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1673-4, M.A. 1678; incorporated 5 Feb., 1679-80.
Pott, James, M.A. of Cambridge; incorporated 8 July, 1614 (perhaps B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge), 1610-11, M.A. 1614, as John. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Potter, Charles, s. Christopher, provost of Queen's Coll. and D.D. Queen's Coll., admitted 4 July, 1645, aged 11; B.A. from Christ Church 27 June, 1649, M.A. 15 July, 1651, usher to Henrietta Maria, Queen Dowager of England; buried in St. Paul's, Covent Garden, Dec., 1663. See Ath. iii. 648; & Al. West. 122.

Potter, Christopher, s. Christoph., provost of Queen's Coll., and D.D. Queen's Coll., admitted 4 July, 1645, aged 10; perhaps B.A. from Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1657-8, M.A. 1661; vicar of Fenstanton, Hunts, 1668, until his death 26 April, 1678, aged 43; M.I. St. Peter-inthe-East, Oxford. See Hearne, i. 136; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Potter, Edward, B.A. from Trinity Coll. 8 Feb., 1593-4.

Potter, Francis, B.A. from Trinity Coll. 8 July, 1613, M.A. 26 June, 1616, B.D. 8 July, 1625 (s. Richard, vicar of Mere, Wilts, 1586-1630, etc.), rector of Kilmington, Somerset, 1626, until his death in April, 1678. See Ath. iii. 1155; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Potter, Robert, B.A. from Trinity Coll. 27 Feb., 1589-90.

Potter, Simon, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1655-6, M.A. 1659, incorporated 12 July, 1662, rector of Foulmire, co. Cambridge, 1662, and of Gedney, co. Lincoln, 1663. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Potter, Thomas, B.A. from Christ Church 1 July, 1590.
Potter, Tobias, subscribed 29 Oct., 1613; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 7 Nov., 1615, M.A. 9 July, 1618, B.D. 21 July, 1627, rector of Binfield, Berks, 1627. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Potter, William, B.A. 12 March, 1540-1; perhaps chantry priest Tetbury, co. Gloucester, 1543, rector of Upper Slaughter, co. Gloucester, 1549. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Potter, William, B.A. 14 Dec., 1568; one of these names vicar of East Tilbury, Essex, 1584. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Potter, William, s. William, of Oxford (city), privilegiatus 28 Nov., 1665, aged 35; subscribes "apothecary;" will at Oxford proved 11 May, 1677.
Pottenger, Thomas, pleb. Hart Hall, subscribed 15 June, 1657.
Pottle, John (Potle, or Potell), at Corpus Christi Coll. 1566, from Gloucester, fellow 1568, B.A. 15 Nov., 1570, M.A. 4 Oct., 1573, B.Med. sup. April, 1580.

Potts, Nicolas, B.A. from Christ Church 12 Dec., 1561, M.A. 17 Jan., 1564-5 (perhaps baptised at Chalgrave 20 April, 1546, s. Ralph); bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1577, bencher 1594, of Chalgrave, Beds, and M.P. Bedford 1584-5; buried at Chalgrave 1622; father of John 1601. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Pougsley, John, of New Inn Hall 1636. See Pugsley.
Poulet, Amias (Paulet), created M.A. 16 Jan., 1642-3, 3s. John, 1st Lord Paulet, of Hinton; brother of Francis and John 1632.

Powlet, John (Paulet, Poulett, or Pollett), B.A. 22 Nov., 1530, M.A. 6 June, 1532, proctor 1534; one of these names rector of Sampford Peverell, Devon, 1540-59, of Hinton St. George 1558-68, and of Seavington St. Michael, Somerset, 1558-68, perhaps dean of Jersey. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Fasti, i. 105.

Powlett, John (Pawlett), son and heir of William, Marquis of Winchester; sometime of Exeter Coll.; 5th marquis 1628; M.P. St. Ives 1621-2, governor and commander-in-chief of Basing House 1643—Oct., 1645, and colonel of a regiment of foot 1643; died 5 March, 1674-5, buried at Inglefield Green. See Ath. iii. 1005; & Foster's Peerage.]
Powlett, John (Pawlett), of Balliol Coll., gent., subscribes 15 June, 1657.
Powlet, Robert (Paulet or Pollett), B.A. 23 Feb., 1537-8.

Poulett, William, arm. fil. n. m.; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. supd. 25 June, 1608, see O.H.S. xii. 282; possibly bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1617, as of Cottells, Wilts. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Poultney, Gabriel, of Merton Coll. 1600. See Pulteney.
Poulton, See also Pulton.
Poulton, Edward, of Hart Hall in and before 1568.
Pound, William, Augustinian friar, B.D. supld. June, 1535.
Pound, William, B.A. from New Coll. 5 Feb., 1591-2, M.A. 3 July, 1595, vicar of Shrivenham, Berks, 1596; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pounsett, William (Pownsett), B.A. 8 July, 1521, M.A. 1525.
Povey, James, subscribed 5 July, 1587; B.A. from Trinity Coll. 8 June, 1591, M.A. 1 April, 1595.
Povey, Thomas, M.A. of Cambridge; incorporated 9 July, 1594.

Powell, Christopher, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1580-1, fellow and M.A. 1584 (incorporated 11 July, 1586), B.D. 1591, vicar of Rainham, Kent, 1595, until his death; buried there 10 Jan., 1609-10. See Cooper, ii. 525; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Powell, David, B.C.L. 1521, B.Can.L. 8 July, 1532; perhaps beneficed in diocese of St. Davids. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Powell, David, B.A. from Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 1559-60, M.A. 1563, prebendary of Llanvair (2nd portion), St. Asaph, 1566, vicar of Ruabon, co. Denbigh, 1570-2 (then M.A.), rector of Llanfylling, co. Montgomery, 1571-9, and of Llanuwchyllyn, co. Merioneth, 1571 (then M.A.), etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.]
Powell, Edmund, secular chaplain, B.Can.L. sup. Nov., 1534.

Powell, Edward, fellow Oriel Coll. 1495, D.D. 1506, rector of Bleadon, Somerset, 1501, canon of Lincoln 1503, and of Sarum 1508, rector of the collegiate church of St. Edmund, Salisbury, 1509, perhaps vicar of Melksham, Wilts, 1526-34, an advocate for Queen Catherine in her proceedings of divorce; for denying the king's supremacy, etc., he was hanged, drawn, and quartered in Smithfield 30 July, 1540. See Ath. i. 119; Lansdowne MS. 979, f. 191; & Foster's Index Eccl.]

Powell, Francis, subscribed 14 Oct., 1614; B.A. from Jesus Coll. 9 Feb., 1617-8; possibly student of Gray's Inn 1618, as son and heir of Lewis, of Pwllcrochan, co. Pembroke, esq. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Powell, Francis, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1701-2, M.A. 1705; incorporated 17 June, 1707; one of these names vicar of Rendham, Suffolk, 1711, and Colchester All Saints 1713. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Powell, Gabriel, (s. Dr. David, 1573), B.A. from Jesus Coll. 13 Feb., 1595-6, B.D. from St. Mary Hall, supd. 2 March, 1604-5, as having been sometime in universities over sea; baptised at Ruabon 13 Jan., 1575, sinecure rector of Llansantffraid-inMechan, co. Montgomery, 1601-7, rector of Chellesworth, Suffolk, 1606, canon of St. Paul's 1609-11, vicar of Northall, Middlesex, Oct., 1610, until his death in Dec., 1611; brother of Samuel 1592. See Ath. ii. 24; Thomas, 772; Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 182; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, Griffith, of Jesus Coll., B.C.L. supld. 12 July, 1593, and D.C.L. 23 July, 1599.
Powell, Henry, B.C.L. 15 Nov., 1565.
Powell, Hugh, principal of New Inn Hall 1557.]
Powell, Hugh, B.A. 19 April, 1559.
Powell, James, B.A. from Hart Hall in or before 1579; one of these names rector of Kilmington, Somerset, 1573. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, John (Pawle or Powle), B.A. 25 Feb., 1521-2; for one of these names see Thomas, St. Asaph, 357.
Powell, John (Poell), B.Can.L. 15 July, 1529.

Powell, John (Powelle or Powle), B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 8 July, 1532; M.A. 11 May, 1537, canon of Windsor temp. Ed. VI., prior of Merton, Surrey; died at Windsor 15 Aug., 1558, buried there. See Fasti, i. 105.
Powell, John, subscribed 18 June, 1613.
Powell, John, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 3 Feb., 1626-7.
Powell, John, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1651-2, M.A. 1655; incorporated 13 July, 1658. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Powell, Lewis, B.A. 16 Feb., 1557-8, M.A. 29 March, 1561; will at Oxford proved 20 Oct., 1565.
Powell, Maurice, B.C.L. supd. April, 1565; one of these names beneficed in Bangor. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, Merideth, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 17 May, 1598, vicar of Dedham, Essex, 1599, and rector of Brompton Ralph, Somerset, 1603. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, Morgan, subscribed 10 May, 1616; one of these names bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1626, as of Llanbadarn, co. Cardigan, gen. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Powell, Moses, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 3 Dec., 1582; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 8 July, 1586. See O.H.S. xii. 107.
Powell, Patrick, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 4 July, 1718.
Powell, Peter (or Piers), B.A. 9 Feb., 1574-5.
Powell, Reginald (Powll), B.A. supd. 3 July, 1514.
Powell, Rice, student Christ Church 1567, B.A. 26 Jan., 1569-70, M.A. 6 July, 1573, rector of Kilgerran 1580, and of Walwyns Castle, co. Pembroke, 1581. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, Richard, B.A. 5 April, 1568; one of these names vicar of Swindon, Wilts, 1579. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, Richard, of Brasenose Coll. 1615, B.A. 4 Feb., 1618-19; M.A. from Hart Hall 21 June, 1621. See O.H.S. xii. 371.

Powell, Richard, s. Morgan, of Cradley, co. Hereford, sacerd. Brasenose Coll. 20 Oct., 1626, aged 16; B.A. 10 July, 1628, M.A. 10 May, 1631, vicar of Morton Jeffray, co. Hereford, 1634, rector of St. Dubritius (? Whitchurch), co. Hereford, 1637; brother of Francis 1632. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, Richard, a retainer to Lord Mowbray; created M.A. 21 Feb., 1642-3.
Powell, Robert, B.A. supd. Dec., 1544; one of these names vicar of Boughrood, co. Radnor, 1547, and canon of St. Davids 1554.
Powell, Robert, B.A. from Christ Church 9 Dec., 1556; perhaps student of Inner Temple 1559, as of Sandford, Oxon. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Powell, Robert, subscribed 23 April, 1613; perhaps B.A. from University Coll. 3 Feb., 1618-19, M.A. 6 July, 1621; one of these names vicar of Cadoxton-juxta-Neath, co. Glamorgan, 1620. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Powell, Stephen (Powle), B.A. 23 March, 1568-9 (incorporated at Cambridge 1571, as Pole), M.A. 9 Dec., 1572; perhaps student of Middle Temple 1574, as 3s. Thomas, of Aldersbrocke, Essex, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Powell, Theophilus, B.A. from Christ Church 17 Feb., 1630-1, M.A. 7 Dec., 1633; perhaps rector of Langtree, Devon, 1662. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Powell, Thomas, ap (Apoyll); B.C.L. supl. 19 March, 1509-10.
Powell, Thomas, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 12 July, 1529; one of these names vicar of Manewden, Essex, 1529. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Powell, Thomas, studied 10 years, B.C.L. 18 July, 1552, D.C.L. 8 July, 1558; perhaps principal of New Inn Hall between 1559-61, an advocate of Doctors' Commons 1559, archdeacon of Worcester 1563-79, perhaps rector of Letterston, co. Pembroke, 1574 (LL.D.). See Lansdowne MS. 981, f. 182; Coote's Civilians, 44; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, Thomas, B.A. from New Inn Hall 10 Dec., 1584.
Powell, Thomas, B.A. from Christ Church 27 Feb., 1589-90, M.A. 10 July, 1601. See O.H.S. xii. 159.
Powell, Thomas, B.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1648-9; incorporated as M.A. 9 July, 1657. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Powell, Thomas, pleb. Magdalen Hall, subscribed 15 June, 1657; B.A. from New Inn Hall 1661.
Powell, Thomas, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1696-7, M.A. 1700; incorporated 9 July, 1700.

Powell, Vavasour, s. Richard Howell, an alekeeper of Knucklas, co. Radnor; perhaps of Jesus Coll., a Welsh nonconformist; died a prisoner in the Fleet 27 Oct., 1670-1, buried in Bunhill Fields. See Ath. iii. 911; Thomas Rees' Nonconformists in Wales, 111-123; & Calamy, iii. 517.]

Powell, Walter, M.A. from Magdalen Coll. 12 Oct., 1652, rector of Alvescott, Oxon, 1661, and perhaps canon of Lincoln 1679-89; father of the next, and of Samuel 1672. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Powell, William, B.A. 17 Feb., 1544-5. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Powell, William, B.A. from Merton Coll. 7 Feb., 1588-9.
Powell, William, "pharmacopola," privilegiatus 7 Oct., 1714; probably son of Martin, a painter in Oxford, and brother of another Martin, also an apothecary; admon. at Oxford 1 Feb., 1723.
Power, Edmund, B.A. from Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1596-7 (incorporated 12 July, 1597), M.A. 1600. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Power, John, B.A. — June, 1518.
Power, Richard (or Powre), B.Can.L. disp. 15 Nov., 1521.
Power, Walter, B.A. sup. July, 1542.
Power, William, B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1598, M.A. 1601; incorporated 14 July, 1602. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Pownoll, Ezechiel, of Kent, cler. fil. Privilegiatus 2 March, 1586-7, aged 18; B.A. from Christ Church 1 July, 1590, M.A. 7 March, 1601-2; licenced 18 June, 1635 (then a widower aged 66) to marry Jane, widow of John Dynham; rector of Durweston 1597, of Rampisham 1601, of Winterborne 1605, of Haselbury Bryan 1609, all Dorset, rector of Wraxall, Somerset, 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl. & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Pownoll, Israel, student Christ Church 1571, B.A. 17 Dec., 1574, M.A. 5 May, 1578, supd. 13 June, 1582, for licence to preach; rector of Sampford Peverell, Devon, 1584, of Frilsham, and of Yattendon, Berks, 1585, and of Albury, Oxon, 1607. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pownoll, Nathaniel, of Kent, cler. fil. Privilegiatus 2 March, 1586-7, aged 28.
Pownall, Richard, M.A. from Brasenose Coll., supd. 15 June, 1594, being then "promotus ad beneficium Litigiosum;" perhaps vicar of Wookey, Somerset, 1592. See Foster's Index Eccl. & O.H.S. xii. 186.
Pownoll, Robert, subscribed 18 June, 1613; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 19 May, 1617, M.A. 1 Feb., 1619-20 (as Pownall); perhaps vicar of Lydden, and of River, Kent, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pownoll, Samuel, subscribed 16 April, 1613; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 21 June, 1615 (as Pownall), vicar of Alkham, Kent, 1627. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Powys, Henry (Powse), B.A. from Balliol Coll. supd. 22 Jan., 1613-14. See O.H.S. xii. 333.
Powys, Thomas, of All Souls' Coll. 1581. See Powes.
Poynier, John, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 17 Dec., 1630.
Poynte, John (or Peynte), B.A. 1 July, 1528.
Poynter, See also Pointer.
Poynter, John, subscribed 15 Nov., 1616.
Poynter, Theophilus, "Chirurgeon," s. John, of Oxford (city), privilegiatus 29 Sept., 1666, aged 30; licenced to practice in surgery 11 Oct., 1666.
Poynter, William, (alias Kidley). See Kidley, alias Poynter 1625, page 848.

Poyntz, Edward, of co. Gloucester, militis fil. Magdalen Coll., admitted 25 Oct., 1588, aged 13; student of Gray's Inn 1593, as of Torkington, co. Gloucester, esq. (s. Sir Nicholas, by his 2nd wife, Mary Stanley); brother of Hugh and Robert 1591. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Poyntz, Hugh, of co. Gloucester, militis fil. Magdalen Hall, admitted 22 Oct., 1591, aged 12; brother of Edward 1588, and Robert 1591.
Poyntz, Humfrey, B.A. 24 Jan., 1512-13.
Poyntz, Robert (Pointz), fellow from New Coll. 1552-64, from Alderley, co. Gloucester, B.A. June, 1556, M.A. 27 May, 1560, settled at Lovaine in Brabant. See Ath. i. 356; & O.H.S. i. 342.
Poyn(t)z, Robert, of co. Gloucester, militis fil. Magdalen Hall; admitted 22 Oct., 1591, aged 10; brother of Edward 1588, and of Hugh 1591.
Poyntz, William (Pointz or Poynes), created M.A. 27 Sept., 1592.
Prater, John, B.A. supd. 9 May, 1528.

Prat, Benjamin, B.A. Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1689, M.A. 1692, fellow 1693, B.D. 1699, D.D. 1705 (incorporated 26 April, 1706), provost 1710; perhaps rector of Cherrington, co. Gloucester, 1699-1700. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pratt, David, B.A. of Cambridge 1533, and admitted to practice medicine there, admitted to practice medicine at Oxford 10 March, 1535-6. See O.H.S. i. 171.

Pratte, Henry, s. Henry, of Weldon, Northants, gent., scholar Caius Coll., Cambridge, 20 Oct., 1587, aged 17; B.A. 1588-9, M.A. 1592; incorporated 11 July, 1592. See Venn, 65; & Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Pratte, John, B.C.L.; B.Can.L. 26 Jan., 1506-7, D.Can.L. sup. 20 March, 1506-7, vicar of Milborne Port, Somerset, 1509, canon of St. Paul's 1509; died about 1513. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pratt, John, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 17 Dec., 1557, M.A. 26 July, 1560, archdeacon of St. Davids 1557, and prebendary of collegiate church of Brecon 1559, prebendary of Wells 1562-73, of Southwell 1563, deprived before 1599, reinstated, and continued until his death, prebendary of Lincoln 1581-1607, rector of Greens Norton, Northants, 1567-86, and of Belton St. Peter, co. Lincoln, 1575, and of Skirbeck, co. Lincoln, 1584, until he died 30 May, 1607. See Le Neve, iii. 451; Foster's Index Eccl.; & Baker's Northants, ii. 63.

Pratt, William, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1580-1, M.A. 1584 (incorporated 14 July, 1584); B.D. 1592; one of these names vicar of Gisburne, Yorks, 1588-99, rector of Gayton-le-Marsh, co. Lincoln, 1597-9, of Stevenage, Herts, 1599-1629. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Praulfe, Evan, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 12 May, 1597 (supd. as John).
Pregion, Philip, of Lincoln Coll. 1596. See Pridgeon.
Prescott, Henry, B.A.; priest of Gloucester Hall, rector of King's Stanley, co. Gloucester, 1560; admon. at Oxford 4 June, 1568. See Foster's Index Eccl.]
Prescott, Henry, admitted Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1675, LL.B. 1682; incorporated 6 July, 1687.
Prescotte, John, of St. Alban Hall, in or before 1572; one of these names rector of Oakford, Devon, 1566, another vicar of Rye, Sussex, 1591-1614. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Prestlond, Albon, B.A. sup. Sep., 1538.
Prestman, John, B.A. 20 June, 1570, M.A. 26 June, 1574, rector of Haversham 1572, and of Emberton, Bucks, 1574. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Preston, John, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 1 April, 1598; perhaps rector of All Hallows, Honey Lane, 1604. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Preston, John, admitted to King's Coll., Cambridge, 1604; B.A. from Queen's Coll. 1607-8, fellow 1609, M.A. 1611 (incorporated 12 July, 1608), B.D. 1620, D.D. 1623, and master of Emanuel Coll. 1622, canon of Lincoln 1610, preacher of Lincoln's Inn 1622; born at Heyford, Northants, died 20 July, 1628, buried at Fawsley, Northants. See Fasti, i. 333; & Lansdowne MS. 987, f. 1.
Preston, Robert, chaplain, B.A. sup. 12 March, 1505-6, M.A. 16 Feb., 1519-20; will at Oxford proved (? May), 1528.
Preston, Samuel, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 13 Feb., 1609-10; perhaps rector of Blisworth 1615-29, and vicar of Rode, Northants, 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Preston, Thomas, B.A. 24 Oct., 1507; perhaps rector of Dowlishwake, Somerset, 1517. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Preston, Thomas, scholar of King's Coll., Cambridge, 1553, from Eton, fellow 1556-81, B.A. 1557-8, M.A. 1561, proctor 1565 (supd. 6 Sept., 1566, for incorporation), LL.D., master of Trinity Hall, Cambridge, 1584-98, vice-chancellor 1589, "commensales" of Doctors' Commons 1586; died 1 June, 1598. See Fasti, i. 173; Coote's Civilians, 59; & Cooper, ii. 247.
Prestwich, John (Prestwych), M.A. supd. 30 April, 1507, chaplain.

Prestwich, John, fellow All Souls' Coll., dean of arts by the parliamentary visitors 1649; died 30 July, 1679, aged 72; buried in the outward chapel of his college. See Gutch, i. 304; Burrows, 474; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Pretherough, Hugh, B.A. from Oriel Coll. 4 Dec., 1596, vicar of Lampeter alias Llanbedr-pontStephen, co. Cardigan, 1605, prebendary of Clyro in collegiate church of Brecon 1608. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pretherch, William (Pritherch or Rytherch), B.C.L. 10 June, 1543.

Pretherch, William (Prythergh or Protherough), B.C.L. 9 Dec., 1567, D.C.L. 13 March, 1580-1, perhaps advocate Doctors' Commons 1601, or rector of Maidwell St. Mary 1579, and of Ashby Castle, Northants, 1586. See Coote's Civilians, 66; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Prez, James Le, D.D. 1685. See Leprez.
Priaulx, William, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1628-9, M.A. 1632; incorporated 8 Feb., 1633-4, rector of Ruspar, Sussex, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, (—), created M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 2 Dec., 1642.
Price, Anton, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 4 Feb., 1591-2, rector of Gladestry, co. Radnor, 1608. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Bartholomew, B.A. 28 Feb., 1575-6; perhaps B.C.L. from St. Edmund Hall 20 Feb., 1590-1, and student of Lincoln's Inn 1577, as of Hereford. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Price, Bartholomew, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 21 Feb., 1578-9, M.A. 15 March, 1582-3, rector of Holton, Oxon, 1584. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Charles (Pryse), B.A. Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1682, as Price; incorporated (Oriel Coll.) 17 Feb., 1682-3, M.A. 3 July, 1685.

Price, Daniel, fellow Trinity Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1671-2, M.A. 1675; incorporated 13 July, 1680 (related to bishop Jones, of St. Asaph), rector of Westmill, 1684, and of Aspenden, Herts, 1685, sinecure rector of Llansantfraidd-yn-Mechan, co. Montgomery, 1697-1702, dean of St. Asaph 1696, until his death 7 Nov., 1706; buried in his cathedral. See Thomas, 243; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Price, David, secular chaplain; B.Can.L. 28 Nov., 1532, B.C.L. supd. 1549; perhaps rector of Penderyn 1542, and of Llansantffraid, co. Brecon, 1549. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, David (or Davithe Preise), B.A. supd. 14 Oct., 1560; one of these names vicar of Andover, Hants, 1560, and of Compton Dundon, Somerset. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, David ap-, B.A. sup. April, 1565; one of these names rector of Bettws-Bledrws, co. Cardigan, 1572. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Price, David, B.A. from Broadgates Hall 11 Feb., 1574-5, M.A. 25 June, 1577; one of these names vicar of Barrow-on-Soar, co. Leicester, 1584, another rector of St. Martin alias Carfax, Oxford, 1606. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, David (Pryse), s. D., of Reese, co. Carmarthen, p.p., subscribed 29 June, 1704, aged 17.

Price, Edmund, s. Edw., of Llanbedr, co. Merioneth, paup. Jesus Coll., 7 April, 1682, aged 20, B.A. 1685; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 1688; vicar of Clynnog-vawr, co. Carnarvon, 1692. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Edward, subscribed Sept., 1583.
Price, Edward, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 10 May, 1638.
Price, Ellis, "Student, residing in the town in July, 1562." Tutor, (Oliver?) Wytherton, of Brasenose Coll. See O.H.S. i. 227.
Price, Ellis, B.A. from Christ Church 22 June, 1585.
Price, Geffry, B.A. supd. May, 1572. See O.H.S. xii. 18.
Price, Geoffrey, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 28 Feb., 1583-4; perhaps canon of Llandaff 1588, and rector of Coytiffe, co. Glamorgan, 1600. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Price, Hugh, ap (Appryce), B.C.L. supd. 4 July, 1512, B.Can.L. supd. 23 Feb., 1523-4, as Pryce, D.Can.L. 2 July, 1526, founder of Jesus Coll., canon of Rochester 1541, treasurer of the cathedral church of St. David 1541; made his will 8 Aug., 1574, proved 31st. See Fasti, i. 70.
Price, Hugh, B.A. 16 Feb., 1580-1; one of these names rector of Dolgelly, co. Merioneth, 1584. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Hugh, B.A. from Oriel Coll. 4 Feb., 1596-7; M.A. from St. Edmund Hall 8 July, 1600. See O.H.S. xii. 201.
Price, Hugh, B.A. from Hart Hall 5 July, 1605.
Price, Hugh, cler. fil. Jesus Coll., subscribed 10 March, 1656-7, B.A. 16 March, 1659-60; one of these names vicar of Llanllwchaiarn, co. Montgomery, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Jasper, B.A. from Hart Hall 4 July, 1583, M.A. 6 July, 1587.

Price, (Sir) John (or Pryce), B.C.L. from Broadgates Hall 12 July, 1534, B.Can.L. sup. June, 1535, knighted 2 March, 1546-7; perhaps chancellor diocese of St. Asaph 1559, and canon 1560 as LL.B.; M.P. co. Brecon 1547-52, Hereford Oct.-Dec., 1553, Ludlow April-May, 1554. See Ath. i. 216; Thomas, 239; Fasti, i. 97; & Lansdowne MS. 980, f. 126.

Price, John, B.A. 29 April, 1562, M.A. 5 July, 1566; incorporated at Cambridge 1576; one of these names 2nd master of Westminster school 1568; another rector of Tempsford, Beds, 1563, and rector of Lexden, Essex, 1567, etc. See Al. West. 11; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, John, B.A. from Gloucester Hall, supd. 13 Dec., 1591.
Price, John, B.A. from St. John's Coll. 16 Jan., 1594-5.
Price, John, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 21 Feb., 1595-6.

Price, John, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 10 July, 1601, M.A. 12 Dec., 1605 (s. William, of Geeler, co. Denbigh), rector of Farnborough, Berks, 1606; his will dated 9 July, 1646, proved 8 Feb., following; father of Bartholomew 1641, and of Richard 1631.
Price, John, B.A. from Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1602-3; incorporated 3 Dec., 1605, and allowed to supplicate for M.A. See O.H.S. x. 366.
Price, John, B.A. from Gloucester Hall 19 June, 1666; perhaps M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1676.

Price, Martin, B.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1582-3, M.A. 1586; (incorporated 10 July, 1593), B.D. 1593, rector of Balsham, co. Cambridge, 1591, of Little Hallingbury, Essex, 1594, and of Castle Camps, co. Cambridge, 1610. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Price, Nicholas (Pryce), B.A. 23 Oct., 1526, M.A. 11 March, 1528-9.
Price, Owen, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 6 July, 1596, query matric. from Jesus Coll. as "Owen John."
Price, Owen, subscribed 23 April, 1613; B.A. from Hart Hall 5 July, 1616, M.A. 30 April, 1619; one of these names vicar of Llanvihangel Crucorney, co. Monmouth, 1632. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Rice, B.A. from Hart Hall 9 May, 1633; one of these names vicar of Ysceifiog, Flints. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Richard, B.Can.L. 17 May, 1534; for one of these names see Thomas, St. Asaph, 1669.
Price, Richard (Prise), B.A. 30 July, 1555; possibly a student of Gray's Inn 1555. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Price, Richard, B.A. from New Coll., supd. 14 June, 1595, M.A. 24 May, 1599; one of these names rector of Baldock, Herts, 1599. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Richard, B.A. from Hart Hall 29 Jan., 1673-4, M.A. 30 June, 1677.
Price, Robert, B.A. 31 Jan., 1577-8, M.A. 1 July, 1580; perhaps vicar of Purton, Wilts, 1582, and rector of Ashley, Wilts, 1602. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Price, Robert, B.A. from Hart Hall 13 Dec., 1625.
Price, Robert, B.A. from Oriel Coll. 11 Oct., 1718.
Price, Rowland, B.A. 15 Oct., 1572.

Price, Theodore, s. Rees ap Tudor, of Bron-y-fael in Llanenddwn, co. Merioneth; chorister All Souls' Coll.; fellow Jesus Coll., B.A. 16 Feb., 1587-8, M.A. 9 June, 1591, principal of Hart Hall, B. and D.D. 5 July, 1614, vicar of Llanrhaiadr-in-Kemmerch, co. Denbigh, 1581, rector of Llanfair, near Harlech, 1581, canon of Winchester 1596, rector of Llanrhaiadr-in-Mochant, co. Denbigh, 1601, rector of Launton, 1609, and of Islip, Oxon, 1626, canon of Lincoln 1621, and of Westminster 1623, until his death, 15 Dec., 1631, buried 21st in Westminster abbey. See Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 158; West. Abbey Reg. 130; Thomas, 432 and 523; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Price, Theodore, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 4 Feb., 1633-4, M.A. 27 Oct., 1636, vicar of St. John Baptist and St. Mary, Cardiff, 1638, rector of Marcross 1639, and of St. Athan, co. Glamorgan, 1661. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Thomas (Preice or Pryse), B.C.L. 14 July, 1572; one of these names rector of Newtown, co. Montgomery, 1583, and of Llangar, co. Merioneth, 1592. See Thomas, 712; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, Thomas, B.A. from Christ Church 30 Oct., 1591.
Price, Thomas, subscribed 30 April, 1613, another 7 May, 1613, and a third Thomas subscribed 10 Feb., 1614-15.
Price, Thomas, M.A. of Cambridge, incorporated 11 July, 1620.
Price, Thomas, B.A. from Christ Church 9 Feb., 1631-2.
Price, William, B.A. 18 June, 1563; one of these names vicar of Penally, co. Pembroke, 1565. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Price, William, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 7 Feb., 1582-3.
Price, William, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 21 March, 1703-4.
Price, William, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 6 March, 1720-1, M.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1724.
Prichard, David (Aprycharde), secular chaplain, B.Can.L. 2 June, 1522, after studying both at Oxford and Cambridge.

Prichard, Griffin, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 10 July, 1594, as Richards; M.A. 22 June, 1597 (as Prichard, then in orders); (perhaps vicar of Llanbadarn Trefeglwys, co. Cardigan, 1598, as ap Richard), rector of Llandduias, 1601, and vicar of Llandrillo-yn-Rhos, co. Denbigh, 1613, rector of Llangelynin, co. Merioneth, 1613, canon of Bangor 1626, rector of Llangynhafel, co. Denbigh, 1627. See Thomas, 427, 551; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Prichard, Henry, B.A. supd. 12 May, 1579. See O.H.S. xii. 85.

Prichard, Humphrey, B.C.L. sup. 26 April, 1555, and in 1556; rector of Llanbeulan, in Anglesey, 1548-86 (ap John, clerk), ordained sub-deacon Christ Church, Oxon, 1554, and priest 1555. See Ath. ii. 62.
Prichard, Humphrey, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 21 Feb., 1622-3, vicar of Martletwy 1632, and rector of Yerbeston, co. Pembroke, 1633. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Prichard, John ap, studied 5 years, B.C.L. 29 May, 1560.
Prichardes, John, B.A. 19 Nov., 1562; perhaps student of Middle Temple 1565, as son and heir of Richard Thomas, of Neath, co. Glamorgan, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Prichard, John, B.A. from Oriel Coll. 5 May, 1604, M.A. 13 March, 1606-7; perhaps rector of Llandyfrydog, Anglesey, 1609. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Prichard, John, B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 13 Feb., 1606-7; perhaps M.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 29 April, 1611; perhaps rector of Troedyraur 1622, and vicar of Llansantffraid, co. Cardigan, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Prichard, Laurence, B.A. from Hart Hall, supd. Oct., 1573, M.A. 28 June, 1578, supd. as "Laurence Pricharde Pengwerne," of Hart Hall, 1579; of Queen's Coll., supd. for B.Med., 8 July, 1581, vicar of Tavistock, Devon, 1586. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Prichard, Morgan, B.A. from New Inn Hall 23 June, 1596. See Price.
Prichard, Philem, B.A. from Gloucester Hall 9 May, 1640.

Prichard, Rice, of Jesus Coll. 1597, aged 18; from Llanymodfri, co. Carmarthen; B.A. 26 June, 1602, vicar of Llandingat, co. Carmarthen, 1602, of Llanymodfri or Llandovery 1602, rector of Llanedy, co. Carmarthen, 1613, canon of Brecon 1614, chancellor of the diocese of St. David 1626, M.A. and rector of Llawhaden 1626, until his death Nov. 1664. See Ath. iii. 116; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Prichard, Richard, B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 8 July, 1608, M.A. 29 April, 1611; perhaps B.D. from Balliol Coll. 13 May, 1624, and B.Med. from St. Edmund Hall 17 Dec., 1631; perhaps vicar of Sellack, co. Hereford, 1624. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Prichard, Robert, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 6 Feb., 1589-90, M.A. 28 June, 1593; one of these names sinecure rector of Denbigh 1605, rector of Llangwyfan 1608, and of Llansannan (1st portion) 1614, and of Llandyrnog (all co. Denbigh), 1616. See Thomas, 369; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Prichard, Robert, B.A. from All Souls' Coll., 16 Oct., 1607, M.A. 5 June, 1610; one of these names rector of Llaneilian, co. Anglesey, 1612. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Prichard, Robert, B.A. from Hart Hall 7 Feb., 1620-1. See Thomas, 357.
Prichard, Thomas ap, B.C.L. 15 July, 1529.
Prichard, Thomas, B.A. 16 Oct., 1570; fellow Jesus Coll., M.A. 27 June, 1573, rector of Penrieth, co. Pembroke, 1574, vicar of Pembury, co. Carmarthen, 1585. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Prichard, Thomas, B.A. from New Inn Hall 11 May, 1586; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 9 July, 1591; one of these names rector of Wootton, Kent, 1590, and another vicar of Chepstow, co. Monmouth, 1604. See O.H.S. xii. 135.
Prichard, William (or ap Richarde), B.C.L. supd. 1548.
Pricharde, William, B.C.L. 22 Oct., 1561 (after 7 years' study), D.C.L. 13 March, 1581; see also Pretherch, page 1201.
Prichett, See also Pritchett.
Pricket, Edward, B.A. sup. 17 Oct., 1567; one of these names admon. at Oxford 25 June, 1622.
Prickett, Richard, subscribed 22 June, 1610.

Prideaux, Denyse, subscribed 2 Dec., 1614; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 28 Feb., 1616-17, fellow 1618-30, M.A. 26 June, 1619, proctor 1626 (s. Sir Thomas, of Nutwell), rector of Lympstone-on-theExe 1630, canon of Exeter 1634, rector of Bishops Morchard 1639; buried 12 Nov., 1640. See Boase, 60; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Prideaux, (Sir) Edmund, B.A. from Sydney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1625; incorporated as M.A. 6 July, 1625; of Ford Abbey, bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1623, and a bencher 1642 (as son of Edmund, also a bencher, afterwards a bart.), recorder of Exeter, "a good chancery man," M.P. Lyme Regis April-May, 1640, 1640-53 (l.p.), 1654-5, 1656-8, 1659 (a Rumper), commissioner of great seal 1643-6, solicitor-general 1648, and attorney-general to Oliver 1649-59, and postmaster for all the inland letters, created a baronet by Cromwell 31 May, 1658; died 19 Aug., 1659; father of Edmund 1650. See Fasti, i. 424; Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Prideaux, Francis, B.A. from Exeter Coll. 19 June, 1609.
Prideaux, John (Predyaux), B.C.L. 8 Feb., 1506-7.

Prideaux, Robert (Prydis), B.Can.L. sup. June, 1532; perhaps vicar of Bickleigh-with-Sheepstor 1546, of Westleigh 1551, and rector of Newton St. Petrock (all Devon), 1569. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Prideaux, Robert (Prideocks), B.A. 3 July, 1577, M.A. 5 June, 1581. See O.H.S. xii. 69.
Prideaux, Roger (Prideris), B.A. 22 May, 1579; one of these names student of Inner Temple 1584, as of Torrington, Devon. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Prideaux, Thomas, subscribed 2 Dec., 1614 (as Predyaux); possibly student of Inner Temple 1616, as of Nutwell, Devon, esq. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Pridgeon, See also Pregion.
Priest, Thomas, of St. Giles parish, a poor scholar of Magdalen Coll.; will at Oxford proved 24 May, 1633.]
Priestman, John, B.A. 1570. See Prestman.
Prime, John, See Pryme, of New Coll., 1572.

Primrose, David, M.A. of Bordeaux University; incorporated 27 Jan., 1623-4, B.D. from Exeter Coll. 24 April, 1624, incorporated at Cambridge 1624 (2s. Gilbert), born in France, minister of the Protestant Church at Rouen in Normandy (1642); brother of James. See Ath. iii. 54.

Primrose, Gilbert, M.A. St. Andrew's University; incorporated 18 Jan., 1624-5, created D.D. same day (D.Med. 1628) — (s. Gilbert, principal surgeon to James VI. of Scotland and to Queen Anne), one of the French preachers of the Protestant Church in Bordeaux, and of the French Church in London, chaplain to James VI. and Charles I., canon of Windsor 1628, until his death Oct., or Nov., 1642; father of James next named, and of David last named. See Fasti, i. 419.

Primrose, James, M.A. Bourdeaux University, and D.Med. Montpelier University; incorporated — March, 1628-9 (s. Gilbert), born in St. Jean d'Angely in Xantoigne, in France, licentiate of college of physicians 1629, practiced at Hull in Yorks, buried 20 Dec., 1659, at Holy Trinity, Hull; brother of David. See Munk's Roll, i. 197; & Fasti, i. 449.
Prince, Hannibal, of St. John's Coll. in and before 1566.
Prince, John, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 6 July, 1601; perhaps rector of East alias Little Shefford, Berks, 1620. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Prince, Robert, s. Phil., of Marcham, Berks, privilegiatus 7 Feb., 1667-8, aged 35; "Hortulanus Gloucester Hall."
Printe, Richard, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 10 July, 1624; perhaps M.A. from Oriel Coll. 8 July, 1637, rector of Steepleton Iwerne, Dorset, 1668. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Prior, Christopher, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 4 July, 1633, M.A. 3 May, 1636, created B.D. 16 Jan., 1642-3, principal of New Inn Hall 1643, rector of Elvetham, Hants, 1640, canon of Salisbury 1641, and of Wells, 1643; died 1659; father of William 1666. See Fasti, ii. 54; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Prior, Thomas, scholar Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1701, B.A. 1703; incorporated as M.A. 14 July, 1712.
Prior, Thomas (Pryor), s. Tho., of Daventry, Northants, gent. New Coll., 16 Aug., 1712, aged 19; B.A. 1716, M.A. 14 Jan., 1719-20.
Pritchett, See also Prichett.
Prithergh, and Pritherch, See Prethergh, page 1201, and Prytherick, page 1217.
Probert, Philip, B.C.L. 2 Dec., 1529. See Propert.
Proctor, Francis, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 14 Feb., 1628-9.

Proctor, James, Cistercian, B.D. 9 March, 1536-7, lately a monk of the order of St. Bernard; archdeacon of Dorset, canon of King's college, Oxford, 1542, canon of Sarum 1561-80, nominated bishop of Ferns in Ireland 1579, but died before consecration, see Fasti, i. 103; perhaps rector of Islip, Oxon, 1541-50, of Thornton in Richmond, Yorks, 1543, and of Normanton-upon-Soar, Notts, 1544-5, vicar of Binbrooke St. Gabriel, co. Lincoln, 1546-8, rector of Ripton Abbotts, Hunts, 1549-60, rector of Winterbourne Cherborough, als. Gunner, 1562-79, and of Berwick-St.-Leonard 1565, vicar of Malmesbury St. Paul (all Wilts) 1565-6, rector of East Hendred, Berks, 1570-2, and of Bratton Fleming, Devon, 1571-4. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Proctor, Samuel, B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 20 June, 1578 (incorporated 15 July, 1578); M.A. 1582, B.D. 1590, canon of Gloucester 1586-1602, of Sarum 1589, vicar of Islington, Middlesex, 1590, rector of Shepperton, Middlesex, 1592. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Progulbicki, Johan, "a Pole;" created B.D. 9 Feb., 1648-9. See Fasti, ii. 114.
Propert, Philip, B.C.L. 2 Dec., 1529, rector of Lampeter Velfrey, co. Pembroke, 1546. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Prosser, Philip, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 20 May, 1634; M.A. 24 April, 1637.
Prothero, See Prytherick, page 1217.
Prothero, William, of All Souls' Coll. 1581. See Prethergh, page 1201.
Prowse, James (Prouse), B.A. from Gloucester Hall 14 March, 1643-4.
Prujean, John, privilegiatus 11 March, 1663-4, subs. mathematical instrument maker.

Prussia, Frederick William of, heir of the King of Prussia, Elector of Brandenburg; created D.C.L. 26 April, 1706; born at Berlin 4 Aug., 1688, succeeded his father 25 Feb., 1713-14; married at Berlin 28 Nov., 1706, Sophia Dorothy, daughter of George I., King of Great Britain.
Prust, John (Prest or Priste), B.A. 12 Nov., 1527, M.A. 19 June, 1531.

Prynne, John (or Pryn), B.C.L. supd. 18 Nov., 1506, chaplain, B.Can.L. 1 July, 1510, D.Can.L. 21 July, 1522, prior of Tunbridge 1518, see Fasti, i. 17; secular chaplain, canon residentiary of Lincoln 1528, treasurer 1532-5, subdean 1535, until his death 29 April, 1558; buried in his cathedral. See Lansdowne MS. 980, f. 273; & Fasti, i. 60.
Puccius, Francis, a Florentine, M.A. 18 May, 1574, secretary to Cardinal Pompeius Arragon; died about 1600. See Ath. i. 587.
Pudsey, George, subscribed 11 June, 1613; one of these names student of Gray's Inn 1616, as son John, of Langley, co. Warwick, esq.; possibly father of Henry and Robert. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Pudsey, Richard, B.A. from St. John's Coll. — March, 1579-80, M.A. 5 July, 1583.
Pugh, Richard (Pewe), B.A. from Jesus Coll. 12 Feb., 1585-6; one of these names beneficed in Wilts. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pugsley, See also Pougsley, page 1188.

Puleston, Hugh (Pylstone), B.C.L. 3 March, 1522-3, prebendary Llanfair (2nd portion) in St. Asaph 1517, vicar of Wrexham 1520-66, sinecure rector of Llanrhaiadr-yn-Mochnant, co. Denbigh, 1533-66, rector of Cwm 1547-60, and of Hope, Flints, 1557, rector of Corwen, co. Merioneth, 1547. See Thomas, 254; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Puleston, John, B.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1573-4, M.A. 1577; incorporated as M.A. 9 July, 1577. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Puleston, Richard, B.A. 16 Dec., 1573, M.A. 30 March, 1577; one of these names student of Inner Temple 1571, as of Alington, co. Denbigh, gent. (younger son of Roger, of Emral, Flints), rector of Astbury, Cheshire, 1577, of Llanegrad, Anglesey, 1593, of Kingsworthy, Hants, 1596, of Eastyn or Queenhope, Flints, 1597. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Puleston, Robert (Pyleston), chaplain, B.Can.L. 11 March, 1510-11.
Pulforde, George, of St. Alban Hall in or before 1572.
Pullein, Joshua, B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 21 June, 1561, M.A. supd. May, 1566.

Pulleyn, Nicolas (Pullayne or Pullan), B.A. March, 1553-4, M.A. 6 July, 1556; fellow Queen's Coll.; principal of St. Edmund Hall 1569-72, vicar of Shabbington, Bucks, 1564, rector of Palgrave, Suffolk, 1665, vicar of Buckland 1568, and of Burwardscott, Berks, 1573. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Pullen, Thomas, 4 years at Cambridge; B.A. from St. Alban Hall 20 May, 1587; M.A. from New Coll. 7 Feb., 1589-90, vicar of Pontefract, Yorks, 1597-1624; father of Francis. See Rawl. iv. 139; O.H.S. x. 369; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Pullein, William, B.A. (perhaps from University Coll.) 30 Jan., 1562-3, M.A. 27 May, 1566; one of these names rector of Mundesley, Norfolk, 1564, another rector of Ripley, Yorks, 1583. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Pullen, William, B.A. from Peter House, Cambridge, 1579-80, M.A. 1583; incorporated 9 July, 1583. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Puller, Thomas, B.A. from Trinity Coll. 15 Oct., 1650, M.A. 4 July, 1653. See Pooler, page 1180.

Puller, Timothy, B.A. from Jesus Coll., Cambridge, 1656-7, fellow, M.A. 1660 (incorporated 9 July, 1661), B.D. 1667, D.D. 1673, rector of Sacombe, Herts, 1671, and of St. Mary-le-Bow, London, 1679-93 (a student of Gray's Inn 1658, as son and heir of Isaac, of Hertford, esq.), licenced 23 Dec., 1676, to marry Alice Codrington, of Kingston, Surrey, spinster; died Nov., 1693; father of William 1704. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.; Fasti, ii. 250; & Chester's West. Abbey Reg. 14.

Pulteney, Gabriel (Poultney), B.A. from Merton Coll. 2 June, 1600; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 6 June, 1603, drowned 21 Aug., 1607, buried in Merton college chapel; perhaps father of Richard. See O.H.S. xii. 220.
Pulton, See also Poulton.
Pulton, Thomas, B.A. 8 July, 1521, M.A. 15 March, 1524-5.
Pumfrett, John, of Corpus Christi Coll. 1665. See Pomfret, page 1178.
Pumme, Francis, created M.A. from Magdalen Hall 14 April, 1648.
Punfold, Richard (or Pounfold), M.A. 15 June, 1514.
Punock, Robert, of St. Alban Hall, subs. 28 March, 1655; privilegiatus of St. Mary Hall in Mat. Reg., where he is called Pennock.
Punshon, John (or Puntion), B.A. from Queen's Coll., supd. 1548, fellow 1551, vicar of Newboldon-Avon, co. Warwick, 1554. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Lee's Thame, 322.
Puntæus, John, an Italian; licenced to practice surgery throughout all England 16 Nov., 1649 "a famous physician," lived at Salisbury; father of Arthur. See Fasti, ii. 122.
Purcell, Robert, B.A. 16 Feb., 1559-60.
Purcell, Thomas, bishop of Waterford and Lismore in Ireland 1486, until his death 1517. See Ath. ii. 712.]

Purchas, Samuel, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1596-7, M.A. 1600; incorporated as B.D. 11 July, 1615; collector of Voyages; born at Thackstead, Essex, 1577, curate of Purleigh and vicar of Eastwood, Essex, 1604-14, and rector of St. Martin Ludgate 1614-26; licenced 2 Dec., 1601, to marry Jane, daughter of Vincent Lease, of Westhall, Suffolk, yeoman; died about 1628, aged 51. See Foster's Index Eccl.; London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 114; & Fasti, i. 363.
Purdew, Thomas, subscribed 18 June, 1613.
Purefoy, Antony (Purphray), B.C.L. 9 May, 1525; admon. at Oxford 9 March, 1527.

Purefoy, Arthur (Purifie), a pensioner of Christ's Coll., Cambridge, Nov., 1555, B.A. 1560-1, M.A. 1564 (supl. 15 July, 1567, for incorporation), proctor 1571; perhaps a commissary to the bishop of Norwich 1580-5. See Cooper, i. 363, 565.
Purefoy, Edward (Purefie), fellow of King's Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1615-16, M.A. 1619 (incorporated 11 July, 1620), B.D. 1620, rector of Batsford, co. Gloucester, 1625. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Purefoy, Francis (Purefie), B.A. supd. May, 1561; one of these names rector of Staunton Wyville, co. Leicester, 1560, rector of Hulcott 1564, and vicar of Aylesbury, Bucks, 1565, rector of St. Pancras, Soper Lane, London, 1567, of Rucking, Kent, 1580, and vicar of Horncastle, co. Lincoln, 1585. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Purefoy, Hugh (Purfy, Purefye or Puryfey), B.Can.L. and B.C.L. sup. July, 1534.
Purefey, James, gent. Brasenose Coll., subscribed 27 Nov., 1650, B.A. 29 Nov., 1650. See Burrows, 483.

Purefoy, Knightley, fellow King's Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1654-5, M.A. 1659; incorporated 12 July, 1664, of Shalleston, Bucks, student of Gray's Inn 1656 (as 3s. George, of Wadley, Berks, esq.); died 13 Jan., 1691. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Purefoy, Michael (Purefrey), B.A. from Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1581-2, M.A. 1585; incorporated 11 July, 1598; perhaps M.P. Clitheroe 1584-5, and Nottingham 1621-2. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Purland, Robert, s. Jeremiah, of Warham, Norfolk, gent; scholar Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge, 1 Feb., 1664-5, aged 16; B.A. 1668-9, M.A. 1672; incorporated 12 July, 1681, vicar of East Walton 1671, rector of South Acre 1695, and of East Walton, all Norfolk, 1696. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Venn, 261.

Pursglove, Robert (alias Sylvester), born at Tideswell, co. Derby; educated at St. Paul's school and St. Mary Overies, studied at Corpus Christi Coll. 14 years, prior of Guisborough, Yorks, until its dissolution 1540, provost of Rotheram college, York, archdeacon of Nottingham 1553, deprived 1554, suffragan bishop of Hull, founded a grammar school at Tideswell and Guisborough; died 2 May, 1579. See Ath. ii. 820; & St. Paul's S. R. 19.]
Purshull, Thomas, of Balliol Coll. 1662. See Persall, page 1150.
Purvis, Thomas, student Christ Church in and before 1564-5. See O.H.S. i. 231.
Pury, Daniel, of Queen's Coll. 1585. See Perry, page 1149.

Pury, William, B.A. of Cambridge 1542, supd., May, 1545, for incorporation, M.A. Michaelmas term 1545, vicar of Stratfield Mortimer, Berks, 1547; perhaps prebendary of Carlisle 1547. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Pycroft, Samuel, s. Thomas, of Ellingham, Norfolk, gent.; scholar of Caius Coll., Cambridge, 13 Feb., 1644-5, aged 17; B.A. 1648-9, M.A. 1652 (incorporated 12 July, 1653), taxor 1654. See Foster's Graduati Cantab. & Venn, 216.
Pye, Robert, student residing in the town in July, 1562; tutor, — Hawle, of University Coll.

Pye, Thomas chaplain, Merton Coll., 1581, B.D. 21 June, 1585, D.D. 4 July, 1588; born at Darlaston, co. Stafford, rector of Newton Toney, Wilts, 1577-89, of Earnley-with-Almodington 1586, canon of Chichester 1587, vicar and schoolmaster of Bexhill, near Hastings, all Sussex, 1589; his will dated 20 Dec., 1609, proved 20 March following. See Ath. ii. 59; Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 169; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Pyke, See Pike.
Pyle, See also Pile.
Pym, Edward (Pim), B.A. from St. Mary Hall 13 Feb., 1609-10.

Pym, William, B.A. supd. April, 1570, and again 25 Nov., 1572, vicar of Croughton, Northants, 1578, until his death, buried there 26 Feb., 1630-1. See Baker's Northants, i. 601; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Pyncombe, See Pincombe, page 1166.
Pyne, John (Pine), subscribed 4 Nov., 1614.
Pyne, Richard, of Broadgates Hall 1596. See Pinne, page 1167.
Pynsent, See Pinsent, page 1167.
Pyrke, Robert, subscribed 1 Nov., 1594.
Pyrke, Thomas, s. Nat (haniel 4to.), of Dean Magna, co. Gloucester, gent. Trinity Coll., subscribed 23 May, 1704, aged 17; bar.-at-law, Middle Temple, 1711. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Quadring, Gabriel, B.A. 11 May, 1539.

Quadring, Gabriel, fellow Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1660-1, M.A. 1664 (incorporated 13 July, 1669); D.D. per Literas Regias 1691, master of his college 1690, rector of Dry Drayton, co. Cambridge, 1691. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Quamerforde, See also Comberford, page 314.
Quammerford, Edward (Quemmerford), secular chaplain; B.A. 5 July, 1535, a learned Irishman. See Fasti, i. 100.

Quarles, Edward, scholar of Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1618-19, fellow, M.A. 1622 (incorporated 13 July, 1624), B.D. 1629, admitted to Merchant Taylors' school 1608; born in Oct., 1599, vicar of Little Abingdon, co. Cambridge, 1630, and rector of Wyke Regis, co. Dorset, 1631, canon of Southwell 1634, and of York 1637, rector of South Stoke, co. Lincoln, 1638, and of Rushton All Saints, Northants, 1639; perhaps father of Solomon. See Robinson, i. 59; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Quarles, John, B.A. from Peter House, Cambridge, 1650-1, M.A. 1654; incorporated 13 July, 1658, rector of Sculthorpe, Norfolk, 1666, and of Ufford, Northants, 1666. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Quarme, John, of Ireland, arm.; privilegiatus 12 May, 1597, aged 22.
Quarre, John, B.A. from Merton Coll. 5 July, 1507, M.A. 21 June, 1511, archdeacon of Llandaff 1541. See Fasti, i. 33.

Queckfeldt, Benedict, created D.C.L. 12 Feb., 1652-3, a noble Swede of high birth and eminent for his learning, commended by Sir George Fleetwood, a baron of Sweden; brother of the next-named. See Fasti, ii. 191.
Queckfeldt, Gustavus, created D.C.L. 12 Feb., 1652-3. See Fasti, ii. 191.
Queen, Richard (Qwene or Quene), B.A. 14 Oct., 1511, M.A. June, 1518 (as Thomas), perhaps rector of South Stoke, co. Lincoln, 1550 (as Quyen). See Foster's Index Eccl.

Quelch, William, B.A. from Broadgates Hall 18 June, 1610, M.A. 8 July, 1613, B.D. from Christ Church 10 July, 1621, rector of Hackford-byResham, Norfolk, 1617, vicar of Carshalton 1620, and rector of East Horsley, Surrey, 1624, rector of St. Bennet, Gracechurch Street, London, 1637. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Querton, Edward, secular chaplain, scholar, and B.A. from Queen's Coll.; admon. at Oxford 18 July, 1502.]

Quicke, Philip, subscribed 23 April, 1613; B.A. from Wadham Coll. 11 Feb., 1617-18, dispensed towards M.A. 1621-2, then in orders, vicar of Quethiocke, Cornwall, 1617, and of Morthoe, Devon, 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Quinney, Richard, B.A. from St. John's Coll. 27 Oct., 1638.

Quinsey, Thomas, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1653-4, M.A. 1657; incorporated 9 May, 1661, vicar of Godalming, Surrey, 1661; licenced 10 Feb., 1675-6 (a widower), to marry Mrs. Gresham Kirk, of Godalming, Surrey, widow; vicar of Horsell, Surrey, 1679. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster, & Foster's Index Eccl.

Rabbet, Michael, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1575-6, M.A. 1579 (incorporated 14 July, 1584), B.D. 1586; of Gray's Inn 1597, rector of Cricket Malherby, Somerset, 1581, of Streatham, Surrey, 1585-1630, and of St. Vedast, Foster Lane, London, 1604-17; died 5 Feb., 1630, aged 78. See Al. West. 49; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Rabet, Thomas, B.A. from Benet (C.C.) Coll., Cambridge, 1630-1 (incorporated 10 Oct., 1632), M.A. 1634. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Rabon, Henry, B.A. supd. 13 July, 1569, and again 8 July, 1572.

Racster, John, fellow Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1591-2, from. Westminster school, B.A. 1588-9, M.A. 1592, incorporated 9 July, 1594. See Fasti, i. 268; Al. West. 58; Cooper, ii. 271; & Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Radbarte, William, B.A. 17 Feb., 1538-9, M.A. 7 March, 1541-2, vicar of Kingsbury Episcopi 1546, and of Somerton, Somerset, 1546. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Radborne, See Rodbourne.

Radcliffe, Anthony, of Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1 Oct., 1645; incorporated 16 March, 1648-9, student of Christ Church by the visitors, B.A. 23 May, 1649, M.A. 6 May, 1652, canon 11 Feb., 1680-1, B. and D.D. 8 July, 1681, chaplain to Henry, Earl of Arlington, vicar of Leigh, Kent, 1661; died June, 1703, will at Oxford proved 3 Nov., 1706. See Lansdowne MS. 987, f. 280; Hearne, i. 5; Burrows, 493; Fasti, ii. 381; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Radcliff, Christopher (Ratcliff), B.A. 2 Oct., 1540, M.A. supd. 14 Dec., 1543; possibly father of the last-named.

Radcliffe, (Sir) Edward (Ratcliffe), B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1573-4, M.A. from Clare Hall 1577; incorporated D.Med. at Cambridge, 1585, and at Oxford 11 July, 1600; lived at Orwell, co. Cambridge, 1595, physician to K. James, (s. Ralph, whom see), described in Berry's Kent as of Senington; knighted 22 Jan., 1604-5; buried 27 Sept., 1631, or 1632, aged 78; brother of Jeremiah. See Fasti, i. 287; and Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Radclyff, Francis (Ratclyff), created M.A. 28 Sept., 1663; perhaps the Earl of Derwentwater, so created 7 March, 1688, and died 1696.

Radcliff, Jeremiah (Ratcliff), pensioner of Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 2 May, 1567, fellow, B.A. 1570-1, M.A. 1574, B.D. 1581, D.D. 1588, vice-master of his college 1597; incorporated 10 July, 1600, vicar of Shudy Camps 1579, and of Trumpington, co. Cambridge, 1580, of Eaton Bray, Beds, 1584-7, of Heversham, Westmorland, 1588-91, and of Orwell, co. Cambridge, 1590, until he died in 1612; brother of Edward 1595. See Fasti, i. 286; Al. West. 46; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Radclyffe, John (or Ratcliffe), B.A. of Cambridge; supd. Dec., 1538, for incorporation, M.A. 10 June, 1540, B.D. supd. June, 1545; perhaps vicar of Crosthwaite, Cumberland, 1547, etc. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Radcliff, John, fellow Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; B.A. 1657-8, M.A. 1661 (incorporated 12 July, 1664), B.D. 1668, and re-incorporated 13 July, 1669, vicar of Milton, co. Cambridge, 1664-8; another vicar of Topcliffe, Yorks, 1681-7. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Radcliff, Percivall (Ratcliff), B.A. from New Inn Hall 4 July, 1626; perhaps served the cure of St. Bees, Cumberland, 1647; another was vicar of Boughton-under-Blean, Kent, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, p. 73.
Radcliffe, Ralph, of Hitchin, Herts, schoolmaster at Hitchin 1538, died 1559, aged 40 (father of Edward 1585), for list of his tragedies and comedies see Ath. i. 215; & Lansdowne MS. 979, f. 167.
Radclyffe, Thomas (Ratclyffe), B.A. dispd. Jan., 1505-6.
Radcliff, William (Ratcliff), B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 28 Jan., 1610-11, vicar of Powick, co. Worcester, 1615. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Radford, John, subscribed 4 Nov., 1614; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 22 April, 1618, M.A. 6 July, 1621, rector of Thelbridge 1632, vicar of Witheridge 1643, and rector of Puddington, all Devon, 1644. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Radford, William, of Christ Church 1639. See Ratford.
Raffe, (—), B.A. 27 May, 1540 (?subs. "Reaff").
Raffe, Gerard, M.A. —, fellow Exeter Coll. 1499, B.D. 30 Jan., 1506-7. See Boase, 27, 200.
Raffe, John, of Exeter Coll. 1663. See Ralphe.
Raffe, Thomas (Raufe), B.A. from Oriel Coll. 19 June, 1635, vicar of Bungay St. Trinity, Suffolk, 1660. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ragon, Francis, B.Med. supd. 23 Oct., 1520.

Rainbow, Edward, scholar of Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, July, 1623, migrated to Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 7 July, 1625, B.A. 1626-7; (incorporated 15 July, 1628), fellow, M.A. 1630, B.D. 1637, D.D. 1643, master of his college 1642-50 and 1660, vice-chancellor of Cambridge 1662, (s. Thomas, vicar of Blyton, co. Lincoln, 1601, etc.), born there 20 April, 1608, incumbent of Childerley, co. Cambridge, 1637, rector of Benefield, Northants, 1658-64, minister of Chesterford, Essex, chaplain to the king, dean of Peterborough 1660, and bishop of Carlisle 1664, until his death at Rose Castle 26 March, 1684, buried in Dalston church; brother of John next-named. See Ath. iv. 865; Foster's Index Eccl.; Fasti, i. 443; & Lansdowne MS. 987, f. 51.
Raine, John, 1521. See Rayne.
Raines, See also Raynes.
Raines, Nathaniel, B.A. from Peter House, Cambridge, 1617-18, M.A. 1621 (incorporated 10 July, 1621), B.D. 1628. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Rainsbie, George, of St. John's Coll. circa 1581, B.A. 21 Oct., 1583, fellow 1579, M.A. 1 June, 1587, B.D. 10 Dec., 1593; admitted to Merchant Taylors' school 1572 (s. John, Coyner), vicar of Kirtlington, Oxon, 1597, and of St. Giles, in the suburbs, Oxford, 1606. See Robinson, i. 20; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Rais, Edward, M.A. Cambridge; incorporated 14 July, 1607; possibly intended for Edmund Hase; B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1596-7, M.A. 1600. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Raisdell, Christopher, of St. Edmund Hall 1576. See Rosdell.

Raleigh, George (Rauleigh), B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 27 Oct., 1598; M.A. from St. Alban Hall 1 July, 1601, sup. for licence to practice medicine 16 March, 1604-5, B. and D.Med. from New Inn Hall 25 June, 1618. See O.H.S. xii. 210.

Raleigh, (Sir) Walter, of Oriel Coll. about 1568 (W. Rawley, 1572, in O.H.S.), a student of the Middle Temple 1574 (as son of Walter, of Budleigh, Devon), served in France, the Low Countries, and Ireland, discovered a new country which he called Virginia 1584, knighted 1 Jan., 1584-5, captain of Q. Elizabeth's guard, lord warden of the stannaries of Devon and Cornwall, governor of Jersey, took an active part against "the invincible Armada" 1588, M.P. Devon 1584-5, 1586-7, of St. Michael 1593, Dorset 1597-8, Cornwall 1601; in 1603 he was concerned in a plot against James I., condemned to death but reprieved, with imprisonment in the Tower for life; in 1618 he set out with an expedition to Guiana, and for sacking St. Thomas was on his return beheaded on the former judgment in Old Palace Yard, Westminster, 29 Oct., 1618; buried in the chancel of St. Margaret's, Westminster; father of Carew, and of Walter 1607. See Ath. ii. 235; Foster's Parl. Dict.; & Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 331.

Raleigh, William (Raylye), incorporated as B.A. of Cambridge 11 July, 1606; fellow Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, M.A. 1608, B.D. 1615, D.D. 1620, rector of Bowthorpe, Norfolk, 1612, and of Landbeach, co. Cambridge, 1617, domestic chaplain to Francis Bacon, viscount St. Albans, chaplain to Charles I., editor of Bacon's works; died 18 June, 1667, aged 79. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ramridge, John (or Ramrich), B.A. from Merton Coll. 5 April, 1527, fellow 1528, M.A. 15 July, 1530, B.D. sup. Jan., 1537-8, D.D. 16 July, 1542, rector of Garsington, Oxon, 1548, rector of St. Michael's, Coventry, canon of Lichfield 1546, and of Westminster 1553, dean of Lichfield 1554-8, and rector of Husbands Bosworth, co. Leicester, 1554, archdeacon of Derby 1558, deprived same year, murdered in Flanders. See O.H.S. iv. 255; Fasti, i. 76; Lansdowne MS. 980, ff. 276, 289b; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Ramsden, John, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 15 Jan., 1635-6.
Ramsden, Roger, B.A. from Christ Church 1 Dec., 1553.
Ramsey, George, of Scotland, M.A.; incorporated 30 June, 1612, and at Cambridge same year; rector of Wells, Norfolk, 1622. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ramsey, Sir James, a Scot; created M.A. 30 Aug., 1605.

Ramsey, John, regular canon; B.A. supd. 16 May, 1514, B.D. 26 June, 1522, D.D. supd. 23 June, 1522, prior of St. Mary's college, Oxford; one of these names rector of Woodchurch, Kent, 1545. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Fasti, i. 36, 60.

Ramsey, John, M.A. of St. Andrews University; incorporated 1 March, 1631-2, and at Cambridge in 1634, rector of West Raynham St. Mary, Norfolk, 1633, vicar of Barton, co. Cambridge, 1634, rector of Compton All Saints, Hants, 1636, and of Mersham, Kent, 1636. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ramsey, Richard (als. Hallynge), Benedictine; B.D. supd. July, 1539, vicar of Wellow, Somerset, 1545-54 (as Hylling alias Ramsey, S.T.P.), rector of Sherrington, co. Gloucester, 1545, canon of Gloucester 1548, deprived 1559. See Fasti, i. 110; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Rance, John, "Typotheta," s. Going, of Speehamland, Berks; privilegiatus 24 Dec., 1691, aged 28; will at Oxford proved 3 Feb., 1725.
Rance, Robert, B.A. 19 March, 1576-7, as Raunce.
Rande, James, B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1573-4, M.A. 1577; incorporated 9 July, 1577, vicar of Norton, co. Durham, 1579, canon of Durham 1606-20. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rand, Ralph, M.A. of St. Andrews University; incorporated 2 July, 1616, rector of Oxtead, Surrey, 1615. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Randall, Antony, B.A. from Christ Church 21 Jan., 1631-2; one of these names vicar of Zennor, Cornwall, 1669-83. See Lansdowne MS. 982, f. 5; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Randall, Edmund, B.A. 21 July, 1522, as Randolph.
Randall, John (Randalfe or Randolf), B.A. (probably from Exeter Coll.) 30 June, 1523, M.A. 9 Nov., 1526.
Randall, Leonard, at Corpus Christi Coll. 1544, from Kent, fellow 1547, B.A. supd. March, 1547-8, M.A. supd. 1551-2.
Randall, Lionel, fellow King's Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1586-7, M.A. 1590; incorporated 12 July, 1603. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Randall, Morgan, of Wadham Coll. 1666. See Randyll.
Randall, Peter (Rondell), B.A. 25 June, 1522.
Randall, Richard, secular chaplain, B.A. supd. June, 1536.
Randall, Robert, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 16 Jan., 1569-70, M.A. 10 Oct., 1573.

Randall, (Sir) Thomas (or Randolph), s. Avery Randolph, of Baddlesmere, Kent; student Christ Church, supl. for B.A. Oct., 1545, B.C.L. 1547-8, D.C.L. supd. 7 Oct., 1566, and 7 June, 1574, a public notary, principal of Broadgates Hall 1549-53, ambassador temp. Queen Eliz., chamberlain of the exchequer, comptroller-general of the post-horses; died at St. Peter Hill, near St. Paul's Wharf 8 June, 1590. See Ath. i. 563; & Fasti, i. 125.
Randall, Tobias, subscribed 16 April, 1613; B.A. from St. John's Coll. 15 Dec., 1614.

Randall, William (Randell), secular chaplain, B.A. 20 Jan., 1536-7, M.A. 11 June, 1539—? the William Randall, of Hart Hall, who was judge in Cat Street 1552, see O.H.S. i. 187; another of the name beneficed in Devonshire. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Randall, William, B.A. 28 Feb., 1575-6, M.A. 14 Dec., 1580. See O.H.S. xii. 57.
Randall, William, a student of law in London; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 8 July, 1602.
Randolph, John, of New Coll. 1534. See Randall, preceding page.
Randolph, Mathew, B.A. from St. John's Coll. 20 March, 1650-1. See Burrows, 548.
Randolff, Richard, chaplain, B.Can.L. 26 Jan., 1506-7.
Randolph, (Sir) Thomas, of Christ Church 1545. See Randall preceding page.

Randolph, Thomas, s. Robert, of Pulham, Norfolk, yeoman, scholar Caius Coll., Cambridge, 14 June, 1603, aged 15; B.A. 1606-7, M.A. 1610, incorporated 10 July, 1610; chaplain Wadham Coll. 1613-17, fellow 1613-21; perhaps vicar of Wilton, Norfolk, 1645. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Venn, 98; & Gardiner, 12.

Randolph, Thomas, "poet," s. William, of Hams, Sussex; born at Newnham near Daventry, Northants, 15 June, 1605; fellow Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1623, from Westminster school, B.A. 1627-8, M.A. 1631; incorporated at Oxford 1631, an eminent poet, "the friend and disciple of Ben Jonson;" buried at Blatherwicke 17 March, 1634-5; brother of Robert 1632. See Ath. i. 564; & Al. West. 93.

Rannew, Nathaniel, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1620-1; M.A. 1624; incorporated 10 July, 1627, vicar of Great Coggeshall, Essex, 1661, and perhaps of Madingley, co. Cambridge, 1628, as William. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Ransom, Richard (Ramson), B.A. supd. 20 June, 1517.

Ranson, Robert (or Rauson), M.A. of Cambridge; incorporated 23 April, 1589; possibly intended for Sir Robert Naunton, fellow Trinity Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1582-3, M.A. 1586; M.P. Helston 1604-11, Camelford 1614, Cambridge university 1621-2, 1624-5, 1625, Suffolk 1626, knighted 7 Sept., 1615, P.C. and secretary of state 1618-20, master of court of wards and liveries; died 27 March, 1635. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Rant, John, LL.B. from Caius Coll., Cambridge, 1652; fellow All Souls' Coll., by the visitors, sup. 12 Dec., 1651, for incorporation as B.C.L.; bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1652, refused to be bencher (s. Roger, of Swaffham Priors, co. Cambridge). See Venn, 212; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Raper, George, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 6 July, 1609.
Rashleigh, William (Rassheley or Raschley), Dominican and B.D., D.D. 17 July, 1525, "manster" of a house of his order. See O.H.S. i. 138.
Raspyse, John, "incept. 30 June, 1505." See O.H.S. i. 39.

Rastall, John, a Londoner born, "where he set up the trade of printing, being then esteemed a profession fit for any scholar or ingenious man;" married Elizabeth, sister of Sir Thomas More, lord chancellor of England; died in 1536; father of William named below. See Ath. i. 100; & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.]
Rastall, Richard, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 6 July, 1621.

Rastell, Thomas, late of Cambridge university; created B.A. from Christ Church 29 Feb., 1643-4; created M.A. 8 July, 1647, "in consideration of the several services he have done his majesty." See Fasti, ii. 105.

Rastall, William, s. John (1505), sent to Oxford 1525; bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1539, treasurer 1555, serjeant-at-law 1554, and a judge of the queen's bench 1558-63; died in Louvain 27 Aug., 1565; buried in the church of St. Peter. See Ath. i. 343; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Rastingham, Dennis, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 5 Nov., 1589.
Rath, Cormac (Ruth), B.Can.L. 5 Nov., 1511.
Rathbone, Thomas, of Trinity Coll. 1651. See Rawbon.
Raunce, Robert, B.A. 19 March, 1576-7.

Raven, Miles, fellow of King's Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1591, M.A. 1594 (incorporated 10 July, 1599), proctor 1605, B.D. 1607, rector of Monxton, Hants, 1612. See Foster's Graduati Cantab. & Harwood's Al. Eton. 195.
Raw, Thomas, B.A. supd. May, 1517, one of these names rector of Shepton Beauchamp, Somerset, 1532, another canon of Windsor 1555-7. See Foster's Index Eccl.
