Rawes, Thomas (Rawse or Rawsey), B.A. — June, 1518, M.A. 1 July, 1521.
Rawles, William, B.A. 10 May, 1631; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 17 Jan., 1633-4 (as Rawlins).
Rawlins, Andrew, B.A. 19 Dec., 1534.
Rawlins, Edmund (Raulyn), Benedictine, B.D. supd. 5 July, 1514.
Rawlins, Henry (Raulyns), B.C.L., B.Can.L. supd. 29 Jan., 1507-8, canon of Sarum 1512, rector of Compton Bassett, Wilts, 1521, archdeacon of Salisbury 1524-6. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rawlings, James, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall disp. 7 Dec., 1603.

Rawlins, James, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1617; (incorporated 14 July, 1618), M.A. 1620, re-incorporated 11 July, 1620, canon of Hereford 1622, rector of Burford (3rd portion), Salop, 1623, and of Stoke Lucy, co. Hereford, 1626. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rawlins, John, of St. Alban Hall in or before 1572; B.A. 15 Feb., 1576-7; perhaps student of Inner Temple 1583, as of London, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rawlyns, Maurice, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 26 June, 1634.

Rawlins, Richard, B.D. 1492, D.D. 1495, fellow 1480, and warden of Merton Coll. 1508-21, rector of St. Mary Woolnoth, London, 1494, canon of St. Paul's 1499, vicar of Hendon, Middlesex, 1504, vicar of Thornton, Yorks, 1505, rector of St. Martin, Ludgate, 1514, subdean of York 1504-7, archdeacon of Huntingdon 1505 or 1514-23, canon of Windsor 1506, archdeacon of Cleveland 1507, king's almoner 1514, bishop of St. Davids 1523, until his death 18 Feb., 1535-6. See Ath. ii. 743; O.H.S. iv. 162; Lansdowne MS. 979, f. 138; & Foster's Index Eccl.]
Rawlins, Richard, B.A. 5 Feb., 1531-2, M.A. 26 Nov., 1534; one of these names rector of South Somercotes, co. Lincoln, 1552. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rawlins, Roger, M.A. of Cambr.; incorporated 8 July, 1614 (one of these names sizar of Trinity Coll. 15 June, 1575, and B.A. 1578), another student of Gray's Inn 1583, as of London. See Foster's Graduati Cantab. & Gray's Inn Reg.

Rawlins, Thomas, B.A. of Cambridge; incorporated 30 June, 1592, from St. John's Coll., Oxford, M.A. 14 July following; perhaps D.Med. from Clare Hall, Cambridge, 1599, a fellow of the college of physicians 1605. See Munk's Roll, i. 121; & Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Rawlins, Thomas, B.C.L. from Jesus Coll. 16 May, 1704, chaplain to the English merchants at Smyrna, vicar of Chatteris, co. Cambridge, 1691, rector of Feltwell St. Nicholas, Norfolk, 1701, until his death in 1732. See Rawl. iii. 387 and xxi. 87; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Rawlins, Walter, B.A. 18 March, 1573-4.
Rawlins, William, B.Can.L. sup. 10 Dec., 1513, B.C.L. 20 Feb., 1513-14.
Rawlins, William, B.A. 10 May, 1631; M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 17 Jan., 1633-4. See Rawles.
Rawlins, William, "ex. aed. Xti. unus e cho." privilegiatus 13 June, 1699; chorister Christ Church.
Rawlinson, Francis, 1605. See Rallinson.
Rawlinson, Richard, of Queen's Coll., 1636. See Rallingson. page 1230.
Rawlinson, William, 1694-5. See Rollinson.
Rawse, Thomas (Rawsey), B.A. — June, 1518, M.A. 1 July, 1521.
Rawson, Christopher (Rauson), B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 28 Dec., 1532, M.A. 10 May, 1535, B.D. 20 April, 1540.

Rawson, James, subscribed 16 April, 1613; B.A. from Broadgates Hall 11 Feb., 1617-18, M.A. 28 June, 1620; perhaps incumbent of Kingston free chapel, Dorset, 1640, and rector of Chesilbourne 1645, and of Haselbury Bryan, Dorset, 1654 by the Westminster assembly, until ejected in 1662 for nonconformity. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Calamy, ii. 130; & Add. MSS. 15,669-70.
Rawson, Nicholas, M.A.; B.D. supd. — Dec., 1508; admon. at Oxford ("Magister") 10 Jan., 1511.
Rawson, William, B.A. from University Coll. supd. Michaelmas term 1546, M.A. 13 Feb., 1553-4; See Bloxam, iv. 103, Hanson or Ranson.
Raybould, Edward, B.A. from Gloucester Hall 31 Jan., 1599-1600.
Ray, See also Rea, Wray, Wrey, etc.
Raye, John (Rey or Ree), B.A. 1547-8, M.A. supd. June, 1556, rector of Ilchester, with free chapel of Whitehall, Somerset, 1554, and of Stocklinch Ottery 1556. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ray, William, created D.C.L. 2 Aug., 1704.

Rayment, Robert, B.A. from Peter House, Cambridge 1598-9, M.A. 1602; (incorporated 14 July, 1602), B.D. 1609, rector of Buers, Essex, 1608-36, licenced 9 May, 1613, to marry Anne, daugher of Thomas Meredith, late parson of North Bemfleet, deceased; died in 1636. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster.

Rayne, John, D.L.L. of Cambridge 1506 (D.Can.L. 1511), supd. 21 Nov., 1521, for incorporation; advocate of Doctors' Commons 1513, canon of Brampton in Lincoln 1521, prebendary of Thame, also in Lincoln, 1531; died before 19 Nov., 1536. See Cooper, i. 60; Lee's Thame, 138; & Coote's Civilians, 11.

Rayner, John, B.A. from Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1711; incorporated as M.A. from Lincoln Coll. 23 April, 1714; one of these names rector of Long Leadenham, co. Lincoln, 1723. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rayner, Robert (Raynarde), B.A. 6 April, 1530.
Raynes, See also Raines.
Raynsforde, Edward, B.A. 19 Feb., 1578-9.

Raynsford, (Sir) Francis (Rainsford), subscribed 18 June, 1613; B.A. from Hart Hall 29 Oct., 1618, "eq. aur. fil."; of Clifford Chambers, student of Gray's Inn 1620 (as son of Henry, of Clifford Chambers, co. Gloucester, kt.), a captain and knighted at Theobalds 22 June, 1632; died 11 June, 1635; brother of Henry 1613. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Raynsforde, (Sir) Henry (Rainsforde), subscribed 18 June, 1613; of Clifford, co. Gloucester, and of Combe, Hants, student of Middle Temple 1616 (as son and heir of Henry, of Clifford Chambers, co. Gloucester, kt.), knighted at Tutbury 17 Aug., 1624, M.P. Andover 1640, until he died 10 April, 1641; brother of Francis 1613, and father of Henry next-named. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Raynsford, Thomas, B.A. from Catherine Hall, Cambridge, 1671-2, M.A. 1675; incorporated 15 July, 1679.
Rea, Ambrose, B.A. from Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, 1664-5, M.A. 1669; incorporated 13 July, 1669, rector of Wakes Colne, Essex, 1678. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rea, John, subscribed 16 April, 1613; one of these names rector of Blethvaugh, Radnor, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Reade, Alexander, M.A. of Aberdeen University; incorporated 28 May, 1620, created D.Med. the day following; incorporated at Cambridge 7 July, 1624, fellow of college of physicians 1624; died in Oct., 1641. See Fasti, i. 396; & Munk's Roll, i. 183.
Reade, Davye, student residing in the town in July, 1562.
Reede, John, B.A. dispd. 16 Feb., 1507-8 (? from All Souls' Coll.); possibly student of Inner Temple 1511, as son and heir of Robert, chief justice. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Reed, John, M.A., B.D.; allowed to receive the cap of D.D. from the vice-chancellor, &c., 29 Jan., 1507-8, D.D. supd. 31 May, 1511, fellow 1517, and master (or warden) of St. Mary's college, Winchester, chaplain to Prince Arthur 1500, prebendary of Lincoln 1503, canon of Chichester, warden of New Coll. 1520, until his death 1 April, 1521. See Gutch, i. 202; & Fasti, i. 3, 23, and 80.

Reede, John, fellow New Coll., 1508-17, from Hedley, Southants, B.C.L. 19 Dec., 1515, B.D. dispd. 21 Feb., 1520-1, D.C.L. supd. 11 Dec., 1528, B.Can.L. 25 Feb., 1528-9, D.Can.L. 15 March, 1528-9; perhaps fellow of Winchester college 1517, see preceding entry. See Fasti, i. 80.
Reade, John, M.A. of Cambridge; incorporated 13 July, 1624.
Reade, John, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 19 Feb., 1629-30.
Re(a)de, Lewis, B.C.L. supd. 8 Feb., 1508-9.
Re(a)de, Richard, M.A. of Cambridge; incorporated 14 Oct., 1560; one of these names beneficed in Wilts. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Reade, Richard, schol. fac. mus. 20 years, B.Mus. 7 July, 1592; yeoman bedel-of-law, of All Saints parish apparently, belonging to the choir of Christ Church; will at Oxford proved 5 April, 1617. See Fasti, i. 257.

Read, Richard, created D.Med. Leyden 27 July, 1656; incorporated 1 June, 1663; of Witham, Essex, licenced 13 Oct., 1660, to marry Elizabeth Ayliffe, of Melbourne, co. Cambridge. See London Marriage Licences, ed. Foster; & Fasti, ii. 267.
Re(a)de, Robert, B.A. supd. 27 June, 1516; one of these names rector of West Bagborough, Somerset, 1516-64. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Re(a)de, Thomas, B.A. 8 July, 1522, B.C.L. 9 May, 1524; one of these names beneficed in Somersetshire. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reade, Thomas, M.A. of Aberdeen University; incorporated 28 May, 1620; he had before been a student at Oxford. See Thomas 1593, & Fasti, i. 394.
Reade, William, B.A. from Lincoln Coll., 12 Feb., 1574-5, fellow 1575-82, M.A. 6 July, 1577; one of these names vicar of Hoby, co. Leicester, 1581. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reader, James, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 24 June, 1656.
Reading, Henry(Redyng),, chaplain, B.Can.L. supd. 18 June, 1505. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reading, John (Redinge), of Hart Hall, in or before 1568; will at Oxford proved, 27 June, 1583.
Readinge, John, subscribed 11 July, 1606.
Reading, Na(thaniel), created M.A. from Merton Coll. 1 July, 1646; perhaps bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1658, as of Northampton, gen. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Reading, Richard (Redyng), B.A. 20 Feb, 1532-3; perhaps rector of Tidmarsh, Berks, 1543. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reading, Thomas, of Brasenose Coll. 1604. See Ridding, page 1256.
Readyng, Thomas, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 9 July, 1624, rector of Brobury, co. Hereford, 1630. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reading, William (Redyng), B.A. 3 Feb., 1525-6, M.A. 13 July, 1530.
Reading, William (Redyng or Ryding), B.A. 8 Nov., 1542, M.A. 24 March, 1543-4, chaplain to King Edward or preacher at Windsor 1552, vicar of Sonning, Berks, 1553. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Ready, Alexander (Redie or Readye), B.A. from St. John's Coll. 10 July, 1568; M.A. 17 Jan., 1571-2, perhaps from Gloucester Hall; vicar of Sherborne, co. Gloucester 1571. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Readie, John, B.A. 22 Feb., 1574-5, M.A. 13 March, 1578-9, vicar of Hoo, Kent, 1587. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reanus, John, B.A. supd. 10 April, 1548.

Reason, Robert, subscribed 11 June, 1613; B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 31 Jan., 1613-14, M.A. 29 Oct., 1617, rector of Otterhampton, 1618, and vicar of Cannington, Somerset, 1619. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Redford, Edward (Radforth), B.A. 9 Feb., 1513-14; one of these names rector of Staunton, Wilts, 1541, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Redman, Edward (Redmayne), secular chaplain, B.Can.L. and B.C.L. supd. 23 June, 1528; one of these names canon of St. Stephen, Westminster, 1543. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Redman, John, B.A. 23 Oct., 1508, see Fasti, i. 24; perhaps rector of Great Birch, Essex, 1518, and vicar of Northall, Middlesex, 1518. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Redman, John, of Corpus Christi Coll. Oxon; B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1525-6, M.A. and fellow 1530, B.D. 1534, D.D. 1537, master of King's Hall 1542-6, Michael House, and first master of Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1546, public orator 1537, lady Margaret professor of divinity 1538-42 and 1549, one of the compilers of the liturgy, prebendary of Westminster 1540, and of Wells 1540, rector of Calverton, Bucks, 1548, archdeacon of Stafford 1540-7, and of Taunton 1541; died 4 Nov., 1551, aged 52, buried in Westminster abbey. See Ath. i. 193; Al. West. 4; Cooper, i. 107; Lansdowne MS. 980, ff. 956-8; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Reece, See also Rees.
Reece, Giles (Reise), student Christ Church 1571, B.A. 19 Feb., 1573-4. See O.H.S. xii. 39.
Reek, John (or Reck), free Baron of; created D.C.L. 25 Feb., 1680-1; in the retinue of the Prince of Hanover. See Fasti, ii. 378.
Rees, See also Reece.

Re(e)ve, Charles, s. Augustin, of Bracondale, Norfolk, gent. Scholar Caius Coll., Cambridge, 29 April, 1663, aged 14, B.A. 1666; incorporated from Wadham Coll. 7 July, 1668, M.A. 16 Nov., 1669, rector of Blofield 1671, and of Long Stratton, Norfolk, 1681. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Venn, 257.
Re(e)ve, Edmund, B.A. 22 Feb., 1535-6, as "Edward."

Reeve, Edward, subscribed 10 Dec., 1613; perhaps B.A. from Merton Coll. 13 May, 1618, as Reyves; one of these names vicar of Staverton-with-Bodington, co. Gloucester, 1625. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Reeve, John (Rive or Reve), B.A. from Magdalen Hall 16 May, 1569, M.A. 26 Feb., 1572-3; perhaps fellow Eton college 1575, rector of Clewer, Berks, 1582, and rector of Stoke D'Alborne, Surrey, 1589. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Re(e)ve, John, B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1576-7, M.A. 1580 (incorporated 11 July, 1592); D.D. 1639. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Reeve, Richard (Ryve or Reve), student at Cambridge and Oxford, canon; B.Can.L. supd. 31 Oct., 1510; perhaps rector of Weeley, Essex, resigned 1514. See Newcourt.
Re(e)ve, Robert, chorister Magdalen Coll., 1501, B.A. 26 June, 1507. See Bloxam, i. 4.
Reeve, Robert, B.C.L. 23 March, 1527-8. See Ryve.

Re(e)ve, Samuel, of Bucks, pleb. Of Magdalen Hall in or before 1572, aged 21; student Christ Church 1573, B.A. 28 Feb., 1575-6, M.A. 22 May, 1579, rector of Marsh Gibbon, Bucks, 1583. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Re(e)ve, Thomas chaplain, B.A. 4 Feb., 1540-1; perhaps rector of Thrandeston als. Franston, Suffolk, 1556. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Re(e)ve, Thomas, s. Daniel, of Tibenham, Norfolk, D.D., scholar Caius Coll., Cambridge, 31 Jan., 1599-1600, aged 16, B.A. 1603-4, M.A. 1607; incorporated 14 July, 1607 (as Rive), rector of Upwell 1625, of Aldborough 1628, and of Coleby, Norfolk, 1628, until sequestered in 1645 by the Westminster assembly. See Add. MS. 15,669, p. 145; Venn, 91; & Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Reeves, George, B.A. from Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 1604-5, M.A. 1608, incorporated 13 July, 1619, vicar of Loders, Dorset, 1611. See Foster's Ind. Eccl.

Regimorter, Assuerus, of London, D.Med. of Padua 11 Feb., 1635-6; incorporated 26 March, 1636, baptised in the Dutch church, London, 6 Jan., 1615, fellow of college of physicians 1643, practiced in Lime Street, London, 'during the reign of Oliver,' died 25 Nov., 1650; father of the next. See Fasti, i. 489; & Munk's Roll. i. 235.
Regius, Petrus, a Frenchman, M.A. of Paris; '12 years ago,' B.D. supd. 23 June, 1576, 'an exile for religion,' catechistical lecturer in the university. Fasti. i. 201.
Relffe, John, of Exeter Coll., 1663. See Ralph.

Remington, Richard, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1564-5, M.A. 1568, incorporated 9 July, 1577, licentiate of medicine, 1585, served the collegiate church of Lowthrop 1579, rector of Brandeston 1580, of Bainton 1590, and of Lockington (all Yorks) 1582, archdeacon of Cleveland, 1582, and of the East Riding of Yorkshire 1589, canon of York 1585, and of Southwell 1599-1607. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Fasti, i. 207.
Reness, Lewis, D.D. by diploma 5 July, 1667, pastor of the church at Breda, and professor of divinity in the Aurangian college there. See Fasti, ii. 300.
Rennell, See also Reynell.
Reppes, Henry, B.A. (? from Christ's Coll.), Cambridge, 1604. incorporated 10 July, 1604; student of Lincoln's Inn 1606, as of Norfolk, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Resbury, Nathaniel, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1661-2, M.A. 1672; incorporated 15 July, 1673, B. and D.D. from Merton Coll. 11 July, 1692, vicar of Wandsworth, Surrey, 1674, rector of Broughton Gifford, Wilts, 1687, rector of St. Paul's, Shadwell, 1689; licenced (V.G.) 14 Sept., 1691, to marry Mary Cordell, of St. Matthew, Friday Street, London, widow; chaplain in ordinary to William III. and Q. Mary; died 21 July, 1711, aged 68; M.I. St. Giles church, Spalding. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Rawl. v. 127; & Fasti, ii. 337.
Reschius, Anthony (or Raschius), friar, B.D. 6 July, 1558,; had studied divinity 12 years in Paris and Oxford. See Fasti, i. 155.
Retyll, Anthony (Mr.), created M.A. 16 Jan., 1642-3.
Reuter, Adam, born in co. Denbigh, sometime of Exeter Coll. (1608), a licentiate in both the laws, "a learned and ingenious Welshman." See Ath. ii. 420.
Reveley, Benedict, 1649. See Rively.
Revett, Jonas, of Trinity Coll. 1608. See Rivett.
Reynell, See also Rennell.

Reynell, Carew, eq. aur. fil.; B.A. from Exeter Coll. 17 Jan., 1617-18; of Rivershill, Hants, student of Inner Temple 1618 (as son and heir of George, of Devon, kt.); licenced 16 Dec., 1628, to marry Mary, daughter of Marcellus Rivers, esq., of St. Saviour, Southwark; marshal of the king's bench; died in Aug., 1657; father of Carew next-named, and of George 1653. See Foster's Inns of Court Register.
Reynell, Edward, of West Ogwell, Devon; admitted to Exeter Coll. 30 May, 1629, aged 17. See Ath. iii. 658; & Prince's Worthies of Devon, 524.]

Reynell, George, gent. Lincoln Coll., subscribed 29 June, 1653; fellow Corpus Christi Coll.; B.A. 15 Oct., 1657, M.A. 4 March, 1660-1, B.D. 16 Nov., 1669, D.D. 10 March, 1680-1 (s. Carew 1618); vicar of Pinhoe 1662, and rector of Ringmore, Devon, 1664, canon of Lincoln 1682; will at Oxford proved 20 July, 1687, buried in college chapel; brother of Carew 1652. See Gutch, i. 408; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Reynell, Walter, subscribed 16 June, 1610; perhaps brother of Richard and Thomas 1602.
Reyner, See Rayner.
Reynes, Buller, of Merton Coll. 1666. See Reymes.
Reynolds, Charles (Raynoldys), B.C.L. supd. 17 July, 1528.

Reynolds, Edmund (Raynolds), at Corpus Christi Coll. 1557, from Devon, fellow 1559. B.A., 27 Jan., 1560-1 M.A. 10 Nov., 1564, a tutor in Gloucester Hall many years, and died a wealthy man; brother of William, 1572, etc. See Ath. i. 615.
Reynolds, Edward, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 4 March, 1580-1, fellow 1581, M.A. 13 Oct., 1584.
Reynolds, Gilbert, B.A. from Clare Hall, Cambridge, 1671-2, M.A. 1675; incorporated 10 July, 1677.
Reynolds, Isaac, cler. fil. Pembroke Coll., subscribed 15 Dec., 1676.

Reynold, Jerome, at Corpus Christi Coll. 1548, from Devon, B.A. 5 July, 1553, fellow 1556, M.A. 29 June, 1557, "supd. for B.Med. and leave to practice Feb., 1563-4;" inventory at Oxford 8 March, 1571, as of Oriel Coll., M.A.; brother of William 1572, etc. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg. & Fasti, i. 162.
Reynold, John (or Rynoldis), Dominican, preacher at Oxford and Exeter, B.D. supd. 12 April, 1526.
Reynolds, John (Raynolds), B.C.L. 28 Nov., 1560; possibly bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1564. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Reynolds, John, fellow of All Souls' Coll., B.A. 19 Nov. 1562, M.A. 22 March, 1566-7; admon. at Oxford 12 Oct., 1571.

Reynolds, John, fellow King's Coll., Cambridge, from Eton, B.A. 1693-4, M.A. 1698; B.D. Oxford by diploma 7 Oct., 1718 (s. John, vicar of the parish of St. Thomas the Apostle, Exeter and prebendary of Exeter); born there 2 July, 1671, master of the high school 1694, and of the free school, both Exeter, prebendary of Exeter 1729, canon residentiary 1737, fellow of Eton college 1733, until his death 27 July, 1758; brother of Joshua 1691, and of Samuel. See Rawl. vi. 53, and xviii. 276; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Reynold, Lewis (or Raynoldes), B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 10 March, 1531-2, M.A. 26 June, 1535, demy 1536, northern proctor 1540; perhaps vicar of Layston, Herts, 1572. See Bloxam, iv. 75; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Reynolds, Thadeus, secular chaplain, B.Can.L. 18 Feb., 1527-8.

Reynold, Thomas (Raynoldes), B.A. 28 Feb., 1518-19, fellow Merton Coll, 1520, M.A. supd. 6 July, 1521, canon of Cardinal college 1524-30, rector of Lambourne, Berks, 1531, and of Lapworth 1540. See Gutch, i. 423; & O.H.S. iv. 251.

Reynolde, Thomas, s. Richard, of Pinhoe, Devon; B.A. from Merton Coll. 25 Feb., 1521-2, fellow 1524, M.A. 8 Feb., 1525-6, B.D. 28 June, 1536, D.D. 13 July, 1536, warden of his college (Dec.) 1545-59, and vice-chancellor 1556-7, rector of Ditcheat, Somerset, 1540, canon residentiary of Exeter, rector of Holsworthy, Devon, 1543, dean of Bristol 1553, and of Exeter 1554, deprived 1559, canon of Westminster 1545, chaplain to Queen Mary, who nominated him bishop of Hereford, but she died before consecration, and Queen Elizabeth committed him a prisoner to the Marshalsea, Southwark, where he died about 24 Nov., 1559, buried in St. Margaret's, Westminster, 26th. See O.H.S. iv. 165; & Fasti, i. 103.

Reynolds, Thomas, subscribed 3 June, 1614; B.A. from Wadham Coll. 10 Feb., 1617-18, M.A. 7 July, 1621; one of these names rector of Merton, Norfolk, 1640, another rector of Radwinter, Essex, 1646, by the Westminster assembly. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reynolds, Thomas, gent. Pembroke Coll., subscribed 7 Nov., 1655, B.A. 11 July, 1659, M.A. 16 April, 1662; one of these names vicar of Aston Rowant, Oxon, 1663. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Reynold, William (Raynolde or Ranalde), B.A. 2 July, 1515, M.A. — June, 1518.

Reynolds, William, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1644-5 (incorporated 10 Oct., 1649), M.A. 1648, minister at St. Mary, Nottingham, 1651, until ejected in 1662; died 26 Feb., 1698-9, aged 73. See Calamy, iii. 101; and Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Rhine, Adolphus John, prince palatine of the; Duke of Bavaria, etc.; created D.C.L. 14 June, 1683 (son of Prince Adolphus, uncle to the king of Sweedland) "twas common report that he came into England with his uncle to break off the match between Prince George of Denmark and the lady Anne." See Fasti, ii. 390-1.

Rhine, Charles, prince palatine of the; created D.Med. 9 Sept., 1680, and diplomated 2 Oct., following (s. Charles Lewis, next-named); born 31 March, 1651. See Foster's Peerage, "Royal lineage of England;" & Fasti, ii. 378.
Rhine, Frederick, prince palatine of the; 5th duke of Bavaria 1613. See Bohemia, page 144.

Rhine, John William, prince palatine of the; created D.C.L. 2 June, 1675; Prince of Newburg, Duke of Bavaria, etc.; came to England purposely to pay his respects to the lady Mary, eldest daughter of James, Duke of York. See Ath. i. p. lxxvi; & Fasti, i. 349.
Rhine, Rupert, prince palatine of the. See Cumberland, p. 362.
Rhodes, George, M.A. from Clare Hall, Cambridge; incorporated 14 July, 1674, rector of Blithfield, 1676 and of Checkley, co. Stafford, 1703. See Foster's Index Ecel.
R(h)odes, John, B.A. 9 July, 1543; perhaps rector of Shipton Oliffe, co. Gloucester, 1562. See Foster's Index Eccl.
R(h)odes, John, subscribed 16 June, 1610, only in subs. book, and there erased.
Rhodes, John, s. William, of Burford, Oxon, pleb., privilegiatus, 1 April, 1664, aged 18; B.C.L. from St. John's Coll. 1670; brother of Thomas 1673.
Riader, Oliver, secular chaplain; B.C.L. 3 July, 1519.
Riall, Haynes, of Wadham Coll. 1584. See Ryall.
Rice, See also Reece and Rees.
Rice, Arthur, See Riches.
Rice, David (Apres), chaplain, B.Can.L. 4 Feb., 1507-8.
Rice, David, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 3 March, 1572-3. See O.H.S. xii. 26.
Rice, Geoffrey ap, B.A. supd. 10 Dec., 1529.
Rice, Hugh Ap (Aprice), B.C.L. 19 Feb., 1527-8, vicar of Skenfrith in the marches of Wales, co. Monmouth, 1536, rector of Llanfawr, co. Merioneth, 1537, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rice, John (Ryse), B.A. and M.A. sup. 7 June, 1524.
Rice, John Ap, Cistercian and Abbot of 'Strata Marcella' in St. Asaph; B.D. supd. 2 May, 1529. See Fasti, i. 82.
Rice, John Ap, secular chaplain, B.Can.L. 8 July, 1532.
Rice, John, B.A. from Exeter Coll. 13 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 10 July, 1590; perhaps rector of Lapford 1599, vicar of Cornwood, Devon, 1603. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rice, Lewis ap (Apres), chaplain, B.Can.L. 26 March, 1508, precentor of St. Davids 1509.
Rice, Maurice ap, B.A. 6 (?May), 1531; one of these names vicar of Nantmel, co. Radnor, 1559. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rice, Morgan ap (Apryce), B.A. supd. 24 Feb., 1505-6.
Rice, Renold (Ryce), secular chaplain, B.Can.L. 24 Oct., 1525.
Rice, Richard (Rise), B.A. from Christ Church 14 June, 1585, M.A. 7 July, 1600, "manciple;" will at Oxford proved 26 May, 1623.
Rice, Richard, See Reece
Rice, Thomas ap (Apryse), B.C.L. 2 July, 1509.
Rice, Thomas (Ryce or Rysse), B.A. from Lincoln Coll. —. Feb., 1541-2, M.A. 1547-8.
Rice, Thomas (Ryce), B.A. supd. 1548.
Rice, Thomas (Ryse), student residing in the town in July, 1562. Tutor: his father, of St. Giles, Oxford.
Rice, Thomas, s. Rice ap Shore, of Penterk, co. Glamorgan; privilegiatus 23 June, 1665, aged 45; "Hortulanus College, Pembroke."
Rich, Charles, subscribed 2 Nov., 1607.

Rich, Robert, 2nd earl of Warwick; M.A. of Cambridge 1624; incorporated 25 Aug., 1624 (s. Robert, 1st earl), K.B. 1603, of the Inner Temple 1604, M.P. Maldon 1604-11, Essex 1614, admiral of the royal navy for the parliament 1642, speaker of the House of Lords 1642, 1648, etc.; died 18 April, 1658; father of the next. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Rich, Robert, baron; created D.C.L. 1 or 2 Nov., 1642, 3rd earl of Warwick 1658, of Gray's Inn 1619, M.P. Essex Jan.-March, 1629 and 1640, until summoned to parliament as Baron Rich 26 Jan., 1641; died 29 May, 1659.

Rich, Samuel, created M.A. of Aberdeen University; incorporated from Trinity Coll. 29 May, 1652; perhaps 4s. Thomas, of North Cerney, co. Gloucester, a master in chancery; rector of North Cerney aged 77 in 1682, and father of the next.

Rich, William, a pensioner of Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1583, B.A. 1586-7, fellow, M.A. 1590 (incorporated 12 Feb., 1590-1), proctor 1598, vicar of Tilney, Norfolk, 1601. See Cooper, ii. 385; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Richardes, David, of co. Anglesey, pleb.; privilegiatus 10 Dec., 1585, aged 19.

Richards, Edward, 16 years student in theology; B.D. 17 Dec., 1585, vicar of Ditchling 1573, and rector of Plumpton 1574, prebendary of Chichester 1582, vicar of Westham (all Sussex) 1585. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Richards, Edward, subscribed 4 Nov., 1614.
Richards, George, gent. Magdalen Hall, subscribed 7 Nov., 1655.
Richards, Gryffin, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 10 July, 1594, M.A. 23 June, 1597, then in orders as Prichard, which see page 1209.

Richards, Hugh, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 15 July, 1578, M.A. 8 July, 1584, vicar of Haddenham, Bucks, 1582, of Horley and Hornton, Oxon, 1582, and of Aldermaston, Berks, 1587, rector of Woolhampton, Berks, 1593. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Richards, Humphrey, B.A. 28 Feb., 1578-9.
Richardys, John, B.Gram. sup. 8 Feb., 1531-2.
Richards, Lewis (Rychardys), chaplain, B.Can.L. 24 May, 1509.
Richards, Leyson, B.A. from All Souls' Coll., supd. April, 1565.
Richard, Martin, B.A. 1 Dec., 1532.
Richard, Rodolph, M.A. 7 years, B.D. supd. 23 June, 1576.

Richards, Thomas, Benedictine, B.D. supd. 29 Oct., 1515, B.D. Cambridge 1517, prior of Totnes, Devon, 1528, rector of St. George, Exeter; made his will 10 Aug., 1553, proved 14 April, 1564. See Cooper, i. 233.
Richards, Thomas, B.A. supd. 7 Dec., 1571. See O.H.S. xii. 10.
Richards, William (Richarddis), B.Gram. 7 July, 1519.
Richards, William, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 27 Oct., 1629, M.A. 5 June, 1632, rector of Irmingland, Norfolk, 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Richardson, Christopher, B.A. 20 March, 1541-2, M.A. 1545.

Richardson, Gabriel, cler. fil.; from co. Lincoln, admitted to Brasenose Coll. 1602, B.A. 20 July, 1604, fellow 1607, M.A. 7 July, 1608, B.D. 8 July, 1619, author of the "State of Europe," rector of Heythrop, Oxon, 1635; died 31 Dec., 1642, buried in the church of St. Mary the Virgin; admon. at Oxford 4 Jan., following. See Ath. iii. 37; Foster's Index Eccl.
Richardson, John, B.C.L. 27 March, 1562-3; perhaps a student of Inner Temple 1556, as of Pershore, co. Worcester. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Richardson, John, B.A. 10 March, 1571-2.
Richardson, John, B.A. from Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1597-8, M.A. 1609; incorporated 11 July, 1609. See Foster's Graduati Cantab & Fasti, i. 336.
Richardson, John, subscribed 18 June, 1613; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 15 Feb., 1614-15, M.A. from Magdalen Hall 9 July, 1619.

Richardson, Joshua, of Brasenose Coll. 1634; B.A. 30 Jan., 1637-8, M.A. 3 July, 1641, vicar of Holy Cross (or St. Crucis), Shrewsbury, 1645-7, rector of Middle, Salop, ejected 1662; died 1 Sept., 1671; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Calamy, iii. 149.
Richardson, Richard, principal of New Inn Hall 1542-5.

Richardson, Richard, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1656-7, M.A. 1660, B.D. 1671; incorporated 15 July, 1673, vicar of Brixworth, 1663, and rector of Lamport, Northants, 1699; father of Adrian. See Ath. iii. 209 (art. Butler); & Foster's Index Eccl.

Richardson, Robert, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1671-2, M.A. 1677; incorporated 10 July, 1677, vicar of Atwick, Yorks, 1680; buried at Hornsea, Yorks, 19 March, 1727-8. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Richardson, Samuel, fellow Sydney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1676-7, M.A. 1680; incorporated 11 July, 1682, vicar of Wilshampstead, Beds, 1687, canon of Lincoln 1699-1724. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Richardson, Stephen, "Typotheta," (9) s. William, of Brailes, co. Warwick (cler.), privilegiatus 24 Dec., 1691; baptised at Brailes 7 Jan., 1670-1, made his will 6 Oct., 1753, proved 20 Sept., 1756; father of Stephen 1720, and of Zaccheus, 1756. See Griffiths' Index & Hearne's Collection, vols. 33 and 36.

Richardson, Thomas, B.A. 9 Feb., 1574-5, one of these names rector of Gloucester St. John Baptist 1576, vicar of Birtsmorton, co. Worcester, 1583, rector of Stratton, co. Gloucester, 1585. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Richardson, William, created M.A. 30 April, 1712; M.A. Edinburgh University 22 April, 1709; came to England and was ordained among the presbyterians, preached at meetings in Pinmakers' hall and St. John's, Clerkenwell, conformed to the church of England, ordained priest 1711, chaplain to Robert, Earl of Londonderry, 1712. See Rawl, iii. 243, and xviii. 284.
Richer, John, fellow Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; B.A. 1666-7, M.A. 1670; incorporated 26 Oct., 1670, rector of Dickleburgh, Norfolk, 1680. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Richier, Peter, of Maremne, France; D.Med. of Bourdeaux university 1634; incorporated 17 Nov., 1662, naturalised 25 Feb., 1661-2, then of the Bayle of Lincoln, described as born at Marennes in Saintonge, in France, eldest son of Peter, sieur de Wandelaincourt and doctor in divinity. See Fasti, ii. 262.
Richmond, Thomas, Cistercian, B.D. supd. 10 Dec., 1529.
Rickmansworth, Ralph, Benedictine, B.D. supd. Feb., 1537-8.

Riddell, William, subscribed 2 Dec., 1614; one of these names bar.-at- law, Lincoln's Inn, 1623 (as son and heir of Thomas, of Gateshead-upon-Tyne, esq.), knighted 17 July, 1633; died 21 Jan., 1654. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Rideout, Thomas, of Exeter Coll. 1624. See Ridat.
Rider, See also Ryder.

Rider, Henry, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1707-8, M.A. 1711 (incorporated 9 July, 1711), D.D. 1749, vicar of Grimley, co. Worcester, 1713, rector of Worcester St. Nicholas 1717, canon of Lichfield 1719, archdeacon of Derby 1719, rector of Hertingfordbury, Herts, 1723, and of Hertford St. Andrew 1723, canon of Lincoln 1742, until his death 19 April, 1755. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rider, Robert (? Richd.), B.A. from Christ Church 6 Feb., 1583-4, M.A. 6 July, 1587. See O.H.S. xii. 118.
Ridgate, Philip, B.A. from Trinity Coll. Dublin, 1699, LL.B. 1700; incorporated 13 Oct., 1701.
Ridge, Benizius or Bevitius (Rigge or Rige), of Brasenose Coll. 1552; B.A. from Merton Coll. 16 Oct., 1556, fellow 1557; died in his year of probation. See O.H.S. iv. 263.

Ridge, Richard, fellow of Brasenose Coll., B.A. 30 June, 1516 (as Ryd), M.A. 26 Jan., 1519-20, the last abbot of Notley, near Long Crendon, Bucks, 1534, rector of St. Vedast, Foster Lane, 1543, vicar of Cholsey, Berks, 1543, and of Eltisley, co. Cambridge, 1543. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & Fasti, i. 49.
Ridgeley, Christopher (Rygely), B.A. 6 Feb., 1524-5, M.A. 27 June, 1532.
Ridgway, Robert, of Brasenose Coll. 1610, B.A. 23 May, 1614, M.A. 10 July, 1617.

Ridley, Nicholas, s. Christopher, 2s. Nicholas, of Unthank, near Willymondwyke, Northumberland; B.A. from Pembroke Hall, Cambridge, 1522-3 (refused to be elected fellow University College, Oxon, 1521), fellow, M.A. 1526, proctor 1534, B.D. 1537, D.D. 1540, master of his hall 1540, chaplain to archbishop Cranmer, vicar of Herne, Kent, 1538, king's chaplain and canon of Canterbury 1541, canon of Westminster 1545, vicar of Soham, co. Cambridge, 1547, bishop of Rochester 1547, and of London 1550-4, sent to the Tower on the accession of Q. Mary, declared a heretic at Oxford, and burnt there with Latimer 16 Oct., 1555. See Ath. i. 277; Cooper, i. 135; Lansdowne MS. 980, ff. 169, 177; Foster's Index Eccl.; & Foster's Northumberland Visitations.
Ridley, Thomas (Rydley), B.A. disp. 22 Dec., 1507, M.A. 27 Feb., 1507-8.

Ridley, (Sir) Thomas, s. Thomas; born in Ely, educated at Eton, fellow King's Coll., Cambridge, 1565, B.A. 1570-1, M.A. 1574, D.D. 1583; incorporated as D.C.L. 7 June, 1598 (grandson of Lancelot, son of Nicholas Ridley, of Willymondswick, Northumberland), master of Eton school, an advocate of Doctors' Commons 1590, M.P. Wycombe 1586-7, Lymington 1601, a master in chancery, knighted 24 June, 1619, chancellor to bishop of Winchester, and vicar-general to George Abbot, archbishop of Canterbury; died 23 Jan., 1628-9, buried 27th in St. Bennet Church, Paul's Wharf. See Fasti, i. 279; Coote's Civilians, 62; Lansdowne MS. 984, ff. 101-3; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Ridley, William, B.A. 11 March, 1543-4.
Rigby, Robert, B.C.L. from Christ Church 22 June, 1672.
Rigby, Thomas (or Rybby), M.A. 21 June, 1511, vicar of Ashill, Somerset, 1502. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rigemorter, See Regimorter, page 1244.
Rigge, Edward (Reg), provost of Queen's Coll. 1508, M.A. and B.D. disp. 30 April, 1513, rector of St. Mildred, Bread Street, London, circa 1514. See Newcourt's Repertorium.
Rilston, Edward, of Brasenose Coll. 1578. See Riddleston, page 1256.
Rillston, John, of Brasenose Coll. 1608, B.A. 15 Feb., 1611-12, M.A. 7 July, 1614.
Ring, John, pleb. Magdalen Hall, subscribed 23 Nov., 1655.
Ringrose, John, probably chaplain Magdalen Coll. in and before 1564; one of these names vicar of Weedon Beck, Northants, 1562. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ringrose, John, B.A. from Merton Coll., 12 Nov., 1633, M.A. 26 June, 1638.
Ringstead, Thomas (or Ryngstede), of St. Edmundsbury, Benedictine, B.D. 2 Dec. 1508, D.D. 15 Feb., 1513-14, from Gloucester Hall. See Fasti, i. 41.

Ringwood, Walter (Ringewode), at Corpus Christi Coll. 1553, from Hants, fellow 1555-6, B.A. 11 Feb., 1557-8; perhaps rector of Portlemouth, Devon, 1563, of Goathurst, Somerset, 1570, and of Broad Oak, Cornwall, 1571. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Risbury, Nathaniel, 1662. See Resbury, page
Risbery, Thomas (Rysberi), B.A. 23 Oct., 1508.
Risdon, Nicholas, B.A. 22 Feb., 1574-5, M.A. 12 June, 1577 (? from Gloucester Hall). See O.H.S. xii. 49.
Risdon, Ogier, B.A. from Gloucester Hall 10 Feb., 1569-70, rector of Sutcombe, Devon, 1571, and of Mawgan, Cornwall, 1572. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Risdon, Tristram, Devon antiquary (son of William); buried at Winscot, Devon, 23 June, 1640. See Ath. ii. 609.]

Rishton, Edward, B.A. (?from Brasenose Coll.) 30 April, 1572, M.A. Douay; became a priest at Rome about 1580, was sent into the mission of England and imprisoned 3 or 4 years; died of the plague at St. Manhou, near Pont-a-Musson, in Lorain, about 1585. See Ath. i. 511.

Rishton, Edward, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 6 Feb., 1629-30; M.A. from St. Mary Hall 6 Feb., 1632-3; perhaps (son of Geoffrey, of Antley, co. Lancaster) rector of Heath or Lowne, co. Derby, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rishworth, See also Rushworth.
Riseley, Crescens, B.A. from New Coll. 10 April, 1630 (s. Paul, of Chetwode, Bucks), said to have been fellow of New Coll.

Ritschell, George, 1s. George, of Deutsch Kana, Bohemia, born there 13 Feb., 1616, educated at Strasbourg and Oxford; chief master of Newcastle school 29 Aug., 1648, vicar of Hexham, Northumberland, 1655, or 1656, until his death 28 Dec., 1683, buried in the chancel of his church; father of George and John. See Ath. iv. 124.
Rive, See Reeve.

Rively, Benedict, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1648-9, M.A. 1652; incorporated 13 July, 1658, wrote against Hobbes, of Malmesbury; chaplain to Dr. Reynolds, bishop of Norwich, and curate of St. Andrew's, Norwich, 1679, vicar of Thurgarton, Norfolk, 1688. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Fasti, ii. 215.
Rivers, John, created M.A. 1 Nov., 1642; possibly student of Middle Temple 1644, as 2nd son of Marcellus, barrister of that society. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rivers, Thomas, B.C.L. from New Coll. 7 Feb., 1604-5, D.C.L. 21 June, 1610. See Ryves.
Rives, See Ryves.
Rivet, Andrew, D.D. of Leyden University; incorporated 30 Aug., 1621; born at St. Maixent in the province of Poictou. See Fasti, i. 403.
Rivett, Charles and George, of New Coll. 1672. See Revett.
Rix, Henry, s. Henry, of Cambridge, brewer; sizar of Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge, 28 March, 1655, aged 17; B.A. 1657-8, M.A. 1661; incorporated 14 July, 1663. See Venn, 238.
Rix, John, Minorite; died 1536. See Ath. i. 101.]
Rixton, Ralph, subscribed 3 June, 1614; B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 10 Feb., 1617-18, M.A. 5 July, 1620 (as Rixon), rector of Kingsdon, Somerset, 1641. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Roane, John, of Brasenose Coll. 1634. See Rone.
Roberbery, Robert, Cistercian, B.D. 5 Nov., 1532.
Roberts, David, secular chaplain, B.Can.L. 2 Dec., 1529.
Roberts, Ellis, B.A. from Hart Hall 28 May, 1612; one of these names rector of Sherston Pinkney, Wilts, 1640. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Roberts, Evan, B.A. from New Inn Hall 13 Feb., 1590-1.
Roberts, Evan, B.A. from Trinity Coll. 25 Feb., 1597-8; one of these names rector of Llanystumdwy, co. Carnarvon, 1604. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Roberts, Fulke, B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1598-9, M.A. 1602, B.D. 1609; incorporated 10 July, 1621, prebendary of Norwich 1616 (one of these names rector of St. Clement-at-the-Bridge, Norfolk, 1602); died in April, 1650. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Fasti, i. 400.

Roberts, Griffin, B.A. —, M.A. from Christ Church 30 May, 1555; one of these names rector of Compton Winyates, co. Warwick, 1571, and of Stow-on-the-Wold, co. Gloucester, 1575. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Roberts, Henry, B.A. 15 Feb., 1571-2, M.A. 18 June, 1574; one of these names vicar of Rupa alias Roch Castle, co. Pembroke, 1575. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Roberts, Hugh (Robardis), secular chaplain, B.Can.L. 18 Feb., 1527-8.
Roberts, Hugh, B.A. from Christ Church 7 May, 1586. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Roberts, Hugh, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 5 Feb., 1591-2; M.A. from Jesus Coll. 5 July, 1594; incorporated at Cambridge 1612. See O.H.S. xii. 170.
Roberts, Humfrey, B.C.L. 14 April, 1564.
Roberts, James, M.A. Aberdeen; incorporated at (Trinity Coll.), Cambridge, 1682, and at Oxford 7 July, 1682; perhaps rector of Linton (mediety), Yorks, 1700. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Roberts, John (Roberddys), B.A. 14 March, 1509-10.
Roberts, John (Roberdys), secular chaplain; B.A. sup. 24 Feb., 1520-1; perhaps brother of William 1522, page 1265. See Fasti, i. 55n.
Roberts, John (Roberdis), B.A. 1 July, 1528, M.A. 22 May, 1531.
Roberts, John, B.C.L. 10 June, 1543.
Roberts, John, B.A. from Broadgates Hall 26 Jan., 1569-70.
Roberts, John, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 29 April, 1570, M.A. 17 June, 1573; for one of these names see Thomas, St. Asaph, 641.
Roberts, John, B.A. 14 Feb., 1576-7, M.A. 24 Jan., 1579-80.
Roberts, John, B.A. from Christ Church 2 March, 1580-1. See O.H.S. xii. 95.
Roberts, John, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 28 Nov., 1636. See also Rabetts.

Roberts, Jonathan, late of Cambridge university, follow of Jesus Coll., Oxford, 1648, B.A. 12 Dec., 1648, M.A. 18 June, 1651, vicar of Llanfair-DyffrynClwyd, co. Denbigh, ejected at the restoration; died 26 Sept., 1684. See Calamy, iii. 478; Burrows, 505; & Thomas, 421.
Roberts, Lancelot, B.A. 12 Feb., 1571-2, vicar of St. Goran, Cornwall, 1578. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Roberts, Lewis, B.A. from New Coll. 23 June, 1630, M.A. from Hart Hall, 11 July, 1634, vicar of Catherington, Hants, 1635. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Roberts, Martin chaplain, B.Can.L. supd. 13 Nov., 1511.

Roberts, Michael, B.A. Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1620, M.A. 1623, incorporated at Cambridge 1624, and at Oxford 13 July, 1624, B.D. from Jesus Coll. 8 July, 1630, D.D. 6 April, 1649; perhaps vicar of Llanbadrig 1628, and rector of Llaneugrad, co. Anglesea, 1631, and vicar of Llangonoyd, co. Glamorgan, 1639; intruded principal of Jesus Coll. 1648, resigned 1657; buried in St. Peter's churchyard, Oxford, 3 May, 1679, will at Oxford 2 June following. See Burrows, 505; Ath. i. p. lxxxv; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Roberts, Oliver, B.A. 18 March, 1573-4.
Roberts, Rice, See Rise Roberts, next page.
Roberts, Richard (Robardis), fellow Exeter Coll. 1482, B.C.L. supd. 24 Jan., 1506-7, canon of Lincoln April-May, 1544. See Boase, 26.
Roberts, Richard (Robertis), B.Can.L. sup. 28 Jan., 1528-9.
Roberts, Richard, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 16 July, 1612; M.A. from Hart Hall 20 June, 1615.

Roberts, Robert, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 25 Oct., 1572, M.A. 6 July, 1575, B.D. 17 May, 1585; perhaps rector of Lampeter Velfrey, co. Pembroke, 1583, and rector of Knockin, Salop, 1591. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Robertes, Thomas, student residing in the town July, 1562. Tutor: Thomas Presse, of St. John's Coll.
Roberts, Thomas, B.A. sup. 26 Oct., 1570.
Robartes, Thomas, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 7 Nov., 1598.

Roberts, William (or Robardis), B.C.L. 3 March, 1521-2, B.Can.L. 17 May, 1534, principal of New Inn Hall. 1530-4 and 1535-42, (son of Robert ap Llywelin ap Ithel, of Castelmarch in Llyn), domestic chaplain to bishop Skeffington, archdeacon of Merioneth 1524, rector of Caerwys, Flints, 1537, and of Llanfachraeth, co. Anglesey, 1542, vicar-general or chancellor to the bishop of Bangor 1543, rector of Festiniog, co. Merioneth, resigned 1544, rector of Llanddeniolen-juxta-Bangor 1544, treasurer of Bangor 1554, and rector of Llanvihangel-y-Traeth, co. Merioneth, 1554, rector of Llanbedroc-in-Llyn 1555 (where he was born); died about 1561. See Fasti, i. 55; Lansdowne MS. 986, f. 62; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Roberts, William, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1645-6, M.A. 1649, incorporated 11 July, 1654; one of these names rector of CarltonCurliew, co. Leicester, 1653. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Robertson, John, of Queen's Coll.; admon. at Oxford 28 Sept., 1529.]

Robertson, Patrick, M.A. of Edinburgh University 1672; incorporated (equivalent to B.A.) 1 July, 1714; according to Cat. Grads. he was created M.A. Oxford, 4 June, 1714; perhaps vicar of Berwickupon-Tweed 1686-1726. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Robertson, Ralph, at Corpus Christi Coll. 1536, from diocese of Lincoln as Robinson, B.A. 20 Oct., 1540, fellow 1542, M.A. supd. March, 1543-4, translator of More's 'Utopia,' 1551. See Fasti, i. 111; & O.H.S. i. 199.

Robertson, Thomas, born at Wakefield; B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 18 March, 1520-1, M.A. 6 July, 1525, fellow 1531, B.D. supd. — Dec., 1539, master of Magdalen College school 1526-34, canon of Lincoln 1531, rector of Sherrington St. Laud, Bucks, after 1529, and of Bladon, Oxon, 1541, treasurer of Salisbury cathedral 1540-8, archdeacon of Leicester 1541-60, vicar of Wakefield 1546, prebendary of St. Stephen's, Westminster; one of the compilers of the Liturgy or Common Prayer; dean of Durham 1557, ejected 1559. See Ath. i. 320; Bloxam, iii. 81; Lansdowne MS. 980, ff. 318, 324; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Robins, George, B.Can.L. sup. July, 1532, B.C.L. 27 Feb., 1532-3.
Robins, James, B.A. Lincoln Coll. 7 July, 1614, M.A. 7 July, 1617.
Robins, John (Robyns), B.A. 10 July, 1562.
Robins, Maurice, B.A. from All Souls' Coll., 19 Oct., 1608, vicar of Llanbeblic, co. Carnarvon, 1614. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Robins, Thomas, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1673-4, M.A. 1677; incorporated 10 July, 1677, rector of Otterden, Kent, 1682. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Robins, William, B.A. from St. Alban Hall, supd. 30 April, 1594. See O.H.S. xii. 185.

Robins, William, subscribed 13 May, 1613; B.A. from Magdalen Hall 30 Oct., 1616, M.A. 9 June, 1619, incorporated at Cambridge 1622; possibly student of Gray's Inn 1618, as son of Edward, of Oxford, gen. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Robinson, Barnard, B.A. from Queen's Coll. 20 June, 1579, fellow 1582, M.A. 14 May, 1582, B.D. 13 Nov., 1591, vicar of Burford, Oxon, 1593, and of Torpenhow, Cumberland, 1613-32, canon of Carlisle 1612-34; perhaps father of Barnard next-named, and of George 1631. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Robinson, Edward, M.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1670; incorporated 11 June, 1671.

Robinson, Henry, tabarder Queen's Coll., B.A. 12 July, 1572, fellow 1572, M.A. 20 June, 1575, B.D. 10 July, 1582, D.D. 6 July, 1590, provost of his college 1582-98, principal of St. Edmund Hall 1576-81, a member of Gray's Inn 1601, rector of Fairstead, Essex, 1580, bishop of Carlisle 1598, until his death 13th of the calends of July, 1616, buried in the college chapel. See Ath. ii. 857; Gutch, i. 160; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Robinson, Henry, paup. Brasenose Coll., subscribed 15 March, 1675-6, B.A. 1679, M.A. 1682, perhaps vicar of Fordington 1683, rector of Winterbourne Came, and Winterbourne St. Germayne, Dorset, 1701. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.
Robinson, James, cler. fil. Queen's Coll., subscribed 17 March, 1713-14, B.A. 1718.
Robinson, John, "Magister," fellow of Balliol Coll.; inventory at Oxford 2 Oct., 1511.]
Robinson, John (Robynson), B.A. 14 June, 1525.

Robinson, John, sizar Pembroke Hall, Cambridge, May, 1550, from Richmondshire; B.A. Jan., 1553-4, fellow, 1554, M.A. 1557; incorporated 19 May, 1563, president of St. John's Coll., Oxon, 1564-72, supl. B.D. (Oxon) 22 March, 1566-7, created D.D. of Cambridge 11 June, 1583, rector of East Treswell, Notts, 1556, of Fulbeck, co. Lincoln, 1560, of Thornton, Yorks, 1560, and of Great Easton, Essex, 1566-76, precentor of Lincoln Cathedral 1573, and canon 1574, rector of Brant Broughton 1575, of Fishtoft 1576, of Caistor, co. Lincoln 1576, rector of Kingston Bagpuze, Berks, 1568, archdeacon of Bedford 1574, and of Lincoln 1586, canon of Gloucester 1594, rector of Gransden, co. Cambridge, 1587, and of Somersham, Hunts, 1589, where he was buried March, 1597-8. See O.H.S. i. 252; Fasti, i. 175; Cooper, ii. 235; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Robinson, John, s. William, of Osgathorpe, co. Leicester, sacerd. 11 Oct., 1633, aged 39; "serv." "Hortulanus Coll. Lincoln."

Robinson, John, s. John, rector of Elsing, Norfolk; scholar of Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge, 29 April, 1651, aged 16; B.A. 1654-5, fellow, M.A. 1658; incorporated 14 July, 1663; perhaps rector of St. Augustine's, Norwich, 1688. See Venn, 232; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Robinson, John, s. William, of Long-Whatton, cler. New Inn Hall, subscribed 7 July, 1694, aged 18; B.A. 1698; M.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1714; perhaps vicar of Send 1701, and rector of Wisley, Surrey, 1703, and of Ashbury, Berks, 1709, see also Henry 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Robinson, Leonard, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 30 March, 1528, M.A. 17 June, 1532.
Robinson, Leonard, B.A. 11 Feb., 1576-7, called Robertson in O.H.S. xii. 65.
Robinson, Ralph, of Corpus Christi Coll. 1536. See Robertson, page 1265.
Robinson, Richard, B.A. supd. 8 March, 1577-8; perhaps student of Lincoln's Inn 1577, as of Northants. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Robinson, Thomas, B.A. supd. 25 March, 1522; one of these names rector of Stanwell, Middlesex, 1522. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Robinson, Thomas, B.A. 28 Jan., 1573-4, M.A. 20 June, 1576; one of these names rector of Full Sutton, Yorks, 1580. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Robinson, Thomas, B.A. from Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1599-1600, M.A. 1603; incorporated 11 July, 1606. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Robinson, Thomas, M.A. of Cambridge; incorporated 9 July, 1622; B.A. from Christ's or Trinity Coll. 1618-19, and M.A. 1622.
Robinson, William, B.A. 12 March, 1540-1, M.A. 8 May, 1543. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Robinson, William, B.A. from Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 1576-7, M.A. 1591, B.D. 1599 (incorporated 12 July, 1603), D.D. 1609. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Robinson, William, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1614-15, M.A. 1618, incorporated 10 July, 1621. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Robinson, William, s. Thomas, of Oxford (city), privilegiatus 21 Jan., 1680-1, aged 23; subs. "bookseller," his will at Oxford proved 1 Feb., 1683; his father's will also at Oxford dated 4 April, 1663.
Robinson, William, s. John, of Easby, Yorks, gent. University Coll. subscribed 1 June, 1704, aged 17. See Arch. Æliana, 2nd series, v. 84.
Robkins, Thomas, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 8 May, 1562.

Robotham, Francis, s. F., of Wainflete, co. Lincoln, clerk, sizar St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 2 July, 1656, aged 16; B.A. 1659-60, M.A. 1663; incorporated as M.A. (from Jesus Coll., Cambridge) 12 July, 1670, vicar of Billinghay, co. Lincoln, 1666. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Mayor, 128.
Robotham, Robert, subscribed 9 Dec., 1631; student of Gray's Inn 1632, as son and heir of John, of St. Albans, Herts. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.

Robson, Charles, pensioner of St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 7 July, 1609, B.A. 1612-13, M.A. 1616; incorporated 15 July, 1617, vicar of Weare, Somerset, 1617, on death of Simon, see below, vicar of Buckland Newton, Dorset, 1624, rector of Bagendon, co. Gloucester, 1644. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Robson, Simon, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1572-3, fellow 1573 from co. Durham, M.A. 1576, B.D. 1583; (incorporated 11 July, 1586), D.D. 1594, rector of Staynton-le-Street, co. Durham, 1588-9, of Birkin, Yorks, 1589-1612, and of Blyborough, co. Lincoln, 1594-5, vicar of Weare, Somerset, 1616-17, dean of Bristol 1598, until his death in May or June, 1617. See Fasti, i. 238; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Robye, Thomas, B.A., 10 Oct., 1512.
Roche, See also Roache.
Roche, Thomas chaplain, B.A. 15 Dec., 1511.
Roche, Walter, at Corpus 1555, from Lancaster, fellow 1558, B.A. supd. 1 June, 1559.

Rochfort, George, s. Rob., of Dublin, Ireland, arm. Christ Church, incorporated 10 Oct., 1701, aged 19; B.A. from Trinity Coll., Dublin, 1701; of Gaulstown, chamberlain court of exchequer, Ireland, 1708-30, M.P. co. Westmeath 1707, until his death 8 July, 1730; father of Robert, earl of Belvedere.
Rochford, William, M.A. from Clare Hall, Cambridge; incorporated 14 July, 1663.

Rocke, John, B.A. from All Souls' Coll. 13 July, 1619; M.A. from Brasenose Coll. 3 July, 1622, rector of Tenby, co. Pembroke, 1624, and of Butleigh, Somerset, 1642; father of the next. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rodbert, William (or Radbarte), B.A. 17 Feb., 1538-9, M.A. 7 March, 1541-2, vicar of Kingsbury Episcopi 1546, and of Somerton, Somerset, 1546, as Radbarte. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rodborn, James (Raborne or Raburn), B.Med. 17 Nov., 1506, D.Med. supd. 15 May, 1511.
Rodborn, Thomas (Radborne), M.A. 14 March, 1540-1.
Rodd, William (Rodde or Rood), B.A. supd. 3 July, 1508, M.A. 8 Dec.; 1511; perhaps rector of Goathurst, Somerset, 1530. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rode, See also Roode.
Rodes, See Rhodes.
Rodley, John, Benedictine, B.D. 26 March, 1514. See Rotheley.
Roe, See also Rowe.
Roe, Cheyney, 1611. See Rowe.
Roe, Henry, of Queen's Coll. 1651. See Rowe.

Rowe, Owen, scoutmaster-general in parliamentary army, created M.A. 19 May, 1649, regicide, originally a silkman, one of the king's judges, signed the warrant, became keeper of the magazines, colonel of the city of London militia 1659, surrendered himself on the restoration, condemned to perpetual imprisonment and his estate confiscated; father of Samuel. See Fasti, ii. 136.
Roe, Richard, B.A. from St. Mary Hall 12 June, 1572. See O.H.S. xii. 16.
Roet, Isaac, 1666. See Ath. iii. 760.]

Rogers, (—), "comptroller," created M.A. 6 Sept., 1566, see Fasti, i. 177; perhaps Richard Rogers, of London, goldsmith, comptroller of the mint in the Tower of London. See Foster's Middlesex Visitation, 1663, p. 4.

Rogers, Andrew, B.A. 10 May, 1567, student of Inner Temple 1568, as of Bryanston, Dorset (son of Sir Richard, M.P.), M.P. Christchurch 1571, Wareham 1584-5, Dorset 1586-7, 1588, Christchurch 1597-8, as of Edmondsham. See Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary, & Foster's Inns of Court Reg.

Rogers, Benjamin, s. George, of Windsor, B.Mus. (Queen's Coll.), Cambridge (1658), licenced 8 July, 1669 (Magdalen Coll.), to proceed D.Mus. chorister and then clerk or singing man in chapel royal, Windsor, organist of Christ Church, Dublin, till 1641, and of Eton College 1660, and of Magdalen College, Oxford, 1665-85; baptised in church of New Windsor 2 June, 1614, buried in church of St. Peterle-Bailey, Oxford, 21 June, 1698, aged 84; perhaps father of John 1670. See Bloxam, ii. 192; & Fasti, ii. 305.

Rogers, Christopher, B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 17 July, 1612, M.A. 28 June, 1615, principal of New Inn Hall 1626-43, and 1646-62, created D.D. 24 April, 1648, intruded canon of Christ Church 1648, and one of the parliamentary visitors 1647-52, and 1654-8, rector of St. Peter-leBailey, Oxford, 1626; will at Oxford, proved 21 Aug., 1671. See Foster's Index Eccl.; Fasti, ii. 118; Calamy, i. 254; Burrows, 493, 569; & Add. MS. 15,669, p. 54.

Rogers, Daniel, s. John, s. John, of Deritend, co. Warwick, B.A. 18 July, 1561, M.A. 5 Aug., 1561, a clerk of the council temp. Queen Elizabeth, M.P. Newport, Cornwall, 1588-9, died 11 Feb., 1590; buried in Sunbury Church, Middlesex. See Lansdowne MS. 982, f. 167; & Ath. i. 569.
Rogers, Edward, B.A. from Merton Coll. 19 Oct., 1597, fellow 1598, M.A. 22 Feb., 1602-3; perhaps bar.-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1611, as of Surrey, gen. See Foster's Judges and Barristers & O.H.S. iv. 277.
Rogers, Francis, of Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A. Oxford 18 April, 1577; perhaps M.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1601-2, B.D. 1608, and D.D. 1613. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Rogers, Francis, B.A. 11 July, 1600; one of these names rector of St. Trinity-the-Less, London, 1606, and vicar of Alkham, Kent, 1606. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, James, B.A. from Pembroke Coll., Cambridge, 1600-1, M.A. 1604; incorporated 14 July, 1607. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.
Rogers, John, B.A. 26 Oct., 1513, M.A. 15 March, 1520-1; perhaps of Exeter Coll. from Batcombe, Dorset; will at Oxford proved 7 June, 1527.

Rogers, John (Rodgers), B.A. 1 Feb., 1523-4, M.A. 27 Feb., 1526-7, a schoolmaster in 1525; one of these names rector of St. Trinity-the-Less, London, 1532, vicar-choral of St. Stephen, Westminster, 1545, rector of St. Margaret Moses 1548, vicar of St. Sepulchre 1550, and canon of St. Paul's 1551. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, John, B.A. from Merton Coll. 11 March, 1569-70, M.A. from St. Alban Hall 3 July, 1576.

Rogers, John, D.Med. of Utrecht; incorporated 13 June, 1664 (s. Nehemiah, of Duddinghurst, Essex), practiced his faculty in Bermondsey, Surrey; B.A. from King's Coll., Cambridge, 1646, rector of Purleigh, Essex, 1648-53, minister of St. Mary Magdalen, Bermondsey, Surrey, 1663; ancestor of Lord Blachford. See Fasti, ii. 279; & Foster's Noble and Gentle Families, i. 71.
Rogers, Owen, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 2 March, 1621-2, vicar of Matherne 1623, of Dingestow 1629, and of Llantillio-Crosenny (all co. Monmouth), 1639. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, Peter, B.A. from Christ Church 1545, M.A. 1547, proctor 1551.

Rogers, Richard, of Sutton Vallence, Kent, said to be B.A. Oxon, and M.A. Cambridge 1552, B.D. 1562, rector of Great Chart 1566, and rector of Midley, Kent, 1569, and canon of St. Paul's 1566-94, archdeacon of St. Asaph, bishop suffragan of Dover 1568; died in Canterbury 19 May, 1597. See Cooper, ii. 224; Thomas, 595, 625; Lansdowne MS. 982, f. 254; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Rogers, Richard, B.A. 18 March, 1555-6, M.A. supd. 21 May, 1560; one of these names vicar of Wendon, Bucks, 1560, and another vicar of Great Dunmow, Essex, 1560, and rector of Great Canfield, 1564. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, Richard, of London, goldsmith; comptroller of the mint in the Tower of London; perhaps created M.A. 6 Sept., 1566. See Fasti, i. 177.

Rogers, Robert, student Christ Church, B.A. supd. 1551, M.A. 12 July, 1551; as B.D. of 10 years' standing, supl. for D.D. 17 Dec., 1574, rector of Gawsworth, Cheshire, 1565, canon of Chester 1580; his will dated 17 June, 1580, proved 20 Jan., 1595-6. See Earwaker, ii. 588; & O.H.S. xii. 48.

Rogers, Samuel, of Cambridge University; created B.D. (Oxon) 1 (or 2) Nov., 1642, perhaps vicar of Great Tey, Essex, 1638, or rector of Boscombe, Wilts, 1641; one of these names B.A. from Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1625-6, M.A. 1629; another B.A. from Queen's Coll. 1630-1, M.A. 1634. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, Simon (Rogeris or Rodgers), B.A. 15 March, 1528-9, etc. See O.H.S. i. 155.
Rogers, Thomas, B.A. from Christ Church 4 Feb., 1540-1, M.A. supd. June, 1544; perhaps chantry priest, Morthoe, Devon, 1541; and rector of Pyworthy, Devon, 1550. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.

Rogers, Thomas, student Christ Church 1571, B.A. 7 July, 1573, M.A. 6 July, 1576, chaplain of Dr. Bancroft, bishop of London, rector of Horninger, Suffolk, 1581, until his death, buried in his church 22 Feb., 1615. See Ath. ii. 162.
Rogers, Thomas, B.A. from Balliol Coll. 28 Feb., 1594-5, M.A. 12 June, 1599; one of these names rector of Cherrington, Wilts, 1616. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, Thomas, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1623-4, M.A. 1627, incorporated July, 1627; one of these names vicar of Iford, Sussex, 1632. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rogers, Thomas, "Chirurgus," s. Philip, of Stratford-on-Avon, co. Warwick, "gen. cond.;" privilegiatus 4 Dec., 1635, aged 38, licenced to practice in surgery 15 Oct., 1636.
Rogers, William, subscribed 9 Dec., 1614; B.A. from Corpus Christi Coll. 16 Dec., 1614; perhaps rector of Dodington, Somerset, 1617, until his death in 1640. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Rogerson, John, s. William, of Worcester (city), pleb. Trinity Coll., subscribed 13 July, 1694, aged 17, B.A. 1698; M.A. from King's Coll. 1713; admitted to Merchant Taylors' school 1687; born December, 1676; rector of Langford, Essex, 1712. See Robinson, i. 319; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Rogerson, Robert, s. Thomas, of Norton, Norfolk, sizar Caius Coll., Cambridge, 1 July, 1645, aged 18, B.A. 1648-9, M.A. 1653; incorporated 12 July, 1653, rector of Denton, Norfolk, 1667-1714; born 14 June, 1627. See Venn, 218; & Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rogerson, William, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1588-9, M.A. 1592; (incorporated 11 July, 1592), B.D. 1599, canon of St. Paul's 1604. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Rokeby, William, (brother of Sir Richard, treasurer of Ireland, son of John, of Thundercliffe Grange, Yorks), fellow of King's Hall, Cambridge, D.Can.L.; rector of Sandal 1487, and of Halifax, Yorks, 1502, rector of Fakenham, Norfolk, 1496, chancellor of Ireland 1498, and 1515, bishop of Meath, and privy councillor 1507, archbishop of Dublin 1512, archdeacon of Surrey 1520, until his death 29 Nov., 1521. See Ath. ii. 717; Cotton, i. 25; & Lansdowne MS. 979, ff. 4, 6.
Rolfe, Augustine (Rolfus), M.A. from Queen's Coll., Cambridge, 1595; incorporated 10 July, 1599.
Rolf, Richard, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1584-5 (incorporated 11 July, 1585); M.A. 1588. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Rolfe, William, s. William, of Stoke-Bruern, Northants, cler. Brasenose Coll. 7 July, 1688, aged 16; student of Inner Temple 1692, buried in the Temple church 1 March, 1692-3. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rolle, William, B.C.L. 14 July, 1528; perhaps vicar of Yarncombe, Devon, 1536. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rolles, Gabriel (Rooles), B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1610-11, M.A. 1614; incorporated 13 July, 1619, rector of East Locking, Berks, 1620, as Rolle. See Foster's Graduati Cantab.

Rolles, William, s. Richard, of Lewknor, Oxon, gent. St. John's Coll., matrie. 12 March, 1637-8, aged 17, B.A. 9 Nov., 1641, M.A. 6 July, 1644; B.D. from Jesus Coll. 12 Sept., 1661, rector of Wheatfield, Oxon, 1660, and of Chalfont St. Giles, Bucks, 1662. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rolles, William, created M.A. from Exeter Coll. 14 April, 1648.
Rolleston, Simon, created M.A. 31 Aug., 1636.
Rollinson, Francis, 1584. See Rallinson.

Rolt, Thomas, B.A. from Sydney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, 1647-8, M.A. 1651 (incorporated 14 July, 1657), B.D. 1658, reincorporated 13 July, 1669, rector of Wilden, Beds, 1657; buried there 2 Dec., 1695. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Romball, Thomas, subscribed 7 June, 1611.
Romney, Joseph, B.A. from Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 1610-11, M.A. 1614; incorporated 8 July, 1614, student of Inner Temple 1610, as of London, gent. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rone, William, of New Coll. 1661. See Roane.
Roode, Edward (or Rode), B.A. 21 July, 1522, M.A. 26 Nov., 1534; perhaps canon of Southwell 1561-73.
Rood, Richard, M.A. from Pembroke Coll. 5 Dec., 1634.

Rooke, Laurence, of Wadham Coll. 24 Aug., 1650 (s. George, of Monks Horton, Kent); scholar of King's Coll., Cambridge, 1640, from Eton, fellow 1643, M.A. 1647, astronomy, then geometry, professor of Gresham college, F.R.S.; born at Deptford 13 March, 1621-2; died 27 June, 1662; buried in the church of St. Martin Outwiche, London. See Ath. iii. 537; & Gardiner, 191.]

Rookes, Christopher (Rokys or Rokkis), B.A. 8 July, 1522, M.A. 1 July, 1527, B.D. supd. Oct., 1540; principal of Magdalen Hall 1529-32, vicar of Stanstead Abbots, Herts, 1534. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rookes, John, B.A. from St. Alban Hall 6 July, 1612.

Rookes, Jonas, B.A. from Magdalen Hall 24 April, 1599, M.A. 11 Feb., 1601-2 (2s. William, of Roydes Hall); vicar of Penistone, Yorks, 1619, see Foster's Index Eccl.; styled fellow and bursar of University Coll. in Foster's Yorkshire Collection, possibly brother of the next-named.
Rookewoode, Thomas, clerk and student of St. Edmund Hall; inventory at Oxford 13 June, 1581, as Ruckwoode.
Rooper, William, of St. Alban Hall 1667. See Roper.
Roos, Brian, D.Can.L. or doctor of decrees of the university of Valentia; incorporated 3 Feb., 1510-11; died 1529, buried in the church of Chelray. See Fasti, i. 31.

Roper, John (or Rooper), demy Magdalen Coll., from Berks, M.A. fellow, 1483, D.D. disp. 27 June, 1506, (first) Margaret professor of divinity, 1500, vice-chancellor of the university 1505, and 1511, principal of Salesurry and George Hall, rector of Witney, Oxon, 1493, vicar of St. Mary's church, Oxford, canon of Cardinal Coll. 1532; died May, 1534. See Ath. i. 76; & Landsowne MS. 979, f. 118.
Roper, John, B.A. disp. 4 July, 1512.
Roper, Thomas, of Trinity Coll. 1699. See Rooper.
Roscarrock, John, B.A. 11 Feb., 1576-7; perhaps from Exeter Coll. (and 1s. Thomas, of Roscarrock, Cornwall); died 24 Nov., 1608; brother of Henry and Richard. See O.H.S. xii. 65.
Roscarrock, Nicolas (Roiscariot), B.A. supd. 3 May, 1568, student Inner Temple 1571, as of Roscarrock, Cornwall. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg.
Rose, Gilbert, Augustinian Canon, B.D. supd. 22 May, 1512, and supd. 12 Dec., 1519, for incorporation as D.D.
Rose, John, subscribed 1 Dec., 1626.
Rose, Matthew, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 21 Feb., 1600-1, rector of Colesbourne, co. Gloucester, 1613. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Rose, Richard, B.A. from Exeter Coll. 14 June, 1621; perhaps student of Middle Temple 1622 (as son and heir of John, of Lyme, Dorset, gent.), and M.P. Lyme Regis April-May, 1640, 1640 (l.p.), till his death after 1648. See Foster's Inns of Court Reg. & Foster's Parliamentary Dictionary.

Rose, Stephen, "ser." Magdalen Coll., subscribed 23 Nov., 1655; B.A. from Wadham Coll. 1659, vicar of Cold Overton, co. Leicester, 1662-3, and rector of Woolhampton, Berks, 1667-95, father of Temple. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rose, Thomas, Minorite, B.D. 22 June, 1509.
Roser, Charles, created D.C.L. 7 Feb., 1642-3.

Rosewell, Adam (Ruswell or Rowswell), B.A. 26 Jan., 1520-1, M.A. 22 March, 1524-5, rector of Orcheston St. George, Wilts, 1532, until deprived in 1554, vicar of Clevedon 1558, and rector of Shepton Mallet, Somerset, 1559, until his death in 1566. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rosewell, Alexander, subscribed 17 Dec., 1619 (apparently erased).
Rosewell, Henry (Roswell or Croswell), B.C.L. 21 July, 1522, D.C.L. sup. 20 Feb., 1527-8, as Ruswell.
Ros(e)well, John, B.A. 31 March, 1522; perhaps vicar of Marston Magna 1519, rector of Sparkford 1522, died rector of Thorne Falcon, Somerset, about 1569. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rosewell, Thomas (Rowswell), M.A. from All Souls' Coll. 19 June, 1509, B.D. supd. 8 Feb., 1531-2, vicar of Bradford, Somerset, 1517. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ros(e)well, Walter, B.A. from Queen's Coll. 20 Nov., 1629, M.A. 26 June, 1632; incorporated at Cambridge 1633, vicar of Doulting, Somerset, 1639. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Add. MS. 15,671, p. 87.
Rosewell, William (Rowsell, Russell, or Ruswell), B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 17 Dec., 1556, M.A. supl. Feb., 1559-60.
Rosier, Charles, D.C.L. 7 Feb., 1642-3. See Roser.
Rosse, Gabriel, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1596-7, M.A. 1600; incorporated 16 July, 1600, rector of Norton-by-Twycross, co. Leicester, 1609. See Foster's Index Eccl.; & Al. West. 63.
Rosse, Richard, of Magdalen Coll. 1672. See Rose.
Rosse, Robert (or Rose), B.A. 19 July, 1567.

Ross, Thomas, created M.A. 28 Sept., 1663 (nearly related to Alexander Ross), tutor to James Crofts, afterwards Duke of Monmouth, keeper of the king's libraries, and groom of the privy chamber; died 27 Oct., 1675. See Fasti, ii. 273.
Rosse, William, created M.A. 28 Sept., 1663.

Rossington, James, admitted Emanuel Coll., Cambridge, 7 Nov., 1626; B.A. from Exeter Coll., 3 June, 1630, vicar of St. Allen, Cornwall, and of St. Clement's, 1663; father of Anthony and James. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rosuggan, Richard (Rosogan), B.A. 11 May, 1526.
Roswell, See also Rosewell or Rowswell.
Roth, John (or Rith), of Christ Church 1561, B.A. 1 July, 1560. See O.H.S. i. 345.
Rotherham, George, subscribed 4 Nov., 1614, B.A. from Lincoln Coll. 10 Nov., 1614.
Rotheram, Thomas, B.A. from Magdalen Coll. 3 July, 1591.
Rotherham, Thomas, fellow Trinity Coll., Cambridge, B.A. 1656-7, M.A. 1660; incorporated 6 April, 1665; perhaps rector of Pett, Sussex, 1695. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rothley, John (or Southeley), chaplain, B.Can.L. and B.C.L. 1 July, 1510, rector of Alresford, Essex, 1521. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rothwell, John, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1662-3, M.A. 1666; incorporated 26 April, 1671, rector of Poynings 1679, and vicar of Henfield, Sussex, 1686. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rotten, Robert (or Wratten), Benedictine, B.D. 5 June, 1523.
Rouse, James (Rowse), B.A. from Exeter Coll. 6 July, 1614, M.A. from New Inn Hall 8 July, 1619, then in orders. See O.H.S. xii. 330.
Rous, John, B.C.L. 9 July, 1562, D.C.L. sup. 3 July, 1564; one of these names vicar of Tounstall, Devon, 1564, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rouse, John, Bodley's Librarian 1620-52. See Russe.
Rouse(us), Lewis, D.Med., of Padua (Leyden) University; incorporated 12 July, 1625. See Fasti, i. 424.
Routh, Henry, B.A. from University Coll. supd. 17 Jan., 1610-11.
Routh, John (Rith or Roth), B.A. from Christ Church 1 July, 1560.
Rowe, See also Roe.

Rowe, Cheyney, 3s. William, of Higham Hill, Essex; B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1610-11, fellow, M.A. 1614, (incorporated 9 July, 1616), B.D. 1622, D.D. 1625, rector of Rampton, co. Cambridge, 1631, of Houghton Conquest, Beds, 1636, and of Overell, co. Cambridge, 1637, until sequestered in 1647, by the Earl of Manchester for non-residence. See Add. MS. 15,671, p. 139; & Foster's Index Eccl.

Rowe, George (Rooe), B.A. from Merton Coll. 14 Feb., 1565-6, M.A. 12 July, 1567, B.D. supd. 1573, supd. for licence to preach 8 July, 1572; perhaps canon of York 1571, rector of Wheldrake, Yorks, 1574. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rowe, George (Roe), B.Med. from Gloucester Hall 20 June, 1634, created D.Med. 1 Nov., 1642.
Row, John (Raw), chaplain, B.Can.L. 4 July, 1513.
Rowe, John (Ro), B.A. June, 1518.
Row, William (Roo), B.A. 30 June, 1505.
Rowham, Edmund, Benedictine; B.D. dispd. 14 April, 1519.
Rowland, David, of St. Mary Hall (1586). See Ath. i. 528.]

Rowlandes, Henry, s. Robert ap Robert, of Mellteyrn, co. Carnarvon; chaplain of New Coll., B.A. 17 Feb., 1573-4, M.A. 27 June, 1577, B.D. 27 March, 1591, D.D. supd. 28 June, 1605; of Gray's Inn 1606, rector of Mellteyrn 1572-81, of Langton, Oxon, 1581-1600, prebendary of Bangor 1584, rector of Aberdaron, co. Carnarvon 1588, archdeacon of Anglesey 1598, dean 1593, and bishop of Bangor 1598, rector of Trefdaeth, Anglesey, 1601, vicar of Llanrhaiadr-in-Kimmerch 1602, and rector of Llanrhaiadr, co. Denbigh, 1612, until his death 6 July, 1616, aged 65, buried in the cathedral. See Ath. ii. 854; Lansdowne MSS. 983, f. 285, and 984, f. 34; Gutch, i. 572; Fasti, i. 311; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Rowland, John, "ser." Jesus Coll., subscribed 7 Nov., 1655.
Rowland, Maurice, B.A. from St. Edmund Hall 15 Feb., 1598-9.
Rowland, Owen, B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1570; incorporated 6 Feb., 1574-5, M.A. 4 July, 1575, rector of Llandwynne and Llanbedr, co. Anglesey, 1592. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ro(w)land, Philip, "theologian," M.A. disp. 27 June, 1514.
Rowland, Richard, subscribed 10 April, 1635.
Ro(w)land, Thomas, Benedictine, Abbot of Abingdon, B.D. sup. 17 May, 1514; sometimes written and called Rowland Pentecost. See Fasti, i. 41.
Rowland, Thomas, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1675-6, M.A. 1679; incorporated 13 July, 1680.
Rowland, William, subscribed 4 May, 1604, one W. R. apparently a servant of Christ Church, will at Oxford, proved 27 Oct., 1606.
Rowley, John, B.A. 19 Dec., 1534.
Rowley, William, B.A. 19 Dec., 1334.
Rowsam, S, of Oriel Coll., in or before 1572.
Rowswell, See Rosewell.
Royall, See also Ryall.
Royden, Mathew, M.A. 7 July, 1580; 4th minor canon St. Paul's cathedral, 1603. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Roylle, Paul, of Lincoln Coll., subscribed 12 July, 1637.
Roylle, Walter, B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 3 Feb., 1573-4, M.A. 25 June, 1577, rector of Nether Seale, co. Leicester, 1579. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Royse, Joshua, of Merton Coll. 1699. See Roissie.

Rudde, Antony, fellow Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1569, B.A. 1566-7, M.A. 1570, B.D. 1577 ("incorporated S.T.B. with standing as of Cambridge 9 July, 1577"), D.D. 1583, (perhaps of Middle Temple 1561, as of New Inn), dean of Gloucester 1584, bishop of St. Davids 1594, until his death 7 March, 1614-15; buried in the church of Llangathen, co. Carmarthen; father of the next-named. See Lansdowne MS. 983, f. 233; & Fasti, i. 207.
Rudde, George (Rude), of Queen's Coll., M.A. 20 March, 1522-3; admon. at Oxford 9 Sept., 1528.

Rudde, Ralph, fellow Queen's Coll., B.A. 27 April, 1531, M.A. 22 June, 1534, B.D. supd. 1540-1, principal of St. Edmund Hall, 1546-69, rector of Enham, Hants, 1543, and vicar of Cropredy, Oxon, 1549. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rudde, Roland (or Rudd), B.A. from Queen's Coll., 2 June, 1511, as Rode, licenced M.A. 4 Feb., 1514-15, and created M.A. 2 July, 1515; perhaps fellow 1516.
Rudde, William, B.A. 14 March, 1523-4.
Rudge, John, pleb. Exeter Coll., subscribed 29 Oct., 1657, B.A. 1661, M.A. 19 Jan., 1663-4, rector of St. Mary Aldermary, London, 1669. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Rudroppe, Robert, of Christ Church 1555, B.A. 9 Dec., 1556.
Ruffus, Nicholas, lord St. Aubin in France; created M.A. 24 Sept., 1593. See St. Aubyn, page 1300.

Rugbie, William, B.A. from Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1612-13, M.A. 1616; incorporated 11 July, 1620, sequestered to the rectory of Bythorne, Hunts, 1646, by the Westminster assembly. See Add. MS. 15,670, pp. 109, 124.
Rugg, See also Rigge.
Rugg, Henry, born in Gloucester, s. Henry, of Youghall, Ireland, "Presbiteri." Magdalen Hall 1 July, 1642, aged 17.
Rugge, John, principal of Hart Hall 1501-3.

Rugge, John, B.C.L. supd. 30 April, 1566, had studied civil law in upper Germany 6 years, rector of Winsford 1572, and of Chedzoy, Somerset, 1573, archdeacon of Wells 1572, canon of Westminster 1576, until his death in 1581, buried in Wells cathedral. See Fasti, i. 172; Lansdowne MS. 982, f. 16; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Rumbold, Thomas, subscribed 7 June, 1611. See Romball.
Rumney, Joseph, 1614. See Romney.

Rumpffius, Christian, doctor of philosophy and physics, chief and principal physician to Prince Frederic V., elector of the palatine, and to Elizabeth, daughter of James I. of England; D.Med. Basle and Heidelberg, subscribed 2 April, 1613, as of Exeter Coll., and incorporated next day. See Fasti, i. 354. Called Christopher in O.H.S. x. 375-6.

Runcorn, Thomas (Runchorne or Runkchorn), B.A. — June, 1518, chaplain 1520, M.A. 5 July, 1521; vicar of Bowden, Cheshire, 1535, canon of Winchester about 1541, rector of Llanrhaiadr, co. Denbigh, 1543, archdeacon of Bangor 1525, and again in 1540, canon of Chester 1554, and rector of Bebington 1554, and vicar of Weaverham, Cheshire, 1554, canon of Lincoln 1555; his will dated 21 Dec., 1556, proved 2 July following. See Foster's Index Eccl. & Fasti, i. 55.
Rupert, Prince, See Cumberland, page 362.

Rusaeus, Antonius, Marquis of Fiat, Lord of Chelly and Longimeau, etc., M.A. of Cambridge; incorporated 25 Aug., 1624, privy counsellor to the king of France, chief master of his horse, and master of the mines, ambassador to the court of St. James. See Fasti. i. 418.

Rushe, Antony, fellow Magdalen Coll., and B.A. 4 July, 1554, M.A. 20 June, 1558; D.D. Cambridge 1566; master of Canterbury grammar school 1561, chaplain to the Earl of Sussex, rector of Woodham Walter, Essex, 1565, canon of Windsor, 1566, rector of Calverton, Bucks, 1566, chaplain to Queen Elizabeth, canon of Canterbury 1568, rector of Osgarwick, Kent 1568, rector of St. Olave, Southwark, 1569, prebendal rector of Brightling, Sussex, 1565, dean of Chichester 1570, until his death 1 April, 1577; buried in St. George's, Windsor. See Ath. i. 429, Cooper, i. 363; Lansdowne MS. 981, f. 167; & Foster's Index Eccl.
Rushton, John, B.A. supd. 19 May, 1579, rector of Pedmore, co. Worcester, 1589. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Rushton, Robert, B.A. 22 June, 1576.

Rushworth, John, of Queen's Coll.; created M.A. 21 May, 1649, secretary to his kinsman Thomas, Lord Fairfax, when generalissimo of the parliament forces, bar.-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1647 (as son and heir of Laurence, late of Acklington Park, Northumberland, gent. deceased), assistant clerk to the house of commons 1640, secretary to lord keeper Bridgman 1667, M.P. Berwick-uponTweed (May) 1657-8, 1659, 1660, 1678-81; died within the rules of the king's bench 12 May, 1690, aged 82; buried 15th in the church of Mildenhall, Suffolk. See Ath. iv. 820; & Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Rushworth, Thomas, 1594 and 1654. See Rishworth, page 1259.
Russell, (—), presented by Sir Nathaniel Brent and admitted M.A. 12 April, 1648, by Dr. Reynolds, vice-chancellor.
Russell, Charles, student Christ Church 1561, B.A. 11 Feb., 1563-4, M.A. 25 June, 1566. See Hugh and John 1567.
Russell, Edward, Lord; B.A. from University Coll. 16 Dec., 1567 (s. Francis, 2nd, Earl of Bedford); died v.p. s.p. See Fasti, i. 179; & Foster's Peerage, Bedford.

Russell, Henry, created M.A. 28 Sept., 1663; possibly bar.-at-law, Inner Temple, 1667 (as of Strensham, co. Worcester, gen.); licenced 14 March, 1665-6, to marry Anne Raves, of Duns Tew, Oxon, and buried in the Temple church 25 April, 1668; see John 1653, and Robert 1659. See Foster's Judges and Barristers.
Russell, Hugh, of Christ Church 1555, B.A. 24 Nov., 1558. See Charles and John 1567.
Russell, Jeremy, M.A. from Christ Church 14 April, 1719.
Russell, John, chaplain, B.Can.L. 2 Dec., 1510.
Russell, John, priest, B.A. 8 Oct., 1521, M.A. 13 July, 1525; one of these names vicar of Ramsey, Essex, 1540, and canon of Lincoln 1542, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Russell, John, Earl of Bedford, high steward of Oxford University 1542-55, M.P. Bucks 1529-36, knighted by the Earl of Surrey 1 (or 2) July, 1522, privy councillor 1538, created Baron Russell 29 March, 1539, and Earl of Bedford 19 Jan., 1550, k.g. 1539, lord keeper of the privy seal; died 14 March, 1555. See Foster's Peerage.
Russell, John, B.A. supd. 5 July, 1567.
Russell, John, student Christ Church 1567, B.A. 26 Jan., 1569-70, M.A. 6 July, 1573; one of these names rector of Strensham, co. Worcester, 1574. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Russell, John, B.A. from Catherine Hall, Cambridge, 1671-2, M.A. 1675; incorporated 10 July, 1677; perhaps vicar of Brundall, Norfolk, 1681, etc. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Russell, Paul, of Brasenose Coll. 1627, B.A. 5 May, 1631, M.A. 6 Feb., 1633-4.

Russell, Robert, student Christ Church 1573, B.A. 28 Feb., 1575-6, M.A. 22 May, 1579, B.D. 14 April, 1600; licenced to preach 18 Jan., 1604-5, curate or vicar of St. Thomas, in the suburbs of Oxford; died 23 Nov., 1626, will at Oxford proved 12 Dec., following. See O.H.S. xii. 56.
Russell, Thomas, B.C.L. 8 Feb., 1516-17.
Russell, Thomas, B.A. supd. 8 Dec., 1516, M.A. 2 May, 1520; one of these names rector of Tiffield 1526, of Cosgrave 1529, of Dingley 1530, and of Holcot (all Northants) 1530-52. See Baker, ii. 134.

Russell, Thomas, sizar of Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 26 June, 1583, B.A. 1586-7, M.A. 1590; incorporated 10 July, 1593; one of these names rector of South Hykeham, co. Lincoln, 1595, and of Sharnford, co, Leicester 1595. See Foster's Index Eccl.

Russell, (Sir) William, of Magdalen Coll., proposed to be created M.A. — July, 1594 (4s. Francis, earl of Bedford); of Lincoln's Inn 1589; knighted in Dublin 11 Sept., 1580, lord deputy of Ireland 1594-7; created baron Russell, of Thornhaugh, 21 July, 1603; died 9 Aug., 1613. See Fasti, i. 268.
Ruswell, See Rowswell.
Ruthyn, Ellis, B.LL. disp. 16 March, 1505-6.

Rutter, Richard, B.A. from St. John's Coll., Cambridge, 1617-18, M.A. 1621, incorporated 20 May, 1622; one Rutter had a private school at Muncaster, Cumberland, circa 1638. See Mayor, 53 art. Richard Kirkbye.

Rutter, Samuel, bishop of Sodor and Man 1661, archdeacon 1640, canon of Lichfield 1660; perhaps rector of Waberthwaite, Cumberland, 1630; died 30 May, 1662. See Al. West. 93; Ath. iv. 809; & Foster's Index Eccl.]
Ruttey, John (or Rytte), B.A. 5 Dec., 1527.
Rutty, Thomas, created B.A. from Magdalen Hall 15 April, 1648; one of these names ejected from Milton, Wilts, 1662. See Calamy, iii. 368.
Ryall, Henry, of New Inn Hall. 1669. See Royall.
Ryan, Dermicius (Rian), student also at Paris and Cambridge; B.Can.L. 5 May, 1522.
Ryan, Donald (Rian), B.Can.L. 12 Aug., 1556, as Daniel. See Fasti, i. 95, 150.
Ryan, Donatus (Ream, Ryam), public reader in canon law in New Inn Hall, regular canon, B.Can.L. supd. Feb., 1532-3.
Ryan, John, B.A. supl. 10 April, 1548, as Reanus.
Rycks, John, Minorite; died 1536. See Ath. i. 101.]
Ryder, John, B.A. 25 June, 1507, M.A. supd. 3 Feb., 1510-11.

Ryder, John (Rider), born at Carrington, Cheshire, B.A. from Jesus Coll. 3 Feb., 1580-1, M.A. 5 July, 1583, rector of Waterstock, Oxon, 1580, of South Ockendon 1583-90, minister of Bermondsey, Surrey, rector of Winwick, co. Lancaster, 1597, dean of St. Patrick's, Dublin, 1597-1608, prebendary of Kildare 1599, archdeacon of Meath 1608, bishop of Killaloe 1613, until his death 12 Nov., 1632, author of an "English and Latin Dictionary." See Ath. ii. 547; Cotton's Fasti Ecc. Hib. i. 463; & Lansdowne MS. 984, f. 179.
Ryder, John, See also Rider.
Ryley, Edward, Minorite, B.D. supd. June, 1533.
Ryley, Hugh (Riley), B.A. from Brasenose Coll. 29 April, 1555.
Ryley, John (Riley, or Rylay), B.A. supd. June, 1537.
Rymer, Michael, B.A. from Trinity Coll., Cambridge, 1672-3, M.A. 1682; incorporated 7 July. 1682, rector of Newton Kyme, Yorks, 1682. See Foster's Index Eccl.
Ryth, John, B.A. supd. Michaelmas, 1540; one of these names rector of Shaw, Berks, 1540, and vicar of Gillingham, Dorset, 1542. See Foster's Index Ecclesiasticus.
Ryther, John (Rether), chaplain, B.A. 11 March, 1544-5.
Rytherch, William, B.C.L. 10 June, 1543; see also Pretherch, page 1201.
Ryves, See also Reeves.
Ryves, Edmund, B.A. from New Coll. 10 Oct., 1651, as Reeves; M.A. 26 May, 1654, as Rives; student of Gray's Inn 1656, as y.s. Edmund, of Drayton, Southants, gent. See Foster's Gray's Inn Reg.
Ryves, Edward, B.A. from Merton Coll. 13 May, 1618, as Reyves.
